methodology.name: hegemon-formal-blueprint-dag-v1
methodology.summary: LeanMarathon-inspired formal-security blueprint: every release claim is a reviewed graph node with implementation bindings, call-order constraints, evidence, dependencies, and cheap falsification cases.

nodes.formal_statement: Every cargo-audit advisory or yanked crate that remains in release code or the formal-core checker lockfile is accepted only when the Lean dependency-audit policy preconditions hold: all waivers are well formed, unexpired, release-review-owned, remediation-tracked, every finding has an exact advisory/package/version/kind waiver match, and every checked-in waiver is used by a current finding.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.DependencyAuditPolicy.accepts_iff_dependency_audit_preconditions` pins the release waiver decision table, companion theorems pin exact-match acceptance and malformed/unwaived/unused rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the policy helper, and `scripts/dependency-audit-gate.sh` remains the executable cargo-audit JSON and waiver-file evidence producer. The formal-core checker crate is audited separately so the assurance tooling does not bypass the same dependency discipline. `Hegemon.Release.DependencyAuditPolicy.accepted_dependency_audit_exposes_policy_facts`, `accepted_dependency_audit_finding_has_explicit_valid_waiver`, `accepted_dependency_audit_waiver_is_valid`, `accepted_dependency_audit_waiver_has_release_metadata`, `waiver_release_metadata_true_exposes_fields`, `accepted_dependency_audit_waiver_release_metadata_fields`, `accepted_dependency_audit_waiver_matches_live_finding`, `waiver_matches_any_finding_exposes_live_exact_match`, and `accepted_dependency_audit_waiver_has_live_exact_finding` now expose accepted waiver/finding accounting, each owner/tracking/reason/remediation/review-date metadata field, and a live finding witness with exact advisory/package/version/kind equality. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_native_backend_release_posture_exposes_preconditions` and `accepted_release_posture_exposes_all_release_gates` compose those dependency facts with PQ binary, CI release-gate, and native-backend release-posture acceptance. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_release_posture_with_native_accepted_mode_yields_production_gate_certificate` carries the explicit waiver/finding accounting inside the accepted-mode production release gate, so waivers must remain exact, valid, live, and review-owned when native backend release acceptance is deliberately required. `production_release_gate_certificate_exposes_dependency_waiver_accounting`, `production_release_gate_certificate_exposes_dependency_waiver_metadata_fields`, `production_release_gate_certificate_exposes_live_waiver_exactness`, and `production_release_gate_certificate_exposes_dependency_audit_ci_binding` expose exact accepted audit, valid waiver, release metadata fields, waived finding, live exact waiver, and dependency-audit CI-binding facts directly from the production release certificate. The live shell gate rejects missing owner/reviewed_at/remediation fields, malformed DEP tracking ids, and future review dates before a waiver can satisfy the release policy.

target_review.notes: The claim is deliberately operational: it proves review coverage for known dependency findings, not absence of future advisories.

falsification_cases.description: A cargo-audit finding with no matching waiver must fail the dependency gate.
The formal-core checker lockfile is audited independently from the root workspace lockfile.
Lean-generated dependency-audit policy vectors must fail on malformed waivers, exact-match drift, and unwaived findings.
A checked-in waiver that no longer corresponds to any cargo-audit finding must fail the dependency gate.
A matching waiver that omits release-review metadata such as owner, reviewed_at, or remediation must fail before it can waive a cargo-audit finding.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim dependency freshness, advisory database completeness, upstream advisory quality, or that a remediation plan is sufficient; it claims theorem-backed waiver accounting for known cargo-audit findings and rejects stale or metadata-poor waiver drift in the checked policy.

nodes.formal_statement: The release PQ-binary audit decision accepts exactly when source, dependency, and binary scans are all clean; any forbidden classical primitive hit in those scan classes rejects, with source findings taking precedence over dependency and binary findings.
nodes.informal_argument: The binary primitive audit is the release-facing check for the design rule that the native node must stay PQ-clean. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.PqBinaryPolicy.accepts_iff_all_scans_clean` pins the release decision table, companion theorems pin rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the release audit helper, and `scripts/security-audit.sh` remains the executable scanner for actual source, dependency, and linked-binary evidence. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_pq_binary_audit_exposes_clean_scans` now exposes the clean source/dependency/binary scan facts from an accepted PQ binary audit, and the release posture certificate carries those facts together with dependency-audit, CI release-gate, and native-backend release-posture acceptance. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_release_posture_with_native_accepted_mode_yields_production_gate_certificate` carries clean source, dependency, and binary PQ scan facts into the accepted-mode production release gate together with dependency, CI, and native-backend posture facts.

target_review.notes: The claim is scoped to the audited release node binary and the current forbidden-primitive pattern set.

falsification_cases.description: Forbidden ECC/pairing/RSA/trusted-setup symbols or dependencies must fail the security audit.
Lean-generated release PQ-binary policy vectors must match the release audit policy helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the release audit decision table. It does not prove that grep/strings discover every possible primitive implementation or that every transitive crate is PQ-secure; those remain scanner-pattern and dependency-review obligations.

nodes.formal_statement: The checked-in CI/release workflow gate is accepted only when dependency-audit, the dependency-audit job's explicit waiver gate, formal-core, security-adversarial, native-backend-security, release-build, release-build dependencies on those security gates, release binary PQ audit wiring, tag-release native backend review/posture gates, and branch-protection/ruleset evidence requiring those release checks are present.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.CiReleaseGate.accepts_iff_ci_release_gate_preconditions` pins the workflow decision table, companion theorems pin rejection precedence including `dependency_audit_waiver_gate_missing_rejects_after_job` and `branch_protection_ruleset_missing_rejects`, generated vectors are checked by `scripts/check_ci_release_gate_policy.py`, and the same checker inspects `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `.github/workflows/release.yml`, and `.github/rulesets/hegemon-release-required-checks.json` so the PR/main release build, tag release builds, and checked branch ruleset evidence cannot drop dependency-audit, the dependency-audit job's `./scripts/dependency-audit-gate.sh` waiver gate, formal-core, security-adversarial, native-backend-security, binary PQ audit, native backend review/posture, required release-build security dependencies, or required ruleset status-check contexts without failing formal-core. `Hegemon.Release.CiReleaseGate.accepted_ci_release_gate_exposes_required_policy_facts` now exposes every required CI job, the dependency-audit waiver-gate step, release-build dependency edge, binary audit, tag-release backend gate, and branch-protection/ruleset evidence from an accepted policy result, while `accepted_ci_release_gate_depends_on_dependency_audit_policy` exposes dependency-audit job, waiver-gate, release-build, and release-build security dependency facts as one downstream-consumable projection. The release posture certificate composes those CI facts with dependency, PQ binary audit, and native-backend release-posture acceptance. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_release_posture_with_native_accepted_mode_yields_production_gate_certificate` composes accepted CI release-gate facts with dependency waivers, PQ scan facts, and accepted-mode native backend release posture in one production release surface, and `production_release_gate_certificate_exposes_dependency_audit_ci_binding` exposes the accepted dependency audit plus accepted CI dependency-gate binding from that production certificate.

target_review.notes: Accepted as repository-local workflow wiring plus checked ruleset-export evidence; live GitHub ruleset installation/enforcement remains an external administrative control.

falsification_cases.description: Removing the PR/main release-build binary PQ audit step must fail the workflow gate.
Leaving a dependency-audit job in place while removing its explicit dependency-audit waiver gate command must fail the workflow gate.
Removing release-build dependency on dependency-audit, formal-core, security-adversarial, or native-backend-security must fail the workflow gate.
Removing dependency-audit/formal-core/native-backend review/native-backend release posture from tag-release security-gates or removing binary audit from platform builds must fail the workflow gate.
Removing dependency-audit, formal-core, security-adversarial, native-backend-security, or release-build from the checked ruleset export must fail the release gate.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and checks repository workflow wiring, including that the dependency-audit CI job runs the explicit waiver gate, and checks a repository-local active branch ruleset export for required release/security status checks. It does not prove GitHub runner integrity, live remote branch-protection enforcement, scanner completeness, or future advisory database completeness.

nodes.formal_statement: The top-level completion certificate is inhabited only when the internal Hegemon theorem bundle supplies the critical chain-security facts and the remaining primitive, proof-system, storage, DA-retention, external-bridge, release-infrastructure, complete-native-node, and performance assumptions are explicit certificate fields, while the production release gate is also present.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Release.HighestStandardCompletionCertificate.accepted_internal_bundle_and_release_gate_yield_highest_standard_completion_certificate` packages the internal theorem bundle, explicit external-assumption bundle, and production release gate. `completion_certificate_exposes_critical_chain_security_and_residuals` projects no counterfeiting, no double spend, no theft/input authorization, authorized deltas and stablecoin exceptions, DA refinement, statement binding, proof-system residual boundary, canonical encoding, replay/reorg/startup refinement, DA sidecar binding, bridge safety, PQ network safety, resource bounds, primitive assumptions, zero-knowledge/ciphertext assumptions, external bridge proof soundness, release infrastructure assumptions, and performance preservation from the one certificate. `completion_certificate_binds_release_gate_and_native_backend_posture` proves the same certificate carries accepted-mode native backend release posture through the production release gate. This node is the accounting closure for the highest-standard goal: it does not erase external assumptions, it makes them first-class theorem parameters.

target_review.notes: Accepted as the final closure/accounting certificate for internal theorem coverage plus explicit external assumptions. It is not an unconditional proof of cryptographic primitive hardness or remote infrastructure behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the final completion certificate or root import must fail the Lean formal gate.
The claims and blueprint ledgers must keep the completion certificate and its critical dependencies visible to the formal-core auditors.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-claims config/formal-security-claims.json && cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node closes the internal formal-verification accounting target by requiring the critical theorem bundle, production release gate, and explicit external-assumption bundle together. It does not prove primitive cryptographic hardness, STARK/FRI/PCS soundness from first principles, simulator zero knowledge, external-chain proof soundness, OS RNG quality, live GitHub enforcement, scanner completeness, DA availability beyond assumptions, storage hardware durability below admitted storage gates, or complete native-node equivalence without the corresponding assumption fields.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction-balance transition, a valid balance witness must have concrete balance slots, the native slot delta must equal `fee - value_balance`, the stablecoin/non-native slot rules must hold, a non-stablecoin transaction has zero delta for every non-native asset, and a stablecoin transaction can have a nonzero non-native delta only for the explicitly selected stablecoin asset where the delta equals the authorized issuance amount. Under the explicit AcceptedProofArtifact balance-soundness assumption from proof acceptance to `validBalance`, the same conservation facts lift to accepted proof wrappers. The SmallWood balance-boundary target additionally requires the deployed inline-Merkle verifier surface to expose canonical signed-magnitude public fields, native delta authorization, selected stablecoin delta authorization, disabled stablecoin zero, and non-selected non-native zero. The P3 AIR balance-boundary target models the deployed final-row public balance equations directly, requiring native value-balance authorization, selected-stablecoin issuance authorization, non-selected non-native zero, disabled-stablecoin metadata/slot zeroing, and selector asset binding. Stablecoin-enabled balances additionally reject the reserved padding-field alias as the selected issuance asset.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_has_slots`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_rejects_slot_overflow`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_rules`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_selected_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_per_asset_transaction_conservation` prove core facts about the executable balance kernel and its no-asset-transmutation boundary as one top-level conservation theorem. `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_selected_delta`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero` lift those facts to accepted proof artifacts under the named proof-system balance-soundness assumption. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodBalanceBoundary.accepted_smallwood_balance_constraints_expose_boundary_facts`, `accepted_smallwood_balance_constraints_authorize_native_delta`, `accepted_smallwood_balance_constraints_authorize_selected_stablecoin_delta`, `accepted_smallwood_balance_constraints_disabled_stablecoin_delta_zero`, and `accepted_smallwood_balance_constraints_nonselected_non_native_zero` package the concrete public balance/stablecoin facts expected from the deployed SmallWood inline-Merkle constraint surface, while the noncanonical signed-zero rejection theorems pin the canonical signed-magnitude boundary. `Hegemon.Transaction.AirBalanceBoundary.accepted_air_balance_final_row_exposes_boundary_facts`, `accepted_air_balance_final_row_native_delta`, `accepted_air_balance_final_row_selected_slot_asset`, `accepted_air_balance_final_row_nonselected_slot_delta_zero`, `accepted_air_balance_final_row_disabled_metadata_zero`, and `accepted_air_balance_final_row_disabled_non_native_delta_zero` model the concrete deployed P3 final-row equations for public balance/stablecoin fields. `gen_transaction_vectors` emits Lean-derived native-fee, value-balance, non-native conservation, counterfeit rejection, stablecoin issuance, stablecoin mismatch, native-asset stablecoin rejection, and balance-slot overflow examples. The formal-core gate runs production `transaction-circuit` conformance against `TransactionWitness::balance_slots`, `TransactionWitness::validate`, focused inline-Merkle public balance/stablecoin mutation regressions that recompute auxiliary Merkle rows from tampered public values and still require rejection, and `p3_air_balance_public_field_mutations_rejected`, which proves a valid P3 stablecoin proof stops verifying if verifier-facing value-balance or stablecoin issuance public fields drift. The existing TLA+ model remains bounded abstraction evidence for the larger transaction balance/nullifier invariant shape. `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_padding_field_alias_rejected` and the generated `stablecoin-padding-field-alias-rejected` vector pin the reserved Goldilocks padding-field representative as non-mintable, and production `verify_balance_slots` rejects noncanonical stablecoin asset ids before applying any stablecoin issuance exception.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for TransactionWitness balance-slot and balance-validation behavior, with the TLA+ model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the transaction-balance theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production balance-slot or balance-validation behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `BalanceInvariant` or `NullifierUniqueness` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the transaction model.
The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must reject public fee, value-balance, and balance-slot asset tampering even when auxiliary Merkle rows are recomputed from the tampered public values.

The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must reject public stablecoin enablement, asset, and issuance-field tampering even when auxiliary Merkle rows are recomputed from the tampered public values.
The deployed P3 verifier must reject verifier-facing value-balance and stablecoin issuance public-field mutations for a valid stablecoin proof.
The transaction verifier must reject the reserved Goldilocks padding-field alias as a stablecoin issuance asset before applying mint/conservation exceptions.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable balance-slot and validation semantics, conditionally lifts those facts to accepted proof wrappers under an explicit balance-soundness assumption, names the SmallWood inline-Merkle public balance/stablecoin verifier-surface facts with focused mutation regressions, and now models the concrete P3 AIR final-row public balance equations with a deployed-verifier public-field mutation regression. It does not prove note commitment hashing, Merkle hash-function security, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full STARK/PCS soundness, witness extraction, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean Merkle-path transition, path folding uses position-bit orientation at every level, accepts the computed root when the sibling depth matches, and rejects mismatched depths or roots.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_empty`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_even_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_odd_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_accepts_computed_root`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_length`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_root` prove the executable Merkle path control-flow facts. `gen_merkle_vectors` emits Lean-derived empty, left/right orientation, high-position, wrong-root, and wrong-length examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `MerklePath::root_with` and `MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node` helpers, while `MerklePath::verify` delegates to the same fold helper with the real Poseidon2 `merkle_node` combiner.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for Merkle path fold, position-bit orientation, explicit-depth admission, and root equality semantics. Poseidon2 hash security/equivalence remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the Merkle path orientation/depth/root theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production Merkle path folding, depth admission, or root comparison away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing SmallWood native membership validation away from explicit MERKLE_TREE_DEPTH gating or MerklePath::verify must be caught by transaction-circuit regression coverage.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks Merkle path control flow only. It does not prove Poseidon2 merkle_node cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean note-commitment input grammar, deployed note commitments absorb domain tag 1 over exactly 18 field limbs: value, asset id, recipient key limbs, rho limbs, commitment-randomness limbs, and authorization-public-key limbs, with each 32-byte field split into four 8-byte big-endian limbs. Real asset ids must be canonical Goldilocks-field representatives and must not equal the external padding sentinel, the padding field alias, or the field modulus.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.NoteCommitmentInputs.note_commitment_domain_tag_is_one`, `note_commitment_inputs_have_eighteen_limbs`, `note_commitment_inputs_start_with_value_and_asset`, `note_commitment_inputs_absorb_recipient_rho_randomness_auth`, and the canonical asset-id theorems pin the note-commitment transcript and field-domain boundary. `gen_note_commitment_input_vectors` emits patterned byte-field and asset-id edge cases. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared production `note_commitment_inputs` helper, while focused regressions require P3, SmallWood, and disclosure preimage builders to route through the same helper and require note/public-input/disclosure admission to reject padding-field alias asset ids. The wallet conformance gate uses the same generated vectors against `NotePlaintext::to_note_data`, checking that wallet plaintext exports feed the exact circuit commitment limbs and that memo bytes remain outside the commitment transcript.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for note-commitment preimage ordering and field encoding, and as production hardening for asset ids that would alias padding or reduce modulo the Goldilocks field.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the note-commitment input or asset-id canonicality theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production note-commitment input ordering, 8-byte big-endian field encoding, input count, domain tag, or asset-id canonicality away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
P3, SmallWood, and disclosure commitment-input builders must continue matching the shared transaction-core note_commitment_inputs helper.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p transaction-circuit commitment_inputs_use_shared_core_preimage -- --nocapture && cargo test -p disclosure-circuit disclosure_commitment_inputs_use_shared_core_preimage -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: Note, serialized public-input, raw public-input, and disclosure boundaries must reject asset ids that alias padding or exceed the field domain.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p transaction-circuit field_alias_asset_id -- --nocapture && cargo test -p transaction-circuit note_validation_rejects_field_aliasing_asset_ids -- --nocapture && cargo test -p disclosure-circuit reject_padding_field_alias_asset_id -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must generate note-commitment vectors and run the production conformance and regression tests.
Wallet NotePlaintext-to-NoteData export must feed the same value, asset, recipient, auth key, rho, and randomness limbs as the Lean/circuit note-commitment transcript, excluding memo data.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks transcript layout, field-limb encoding, and asset-id field canonicality only. It does not prove Poseidon2 cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence beyond the input transcript, note commitment hiding or binding reductions, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, deployed STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier input grammar, deployed note nullifiers absorb domain tag 2 over exactly six field limbs in one Poseidon2 absorb block: prf_key, note position, and four 8-byte big-endian rho limbs.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.NullifierInputs.nullifier_domain_tag_is_two`, `nullifier_inputs_have_six_limbs`, `nullifier_inputs_start_with_prf_and_position`, `nullifier_inputs_absorb_rho_after_position`, and `nullifier_inputs_one_absorb_block` pin the nullifier transcript shape. `gen_nullifier_input_vectors` emits patterned rho and boundary-position cases. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared production `nullifier_inputs` helper, while focused regressions require P3 and SmallWood nullifier preimage builders to route through the same helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for nullifier preimage ordering and field encoding, with production helper reuse across core, Plonky3, and SmallWood paths.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier input theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier input ordering, 8-byte big-endian rho encoding, input count, or domain tag away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
P3 and SmallWood nullifier-input builders must continue matching the shared transaction-core nullifier_inputs helper.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate nullifier vectors and run the production conformance and regression tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks transcript layout and field-limb encoding only. It does not prove Poseidon2 cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence beyond the input transcript, nullifier PRF pseudorandomness, deployed AIR or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public-input shape predicate, fixed-width vectors, boolean flags, inactive-field zeroing, active nullifier/commitment nonzero checks, indexed output-slot commitment/ciphertext-hash facts including direct list-index facts, nonempty transaction admission, canonical balance-slot asset ordering, and stablecoin asset membership must hold before verifier-facing public inputs are accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_input_flag`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_input_nonzero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_active_input_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_output_nonzero_commitment`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_empty_transaction`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_slot_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_padding_not_suffix`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_duplicate_asset`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_stablecoin_missing_asset`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_stablecoin_present` prove representative public-input shape acceptance and rejection facts over the executable predicate. `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_output_slot_facts_at`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.output_slot_at_get_indices`, and helper theorems lift every indexed output slot to active/nonzero-commitment or inactive-zero-commitment-and-ciphertext-hash facts and direct `List.get?` facts for output flags, commitments, and ciphertext hashes. `gen_public_input_vectors` emits Lean-derived malformed flag, inactive nonzero, active zero, empty transaction, balance-slot ordering, and stablecoin-membership examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `TransactionPublicInputsP3::validate` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the TransactionPublicInputsP3 verifier-admission shape. Statement-hash binding and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production TransactionPublicInputsP3 shape validation away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks verifier-facing public-input shape admission only. Output-slot facts and direct output-slot indices are public shape facts, not proof of recipient authorization, note plaintext correctness, encryption confidentiality, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, statement-hash preimage binding, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public/serialized input binding predicate, the public transaction fields and serialized STARK public-input fields must agree on merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot assets, and stablecoin binding payload before verifier-facing TransactionPublicInputsP3 construction succeeds; successful binding exposes the exact P3 public-input field facts consumed by the deployed verifier boundary.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.bindPublicInputs_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_omitted_balance_assets`, and the companion rejection theorems prove representative binding accept/reject facts over the executable predicate. `stablecoinBindingMatches_true_fields`, `bindPublicInputs_some_implies_p3_binding_facts`, and `validBinding_true_has_p3_binding_facts` lift a successful binding into field-level P3 public-input facts: public merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot asset selection, input/output flags, and stablecoin payload fields match the serialized inputs used to construct `TransactionPublicInputsP3`. `gen_public_input_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, omitted-balance-assets fallback, merkle-root mismatch, fee mismatch, signed value-balance mismatch, balance-slot asset mismatch/count mismatch, and stablecoin payload mismatch examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `transaction_public_inputs_p3_from_parts` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the public TransactionPublicInputs / SerializedStarkInputs binding seam and theorem-level P3 public-input field facts. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding; proof-system soundness remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input binding theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production public/serialized input binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input binding vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks public/serialized input field binding before verifier public-input construction, and packages successful binding as P3 public-input field facts. It does not prove Poseidon2 or BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, transaction statement-hash preimage binding, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction proof-wrapper wire and admission predicates, accepted raw TransactionProof wrappers must be exact-consumed canonical bincode with the modeled backend tag and no trailing, truncated, malformed, or invalid-backend alternative bytes; raw canonical wrappers that exact-decode but drift top-level nullifiers away from the public statement must fail decoded admission at the nullifier-vector gate. Accepted decoded wrappers must pass exact/canonical byte admission, supported backend routing, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, top-level nullifier/commitment vector agreement with decoded public inputs, wrapper balance-slot agreement, and final verifier acceptance in that order. Accepted wrappers expose those statement-surface facts as a theorem-level conjunction for downstream proof-artifact lifts. For the SmallWood candidate proof wrapper selector, accepted candidate wrappers must be exact/canonical current or legacy encodings, must expose nonempty inner ARK proof bytes, must select current before legacy when both modeled decoders could accept, and must fail closed on malformed/trailing-byte or empty-inner-proof cases before profile routing. For the SmallWood recursive proof envelope, accepted envelope bytes must exact-decode and canonically reserialize, reject trailing/truncated/invalid-enum/truncated-proof-vector bytes, and match the expected recursive verifier descriptor before proof verification continues.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.valid_dummy_proof_wrapper_accepts`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.trailing_dummy_proof_wrapper_rejects`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.invalid_backend_proof_wrapper_rejects`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.truncated_proof_wrapper_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.malformed_proof_wrapper_rejects` prove representative raw outer TransactionProof wire outcomes. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperWire.top_level_nullifier_drift_wire_accepts_admission_rejects` proves the schema-v2 raw-to-admission fixture is exact/canonical at the wire layer and expected to fail decoded admission at the nullifier-vector mismatch gate. `gen_proof_wrapper_wire_vectors` emits canonical, trailing, invalid-backend, truncated, malformed, and top-level-nullifier-drift bytes, and the production gate compares the canonical fixture to `bincode::serialize(dummy_proof())` before checking `decode_transaction_proof_bytes_exact` rejection labels, canonical reserialization, and decoded admission rejection for the drifted raw fixture. The production gate now also runs an arbitrary-byte mutation corpus for `TransactionProof` wrapper exact decode, comparing `decode_transaction_proof_bytes_exact` to an independent bincode deserialize, full-consumption, and canonical-reencode oracle over malformed, truncated, trailing-byte, inserted-byte, pseudo-random, and mutated-valid wrappers. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.accepts_iff_proof_wrapper_preconditions` proves the executable decoded-admission predicate, and `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.proofWrapperAccepts_implies_statement_surface` packages accepted exact/canonical byte admission, backend support, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, nullifier/commitment vector agreement, balance-slot agreement, and verifier acceptance as theorem-level facts. Companion theorems pin valid acceptance, every rejection label, and the codec/backend/public-input/nullifier-vector/commitment-vector/balance/verifier precedence. `gen_proof_wrapper_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_transaction_proof_wrapper_admission`. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodCandidateWrapperAdmission.wrapper_accepts_iff_selected_canonical_nonempty`, `current_wrapper_selected_precedes_legacy`, `legacy_wrapper_selected_requires_current_rejected`, and companion rejection theorems prove the SmallWood candidate wrapper selector surface, while `gen_smallwood_candidate_wrapper_admission_vectors` checks current, legacy, empty-inner-proof, malformed, and trailing-byte fixtures against production `decode_smallwood_candidate_proof`. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodRecursiveEnvelopeWire.valid_recursive_envelope_accepts`, `alternate_recursive_envelope_accepts`, `trailing_recursive_envelope_rejects`, `truncated_recursive_envelope_rejects`, `invalid_profile_recursive_envelope_rejects`, `truncated_proof_vector_recursive_envelope_rejects`, `matching_descriptor_admission_accepts`, and `mismatched_descriptor_admission_rejects` prove the representative recursive-envelope wire and descriptor handoff table. `gen_smallwood_recursive_envelope_wire_vectors` emits the modeled bincode bytes and descriptor expectations, and production conformance checks `decode_smallwood_recursive_proof_envelope_v1`, canonical reserialization, and descriptor equality before recursive proof verification. Production consensus inline-tx artifact decode and tx-proof manifest summarization use `decode_transaction_proof_bytes_exact`; production Plonky3 and default SmallWood wrapper verification call the shared admission helper after decoded-field admission and around verifier acceptance; the SmallWood candidate verifier exact/canonical-decodes candidate wrappers before profile routing and rejects empty inner proof bytes; recursive proof envelope verification exact-decodes the envelope and checks descriptor equality before direct recursive statement verification; and the tx-proof manifest production summarizer exact-decodes nested proofs, reconstructs verifier public inputs, checks wrapper summary-vector agreement, and routes verifier rejection through the same admission helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors, a raw-to-decoded-admission drift fixture, a recursive-envelope parser/descriptor handoff fixture set, and an arbitrary-byte mutation corpus for proof-wrapper exact/canonical decode. Scope is admission over decoded predicates and production Plonky3, SmallWood, recursive-envelope, and manifest verifier result propagation plus parser-drift detection around bincode full consumption/canonical reserialization, not proof-system soundness or a proof of bincode internals.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the proof-wrapper admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production proof-wrapper admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production raw TransactionProof wrapper exact decoding, trailing-byte rejection, parser rejection labels, canonical reserialization, or decoded-admission rejection of the top-level nullifier drift fixture away from the Lean modeled wire fixtures must fail generated-vector conformance.

Changing production TransactionProof wrapper exact decode acceptance away from bincode deserialize, full-consumption, and canonical-reencode oracle semantics over malformed and mutated byte streams must fail the arbitrary-byte corpus gate.

Changing production SmallWood candidate wrapper decoding, current/legacy selection, trailing-byte rejection, or empty inner proof rejection away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production SmallWood recursive proof-envelope exact decode, canonical reserialization, trailing/truncated/invalid-enum rejection, proof-vector length handling, or descriptor equality handoff away from the Lean modeled fixtures must fail generated-vector conformance.

The tx-proof manifest must reject nested transaction wrappers with trailing bytes, unsupported backend, missing proof bytes, missing serialized public inputs, invalid serialized public inputs, nullifier/commitment vector drift, or balance-slot drift.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate proof-wrapper admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks representative raw TransactionProof wire exact/canonical admission, raw-to-decoded-admission rejection for top-level nullifier drift, decoded proof-wrapper admission ordering over production-derived decoded predicates, wrapper summary-vector agreement, verifier results, and the SmallWood candidate wrapper current/legacy selector surface. It also checks arbitrary-byte parser drift around TransactionProof wrapper bincode full-consumption/canonical-reencode oracle semantics. It does not prove bincode implementation correctness, STARK/SmallWood cryptographic soundness, verifier implementation correctness, statement-hash binding, or complete Rust refinement. The wire fixtures and mutation corpus close representative production-boundary gaps for canonical/trailing/truncated/malformed/invalid-backend, nullifier-drift, and mutated outer wrapper bytes, while arbitrary bincode internals remain an explicit residual.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction spend-authorization relation, successful authorization forces the public input-flag vector, public-nullifier vector, and private witness vector to have matching lengths; each indexed public input slot is then either inactive with a zero public nullifier or active and authorized only by a private witness that reconstructs the note commitment, derives the spend-authority public key from the spend secret, derives the public nullifier from the same secret/rho/position tuple, and verifies Merkle membership under the stated root. Accepted proof wrappers imply this relation only under an explicit spend-authorization proof-system soundness assumption. The SmallWood active-auth-link surface additionally requires active input commitment auth limbs to equal previous commitment-state auth limbs plus PRF-derived spend-auth limbs modulo the deployed Goldilocks field, the SmallWood spend row-boundary surface requires active commitment/nullifier/Merkle rows to bind to the same opening/nullifier/root/position while inactive public nullifier rows are zero, and the SmallWood output row-boundary surface requires active output commitment rows to bind to the note opening and public commitment, active ciphertext rows to bind to the public ciphertext hash, and inactive public output fields to be zero.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_lengths_match` and `transactionSpendAuthorized_witnesses_align_with_public_inputs` prove that successful slot authorization and transaction spend authorization cannot be established over mismatched public flag/nullifier vectors or mismatched private witness vectors. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizedInputWitness_implies_facts` exposes commitment, spend-authority, nullifier, and Merkle-membership facts for every authorized input witness. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_head_active_facts` lifts those facts to the head active public input slot, while `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_active_input_facts_at` lifts them to any indexed active slot. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_input_slot_facts_at` covers every indexed slot, proving inactive slots carry zero nullifiers and active slots expose spend facts. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.accepted_wrapper_implies_spend_authorization` plus `accepted_wrapper_implies_input_slot_facts_at` tie accepted proof-wrapper verification to total slot authorization through the named `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. Negative theorems reject wrong spend secrets, wrong public nullifiers, and wrong Merkle roots in the executable model. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodSpendAuthorization.active_auth_link_constraints_imply_goldilocks_auth_link`, `active_auth_link_mismatched_active_limbs_reject`, `active_auth_link_wraparound_accepts`, and `inactive_auth_link_allows_mismatched_limbs` model the packed SmallWood Goldilocks-field auth-link surface; Lean-generated no-wrap, wraparound, per-limb mismatch, and inactive cases are checked against Rust, while the packed SmallWood wrong-secret regression checks the production constraint-builder path. `active_input_commitment_rows_bind_note_opening`, `active_input_nullifier_rows_bind_public_nullifier`, `active_input_merkle_rows_bind_public_root`, `inactive_input_nullifier_rows_zero`, and `accepted_smallwood_spend_constraints_imply_active_input_spend_boundary` add the row-boundary surface for active commitment/nullifier/Merkle facts and inactive zero-nullifier facts. `active_output_commitment_rows_bind_note_opening`, `active_output_commitment_rows_bind_public_commitment`, `active_output_ciphertext_rows_bind_public_hash`, `active_output_public_commitment_binds_note_opening`, `inactive_output_public_fields_zero`, and `accepted_smallwood_output_constraints_imply_active_output_binding_boundary` add the output row-boundary surface for active commitment/ciphertext facts and inactive zero-output facts. The production builder now zeroes inactive public-nullifier limbs and ties active nullifier rho rows to note-opening rho rows; formal-core gates the valid inline-Merkle witness plus note, nullifier, Merkle, inactive-nullifier, active-output, and inactive-output mutation regressions. The tx-leaf input projection vectors and theorems bind concrete input flags plus compact transaction nullifiers to padded public-input nullifier slots before native tx-leaf statement hashing and P3 public-input reconstruction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production spend-authorization relation and explicit soundness-boundary theorem. It does not prove that deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood verification implies the relation.

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must include the spend-authorization module in the Lean root and claims ledger.
Removing or weakening the spend-authorization boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The Rust SmallWood auth-link helper must match Lean-generated active no-wrap, active wraparound, per-limb mismatch rejection, and inactive mismatch acceptance cases over the Goldilocks field.
The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must reject a witness whose spend secret derives a different active input auth key than the committed note pk_auth.

The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must reject note commitment, nullifier position/rho, Merkle sibling/root, public balance/stablecoin, inactive public-nullifier drift, active output public/row binding drift, and inactive output ciphertext-hash drift.
The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must continue accepting the valid compact inline-Merkle witness after adding spend row-boundary constraints.
The production tx-leaf input-nullifier projection must match Lean-generated prefix, non-prefix, alias, active-zero, missing/extra compact, and malformed-flag cases.
Native tx-leaf statement hashing must use the projected padded public-input nullifier slots, not only the compact transaction nullifier vector.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the executable spend-authorization relation and its accepted-wrapper assumption boundary, plus the SmallWood active-auth-link and spend row-boundary Goldilocks-field constraint surfaces, Lean-generated vector conformance, and production mutation regressions. It uses abstract digest arithmetic and does not prove Poseidon2/BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, hash collision resistance, nullifier PRF security, note commitment hiding/binding, complete deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood proof-system soundness, witness extraction, wallet key custody, note encryption correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean accepted-transaction boundary, accepted proof-wrapper verification implies one abstract transaction relation containing statement-surface facts, valid balance slots and per-asset delta facts, public input shape validity, total indexed input-slot authorization facts, and total indexed output-slot facts, but only under explicit balance and spend proof-system soundness assumptions.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedTransactionSoundness.accepted_wrapper_implies_transaction_relation` combines accepted proof-wrapper admission with `BalanceSoundnessAssumption` and `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. The relation exposes accepted wrapper statement-surface facts, balance slot validity, public input shape validity, input slot authorization, and output slot validity. Companion theorems lift native and stablecoin per-asset deltas plus head active, arbitrary indexed active, total indexed input-slot facts, and total indexed output-slot facts out of that relation. This is the single Lean theorem boundary that future deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness work must discharge. The production residual certificate lifts this accepted-transaction soundness boundary into a review object with no-theft, balance/public, input-slot, output-slot, authorized-delta, and explicit proof/verifier/hash/serialization residual fields.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production accepted-transaction soundness boundary. It is deliberately assumption-bearing and does not certify deployed proof-system soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the accepted-transaction soundness boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the accepted-transaction soundness module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

Canonical, compact SmallWood, and materialized no-theft residual certificates must expose their proof/verifier/hash/serialization/storage/native-node residual fields through named Lean projection theorems.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the abstract accepted-transaction relation and its assumption boundary only, including public output-slot shape facts. It does not prove deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness, witness extraction, verifier implementation correctness, cryptographic hash security, proof privacy, note encryption correctness, recipient ownership, plaintext-to-commitment correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean canonical transaction verifier boundary, one canonical statement surface containing accepted wrapper admission, public/serialized input binding, theorem-level P3 public-input field facts, statement preimage construction, proof binding-message construction, public nullifier/commitment/ciphertext vector agreement, total input/output-vector binding, indexed input/output-slot statement/binding facts, root/fee/balance-slot/stablecoin core statement binding, value-balance agreement, stablecoin payload agreement, active spend binding to the exact anchor/nullifier statement surface, field agreement, and balance-slot asset agreement is sufficient to derive deployed relation facts, native and per-asset authorized-delta projections, and the existing accepted transaction relation under explicit deployed verifier soundness. The boundary now also states spend-only and balance/public-field-only deployed soundness assumptions, direct split-boundary fact packages, direct input-slot, public-delta, and live-policy-authorized stablecoin exception consequences from those split assumptions, a proof-artifact admission statement certificate tying proof-wrapper metadata projection and balance artifact facts to the same canonical statement witness, a packaged no-theft/public-conservation boundary over those split assumptions, SmallWood public balance-boundary facts for native/stablecoin deltas and signed-magnitude canonicality, SmallWood public-statement value binding facts for P3 public-input prefix plus circuit/crypto suffix, SmallWood transcript/profile byte binding facts for domain, verifier profile material, serialized statement bytes, and eight-byte padding, a SmallWood verifier envelope carrying candidate wrapper admission, transcript/profile binding, active auth-link facts, active input/output row facts, balance facts, AIR final-row facts, and the packaged no-theft/public-conservation boundary, a SmallWood public-statement verifier export theorem tying public-statement binding and statement-byte equality to that verifier envelope plus no-theft/public-conservation/non-native exception consequences, P3 AIR final-row public balance/stablecoin equation facts, anti-mismatch theorems proving unauthorized, asset/delta-mismatched, public-payload-mismatched, or policy-metadata-mismatched authorized stablecoin surfaces are impossible, plus a compatibility theorem back to the monolithic assumption. The canonical proof-system production residual certificate additionally packages canonical proof artifact admission, no-theft boundary facts, spend/balance split boundary facts, input/output slot facts, authorized deltas, native delta authorization, exception surfaces, and explicit verifier/proof/hash/serialization residual assumptions. A SmallWood production review certificate now maps the concrete SmallWood residual fields into that canonical residual certificate while keeping wrapper decode equivalence, public-input serialization equivalence, statement-hash collision resistance, STARK/AIR/PCS soundness, witness extraction, and hash/security assumptions explicit. It also includes the SmallWood verifier statement projection boundary: accepted projection exposes candidate-wrapper admission, public-statement binding, transcript binding, exact verifier arithmetization, public values, row/packing/degree metadata, linear-constraint offsets/indices/coefficients/targets, auxiliary witness limb count, profile material, transcript bytes, proof-byte presence, and the explicit verifier-result handoff before the residual proof-system assumption is used.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.deployed_soundness_implies_accepted_transaction_soundness_assumption` collapses the older independent balance and spend assumptions into `DeployedTxVerifierSoundnessAssumption` over `CanonicalTxStatementSurface`. `accepted_wrapper_and_canonical_statement_implies_transaction_relation` then reuses the accepted-transaction theorem, while companion theorems expose public binding validity, P3 public-input binding facts from `canonical_statement_surface_p3_public_input_binding_facts`, statement-preimage length, wrapper preconditions, public shape validity, shape/statement/binding vector agreement for nullifiers, commitments, and ciphertext hashes, input-flag/nullifier vector agreement across public shape, bound inputs, statement preimage, and proof binding message, output-flag/commitment/ciphertext-hash slot facts bound across public shape, statement preimage, and proof binding message, a single `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.canonical_statement_surface_core_binding` package for the merkle-root anchor, fee, balance-slot assets/count, value-balance fields, and stablecoin identity/payload fields across public inputs, statement hash, and proof binding message, active and total input-slot facts bound to the exact statement and binding nullifier vectors, native-delta conservation, and indexed input/output-slot facts from the same canonical surface. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_implies_boundary_facts` packages those surface facts with `DeployedTxRelationFacts`, `AcceptedTransactionRelation`, and the core statement/input/output-vector binding into one target for future AIR/refinement work; `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_public_input_p3_binding_facts`, `canonical_boundary_facts_core_statement_binding`, `canonical_boundary_facts_native_delta`, `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta`, `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta_value`, `canonical_boundary_facts_input_slot_bound_to_statement`, `canonical_boundary_facts_output_slot_bound_to_statement`, and `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_exposes_spend_and_balance` expose the exact P3 public-input field package, core binding, native/per-asset authorized-delta facts, total input-slot statement/proof-binding facts, output-slot statement/proof-binding facts, balance-slot, valid-balance, and spend-authorization facts carried by that target. `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_input_slot_boundary_facts` and `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_output_slot_boundary_facts` now package those input/output slot projections directly from the canonical surface plus the explicit deployed-verifier soundness assumption for downstream proof/AIR refinement work. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.accepted_canonical_statement_surface_with_balance_soundness_implies_proof_artifact_admission_statement_certificate` separately takes an accepted canonical statement surface plus the existing balance-soundness assumption and returns a certificate carrying proof-wrapper metadata projection, wrapper/public/statement/proof-binding facts, balance artifact facts, and native delta on that same statement witness. `Hegemon.Native.TxLeafCanonicalSurface.native_tx_leaf_deployed_verifier_boundary_facts` then composes the packaged boundary with accepted native tx-leaf/action equality admission, `native_tx_leaf_full_statement_artifact_output_slot_full_binding` packages selected output rows with the assumption-free full statement/artifact facts, and `native_tx_leaf_canonical_artifact_boundary_facts` packages the deployed-verifier boundary together with native artifact equality facts, wrapper surface facts, root/fee/balance-slot/stablecoin identity bindings, spend/balance exposure, authorized asset deltas, and receipt/public-input/proof/backend/ciphertext-payload binding. `native_tx_leaf_canonical_artifact_boundary_active_input_no_theft` exposes active artifact input spend facts with wrapper preconditions, wrapper surface facts, public-input binding, statement preimage, proof binding message, root/anchor binding, statement/proof nullifier-slot alignment, and native receipt/public-input/proof/backend equality gates. The split-soundness extension adds `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.signedMagnitudeMatches_true_eq`, `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.canonical_statement_spend_soundness_active_input_bound_to_statement`, `balance_public_field_facts_authorized_asset_delta_value`, `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_public_authorized_asset_delta_value`, and `deployed_soundness_parts_imply_deployed_tx_verifier_soundness_assumption`, making spend authorization and public balance conservation separate future AIR/refinement targets instead of one oversized assumption. The AIR final-row extension adds `Hegemon.Transaction.AirBalanceBoundary.accepted_air_balance_final_row_exposes_boundary_facts` and companion native/stablecoin/non-selected/disabled equations, and the formal-core gate runs `p3_air_balance_public_field_mutations_rejected` so the deployed P3 verifier rejects verifier-facing value-balance and stablecoin issuance public-field drift for a valid stablecoin proof. The SmallWood public-statement extension adds `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodPublicStatementBinding.base_p3_public_vector_length`, `smallwood_public_statement_values_append_version_binding`, `smallwood_public_statement_values_length`, `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_binding_exposes_p3_prefix`, `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_binding_exposes_version_suffix`, `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_binding_forbids_public_value_extension`, and `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_binding_feeds_transcript_surface`; formal-core now generates Lean vectors and runs `lean_generated_smallwood_public_statement_binding_vectors_match_production` so production SmallWood candidate surfaces are checked to expose exactly `TransactionPublicInputsP3::to_vec()` plus circuit/crypto version suffix for normal and stablecoin witnesses before transcript/profile binding. The SmallWood transcript/profile extension adds `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodTranscriptBinding.smallwood_transcript_binding_starts_with_domain`, `smallwood_transcript_binding_includes_profile_material`, `smallwood_transcript_binding_includes_statement_bytes`, `smallwood_transcript_binding_padding_aligned_to_eight`, profile mutation theorems, active-vs-legacy DECS opening-count theorems, and distinct profile-material coverage for all production-recognized SmallWood arithmetization tags; formal-core now generates Lean vectors and runs `lean_generated_smallwood_transcript_binding_vectors_match_production` so production transcript bytes bind domain, version/profile material, every deployed/legacy arithmetization label, serialized statement bytes, and padding. The authorized stablecoin extension adds `stablecoin_mint_exception_authorized_payload_bound_to_statement` and `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_authorized_stablecoin_mint_exception_surface`, carrying an explicit live-policy authorization predicate through the exact public, statement, and proof-binding payload rows without claiming the production lookup is proved. The deeper split-boundary surface adds `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.spend_soundness_canonical_surface_implies_spend_boundary_facts`, `balance_public_soundness_canonical_surface_implies_balance_public_boundary_facts`, `canonical_split_boundary_facts_imply_full_boundary_facts`, `deployed_soundness_parts_canonical_surface_implies_boundary_facts`, `canonical_balance_public_boundary_facts_authorized_public_delta_value`, `canonical_spend_boundary_facts_input_slot_bound_to_statement`, `balance_public_soundness_canonical_surface_authorized_public_delta_value`, `spend_soundness_canonical_surface_input_slot_boundary_facts`, `deployed_soundness_parts_canonical_surface_implies_no_theft_boundary_facts`, and `balance_public_soundness_canonical_surface_authorized_stablecoin_mint_exception`, so downstream refinement can target spend input-slot authorization, public balance-delta consequences, public non-native exception surfaces, and live-policy-authorized stablecoin exceptions separately before rejoining the legacy full boundary. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodVerifierSoundnessEnvelope.accepted_smallwood_surfaces_with_split_soundness_imply_verifier_envelope` composes accepted SmallWood candidate wrapper admission, transcript/profile binding, active auth-link facts, active input row-boundary facts, active output row-boundary facts, SmallWood balance facts, P3 AIR final-row facts, canonical deployed-verifier facts, and the packaged no-theft/public-conservation boundary into one concrete SmallWood verifier-surface envelope while leaving STARK/FRI/PCS soundness as explicit premises. `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_surfaces_with_split_soundness_imply_verifier_export` now composes that verifier envelope with accepted SmallWood public-statement binding and explicit statement-byte equality, exporting no-theft, public conservation, and non-native stablecoin exception consequences through the same public-statement surface. The authorized stablecoin anti-mismatch theorem family projects the exact canonical payload, authorization predicate, public-field equality, bound/statement/proof-binding payload rows, and policy metadata preservation from `AuthorizedStablecoinMintExceptionSurface`, then proves unauthorized, asset-mismatched, delta-mismatched, public-payload-mismatched, and policy-metadata-mismatched surfaces cannot be constructed. `accepted_smallwood_public_statement_surfaces_with_split_soundness_review_certificate` now packages the split spend/balance soundness assumptions with the public-statement verifier export and no-theft/public-conservation consequences as one review certificate. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodVerifierSoundnessEnvelope.accepted_smallwood_public_statement_surfaces_with_split_soundness_and_residuals_imply_canonical_soundness_certificate` packages the SmallWood verifier export, canonical proof-system boundary, proof-artifact statement certificate, balance/AIR facts, and explicit STARK/AIR, PCS, transcript, hash, extraction, and implementation-equivalence residual assumptions into one canonical soundness review certificate. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.accepted_canonical_surface_with_split_soundness_and_residuals_imply_proof_system_production_residual_certificate` takes the canonical surface plus split soundness assumptions and packages proof-artifact statement admission, canonical no-theft boundary facts, public and witness authorized deltas, all input/output slot boundary facts, and explicit wrapper decode, public-input serialization, statement hash, transcript binding, verifier implementation, STARK/AIR/PCS, witness extraction, and Merkle/commitment hash residuals. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.packaged_canonical_boundaries_with_residuals_imply_proof_system_production_residual_certificate` now rebuilds that production residual certificate from already-packaged proof-artifact and no-theft boundary records, `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodVerifierSoundnessEnvelope.accepted_smallwood_public_statement_surfaces_with_split_soundness_and_residuals_imply_production_review_certificate` uses it to co-witness the SmallWood residual export and canonical production residual certificate, and `smallwood_production_review_certificate_projects_compact_residual_certificate` projects that review into a compact certificate carrying statement preimage, proof-binding message, public-input binding, wrapper admission, spend authorization, public asset deltas, native delta, stablecoin exception surface, and explicit residuals without discharging any cryptographic or proof-system residual. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodVerifierStatementProjection.accepted_smallwood_verifier_statement_projection_implies_export_surface` proves that an accepted projection carries every verifier-call field needed by the deployed SmallWood verifier surface. `gen_smallwood_verifier_statement_projection_vectors` emits valid normal/stablecoin inline-Merkle cases plus wrapper, public-statement, transcript, arithmetization, public-value, row-count, packing-factor, constraint-degree, linear-vector, auxiliary-count, profile-material, transcript-byte, empty-proof, and verifier-result drift cases. The production gate reconstructs the actual `PackedStatement` verifier input from `build_smallwood_candidate_profile_surface_for_arithmetization`, checks the public values against the P3 surface, checks the transcript/profile helpers, checks all final packed-statement getters, and keeps the final verifier result as an explicit soundness handoff instead of claiming STARK/AIR/PCS soundness.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production canonical verifier-boundary refinement. It packages the deployed relation, accepted-transaction, transcript, native tx-leaf, and split spend/balance proof-boundary facts for future AIR/refinement proof work, but does not certify deployed STARK, SmallWood, or native verifier soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the canonical verifier-boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the canonical verifier-boundary module in the Lean root and claims ledger.
The SmallWood inline-Merkle production path must reject public balance-field mutation even if the auxiliary Merkle rows are recomputed from the tampered public values.
The SmallWood inline-Merkle production path must reject public stablecoin-field mutation even if the auxiliary Merkle rows are recomputed from the tampered public values.
The P3 production verifier must reject verifier-facing value-balance and stablecoin issuance public-field drift for a valid stablecoin proof.

The SmallWood production transcript binding must include the domain, verifier profile material, every production-recognized arithmetization label/profile split, serialized statement bytes, and zero padding exactly as modeled by Lean.
The SmallWood production public statement must be exactly the P3 public-input vector plus circuit-version and crypto-suite suffix before transcript/profile binding.
Removing the SmallWood formal soundness review certificate must fail the Lean aggregate build or claims check.

The SmallWood production verifier-call surface must match the Lean-modeled projection for arithmetization, public values, row/packing/degree metadata, all linear constraint vectors, auxiliary count, profile material, transcript bytes, proof-byte presence, and verifier-result handoff.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves an assumption-sharpening canonical statement boundary only, including modeled vector agreement, theorem-level P3 public-input field facts, packaged input/output vector agreement, indexed output-slot facts across public shape, statement preimage, and proof binding message, packaged core root/fee/balance-slot/stablecoin statement binding, a proof-artifact admission statement certificate over existing accepted wrapper and balance-soundness assumptions, split spend-only and balance/public-field soundness assumptions, split spend/balance fact packages, a packaged no-theft/public-conservation boundary over those split assumptions, direct input-slot statement binding, public-delta consequences, SmallWood public balance-boundary verifier-surface facts and mutation regressions, SmallWood public-statement P3-prefix/version-suffix binding and vector conformance, SmallWood transcript/profile byte binding and vector conformance, a SmallWood verifier envelope carrying candidate wrapper, transcript, auth-link, input/output row, balance, AIR, and no-theft/public-conservation facts, a public-statement verifier export tying public-statement binding and statement-byte equality to that envelope plus no-theft/public-conservation/non-native exception facts, P3 AIR final-row public balance/stablecoin equation facts and mutation regressions, an explicit live-policy-authorized stablecoin payload surface from those split packages, anti-mismatch authorized stablecoin payload projections, a compatibility theorem back to the monolithic deployed-verifier assumption, native/per-asset authorized-delta projections, native tx-leaf/action admission composition, and a SmallWood-to-canonical production residual review certificate over explicit residual assumptions. It does not prove deployed STARK/PCS soundness, full AIR completeness, SmallWood PCS soundness, verifier implementation correctness beyond targeted gates, witness extraction, hash collision resistance, bincode/postcard implementation correctness, production stablecoin policy/oracle validity, bridge mint authorization, note encryption correctness, recipient ownership, proof privacy, or complete Rust/native-node refinement. The SmallWood verifier statement projection gate proves construction and ordering of the verifier-call surface, not cryptographic verifier soundness, witness extraction, bincode correctness, hash security, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean accepted-transaction boundary, every per-asset delta is authorized: native asset deltas match the modeled native balance, non-native unselected deltas are zero, and any nonzero non-native delta implies the selected stablecoin issuance/burn exception. The canonical proof-system boundary exposes the same native and per-asset authorized-delta facts directly from packaged deployed-verifier boundary facts, and the canonical balance/public-field boundary proves that a non-native nonzero public delta requires a full stablecoin mint exception surface binding the asset, delta, policy hash, oracle commitment, attestation commitment, policy version, statement fields, proof-binding fields, and any explicit live-policy authorization predicate supplied for that exact payload. The public asset-isolation certificate packages public authorized-delta equality, non-native no-exception zeroing, public exception forcing, authorized stablecoin payload binding, and unauthorized-payload rejection as one reusable canonical surface. The authorized surface additionally preserves exact policy metadata through public, bound, statement, and proof-binding rows and cannot be inhabited by unauthorized, mismatched-asset, mismatched-delta, mismatched-public-payload, or mismatched-policy-metadata payloads. The native stablecoin policy authorization table instantiates that live-policy predicate only for an explicitly equal production-authorized payload and proves not-live policies cannot authorize the exact payload. For any list of accepted transaction transitions, the aggregate chain asset delta equals the aggregate authorized delta, and a nonzero non-native aggregate delta requires at least one selected stablecoin exception in the chain.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.AssetIsolation.accepted_transaction_relation_authorized_asset_delta` lifts the accepted-transaction relation into one asset-delta policy. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.canonical_boundary_facts_native_delta`, `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta`, and `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta_value` expose the same policy directly from `CanonicalDeployedVerifierBoundaryFacts`, so future deployed-verifier/AIR refinement work can target the packaged canonical proof boundary instead of re-deriving asset facts separately. `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.public_authorized_asset_delta_value_non_native_nonzero_requires_stablecoin_exception` and `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_non_native_nonzero_public_stablecoin_exception` additionally expose the public-field version: once balance/public-field soundness ties a slot delta to public fields, any non-native nonzero public delta forces `stablecoinEnabled = 1` and `assetId = stablecoinAsset`, while `public_authorized_asset_delta_value_non_native_no_exception_zero` names the zero-delta converse for non-native assets without a public exception. `canonical_statement_surface_stablecoin_mint_exception_surface` and `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_non_native_nonzero_stablecoin_mint_exception_surface` strengthen that consequence into a first-class public exception surface carrying the selected asset, issuance delta, public policy hash, oracle commitment, attestation commitment, policy version, and the matching statement/proof-binding rows. `stablecoin_mint_exception_authorized_payload_bound_to_statement` and `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_authorized_stablecoin_mint_exception_surface` add the explicit authorization-predicate version: if a live-policy predicate authorizes that payload, the theorem carries the authorization through the same public, statement, and proof-binding rows. `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_public_asset_isolation_certificate` now packages public authorized-delta equality, no-exception zeroing, public exception forcing, authorized exception payload binding, and unauthorized payload rejection in one reusable certificate. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodVerifierSoundnessEnvelope.smallwood_public_statement_export_yields_public_asset_isolation_certificate` derives that same certificate from concrete SmallWood public-statement verifier export facts, and `Hegemon.Native.MaterializedTransferNoTheftPublication.smallwood_public_statement_export_yields_native_live_public_asset_isolation_certificate` instantiates it with the native live-policy authorizer for materialized publication paths. `Hegemon.Native.StablecoinPolicyLiveAuthorization.native_policy_authorization_accepts_authorizes_matching_payload` instantiates that predicate from the accepted native table only when the production-authorized payload equals the canonical public payload, and `native_policy_not_live_denies_exact_payload_authorization` proves a not-live policy cannot authorize that exact payload. Companion theorems prove that unselected non-native assets have zero delta and that any nonzero non-native delta must be the selected stablecoin exception. `accepted_chain_asset_delta_authorized`, `accepted_chain_non_native_no_exception_delta_zero`, and `accepted_chain_non_native_nonzero_requires_exception` lift the same policy across accepted transaction lists, making the chain-level stablecoin exception predicate explicit while keeping deployed verifier soundness and bridge authorization outside the theorem. The native projection theorem `tx_leaf_action_accepts_implies_stablecoin_payload_matches` names the production tx-leaf/action stablecoin payload equality gate so asset-isolation review can cite the decoded artifact/action admission check directly. The wallet helper `stablecoin_policy_binding_from_admitted_state` binds the storage-backed policy lookup, policy hash, oracle commitment, attestation commitment, issuance delta, and policy version into the exact submitted stablecoin payload while rejecting inactive policies, ambiguous oracle-feed records, stale oracle commitments, disputed attestations, zero issuance, and over-range issuance. The native helper `evaluate_native_stablecoin_policy_authorization`, mirrored by `Hegemon.Native.StablecoinPolicyAuthorization`, now checks the import-time manifest policy/oracle/attestation/lifecycle/freshness/bounds predicate before transfer state admission accepts enabled stablecoin payloads; replayed dynamic policy/oracle/attestation state remains outside this theorem.
