The transaction balance kernel now names the padding-field-alias rejection theorem, and production `verify_balance_slots` validates canonical stablecoin asset ids before the exception path, so a reserved field representative cannot satisfy the authorized non-native mint surface.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production transaction and accepted-transaction-chain asset-isolation boundary with a production-bound public stablecoin exception payload surface, explicit live-policy authorization predicate, wallet-side storage-backed policy/oracle/attestation payload-construction regression, native import-time protocol-manifest policy/oracle/attestation/lifecycle/freshness/bounds admission, and a theorem bridge from accepted native authorization to the live-policy predicate for exactly matching production/public payloads. It does not certify replayed dynamic stablecoin policy state or bridge mint authorization.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the asset-isolation boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the asset-isolation module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

Wallet stablecoin policy payload construction must bind exact storage-backed policy/oracle/attestation records and fail closed on inactive, ambiguous, stale, disputed, zero-issuance, or over-range records.
The reserved Goldilocks padding-field alias must not inhabit the selected stablecoin issuance exception in Lean or production verification.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves transaction-level and accepted-transaction-list asset isolation under the accepted transaction relation and now projects those facts from the packaged canonical deployed-verifier boundary. It binds the stablecoin exception payload and any explicit authorization predicate to public, statement, and proof-binding fields, proves unauthorized or mismatched authorized payload surfaces impossible, cites the production-bound native tx-leaf/action stablecoin payload equality gate, and gates wallet-side policy/oracle/attestation payload construction plus native manifest lifecycle admission. It does not prove deployed proof-system soundness, replayed dynamic stablecoin policy/oracle/attestation validity against chain state, bridge mint authorization, external proof soundness, cryptographic hash security, proof privacy, note encryption correctness, block/native-node execution equivalence, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction statement-hash, serialized public-input digest, and proof-binding grammars, transaction receipts, consensus tx-leaf binding, public-input digest records, and shielded proof binding hashes must commit to the same byte layouts: statement domain separator, merkle root, padded nullifier/commitment/ciphertext-hash lists, little-endian scalar fields, raw stablecoin flag byte, two's-complement signed i128 balances, tx-public-inputs-digest-v1 postcard bytes, and binding-hash-v3 length-tagged public partitions plus chunk-domain-separated BLAKE2 preimages.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_valid_length`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_padded_vectors`, `Hegemon.Transaction.StatementHash.statementPreimage_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_distinguishes_field_padding_collision_from_padding`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingMessage_distinguishes_vector_partition_lengths`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingHashPreimage_valid_chunk0_length`, `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofStatementBinding.bindingHashPreimage_valid_chunk1_length`, and the companion rejection/separation theorems prove representative executable facts for the statement-hash and proof-binding preimage grammars. `gen_statement_hash_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact statement preimage bytes for full-width, padded, and stablecoin cases plus rejection examples, and emits exact serialized public-input digest preimage bytes for normal and stablecoin verifier inputs. `gen_proof_statement_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact binding-hash-v3 message bytes, chunk-0/chunk-1 BLAKE2 preimage bytes, a same-digest nullifier/commitment repartitioning case, and a padding/sentinel collision case. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared Rust `transaction_statement_preimage_from_parts` / `transaction_statement_hash_from_parts` helpers, `transaction_public_inputs_digest_preimage_from_serialized`, and the production `StarkVerifier::compute_binding_hash` transcript helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for statement-hash byte preimage binding, serialized public-input digest preimage bytes, and proof binding-hash-v3 transcript bytes. SuperNeo receipt paths are regression-checked against the shared statement-hash helper. BLAKE2/BLAKE3 cryptographic security, note/ciphertext derivation, and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the statement-hash preimage theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production statement-hash preimage byte order, padding, signed integer encoding, serialized public-input digest preimage, or digest input away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing consensus receipt or tx-leaf statement-hash binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production binding-hash-v3 public partition lengths, nullifier/commitment repartitioning behavior, scalar encodings, stablecoin encodings, padding-sentinel handling, chunk domain separation, or BLAKE2 preimage bytes away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate statement-hash and proof-binding vectors and run the production conformance tests.
Focused consensus tests must reject malformed inline transaction public-input vectors during receipt and claim derivation without panicking.
Focused SuperNeo receipt tests must reject malformed transaction public-input vectors during canonical and native receipt construction without panicking.
Focused SuperNeo receipt tests must compare canonical and native receipt statement hashes against the shared transaction statement-hash helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the transaction statement-hash byte preimage grammar, serialized public-input digest preimage grammar, the proof binding-hash-v3 transcript grammar, and Rust digest calls over those checked preimages only. Blueprint bindings and regressions additionally require consensus and SuperNeo receipt derivation to use the fallible statement-hash path for untrusted inline artifacts. Proof binding-hash-v3 does not include transaction balance_tag, circuit_version, or crypto_suite; those are covered by the transaction statement-hash grammar. It does not prove BLAKE2/BLAKE3 collision resistance, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, postcard implementation correctness beyond the checked vector cases, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean wallet note-ciphertext wire/decrypt/plaintext grammar, crypto-format note ciphertexts, chain-format fixed containers, and DA sidecar fixed containers are accepted only when their byte fields are exactly consumed, the active note-ciphertext version is v3 on chain/DA wallet admission, the ML-KEM ciphertext length is fixed to 1568 bytes, chain/DA container padding is zero, chain KEM length uses canonical SCALE compact encoding, accepted parser summaries bind the expected version/suite/KEM fields, byte-bounded accepted chain wires have the fixed production wire length and project to the exact DA ciphertext-hash preimage bytes by deleting the canonical compact KEM-length field, valid and alternate valid chain fixtures project to DA bytes that parse under the DA grammar with the same public summary while raw/projected bytes differ, selected native observer rows can bind those projected DA bytes to the public ciphertext-hash row through an explicit hash-match predicate, decrypt admission rejects version, crypto-suite, and diversifier mismatches before crypto, decrypt success implies those metadata fields match and the explicit crypto-authentication predicate is true, decrypted plaintext plus recipient material exports the exact note-data fields whose commitment is the spend-authorization note commitment relation, active public output slots can be tied to that exported plaintext commitment, selected native tx-leaf privacy-game rows that successfully decrypt under recipient material reconstruct the same plaintext-derived commitment carried by public-shape, statement, and proof-binding rows, and representative truncation, overrun, wrong-version, padding, noncanonical, trailing-byte, DA-projection, metadata-mismatch, wrong-recipient, wrong-key, malleated-ciphertext, full-view witness-binding, native tx-leaf commitment/ciphertext-hash consistency, and same-plaintext re-encryption freshness cases reject or agree as expected.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_valid_accepts`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_truncated_after_kem_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.crypto_trailing_byte_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_valid_accepts`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_memo_overrun_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_nonzero_padding_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_wrong_version_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_noncanonical_compact_kem_length_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.chain_trailing_byte_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_valid_accepts`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_memo_overrun_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_nonzero_padding_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_wrong_version_rejects`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_truncated_kem_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.da_trailing_byte_rejects` pin representative accept/reject facts over the executable parser grammar. `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_crypto_ciphertext_has_fixed_kem_len`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_container_has_gamma_suite_and_fixed_kem`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_container_has_supported_version`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_ciphertext_has_gamma_suite_and_fixed_kem`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_ciphertext_has_supported_version`, `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_da_ciphertext_has_gamma_suite_and_fixed_kem`, and `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_da_ciphertext_has_supported_version` lift the sample grammar to generic accepted-summary version/suite/KEM facts. `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.bounded_parse_compact_mlkem_consumes_two` and `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.parsed_chain_ciphertext_has_fixed_wire_length_of_bounded` prove that bounded accepted chain wires consume exactly the fixed chain container, the two canonical compact ML-KEM length bytes, and the ML-KEM ciphertext bytes; `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextWire.valid_chain_da_projection`, `valid_chain_da_projection_length`, `alternate_chain_da_projection`, `valid_chain_da_projection_parses_as_da_same_summary`, `alternate_chain_da_projection_parses_as_da_same_summary`, `chain_noncanonical_compact_kem_length_da_projection_rejects`, `chain_trailing_byte_da_projection_rejects`, `parsed_chain_ciphertext_has_projected_da_bytes_of_bounded`, `parse_da_note_ciphertext_of_container_and_fixed_kem`, `parsed_chain_ciphertext_projected_da_parses_same_summary_of_bounded`, `accepted_chain_ciphertext_binds_da_projection_boundary_of_bounded`, and `accepted_chain_ciphertext_binds_da_hash_preimage_of_bounded` prove that accepted bounded chain wires project to fixed DA preimage bytes by removing those compact length bytes, that the projected bytes reparse under the DA grammar with the same summary for every bounded accepted chain wire, and that the public ciphertext-hash row is tied to those DA bytes through an explicit hash-match predicate. The bounded hypotheses are required because the Lean byte model is Nat while production bytes are u8. The Lean decrypt-boundary theorems `Hegemon.Wallet.NoteCiphertextDecrypt.decrypt_admission_rejects_version_mismatch`, `decrypt_admission_rejects_crypto_suite_mismatch`, `decrypt_admission_rejects_diversifier_mismatch`, `wrong_recipient_or_malleated_ciphertext_fails_under_crypto_assumptions`, `decrypt_success_implies_metadata_matches`, `accepted_chain_ciphertext_decrypt_binds_wire_parser_metadata`, `accepted_chain_ciphertext_decrypt_binds_da_hash_and_residuals`, and `accepted_chain_ciphertext_decrypt_binds_production_serialization_profile` prove the wallet decrypt admission ordering, package accepted chain-wire supported-version/parser metadata plus projected DA reparse agreement, bind production AAD bytes to decrypt material, carry malformed-ciphertext fail-closed facts, and state wrong-recipient/malleated-byte rejection plus ML-KEM IND-CCA, AEAD ciphertext security, wallet KDF domain separation, and encryption RNG freshness as explicit residual assumptions rather than in-repo primitive proofs. The Lean plaintext-binding theorems `Hegemon.Wallet.NotePlaintextCommitment.exported_note_data_replays_plaintext_and_material_fields`, `exported_note_data_commitment_uses_spend_authorization_relation`, `input_witness_from_recovered_reconstructs_note_commitment`, `input_witness_from_exported_plaintext_reconstructs_commitment`, `decrypt_success_plaintext_to_commitment_boundary`, and `active_output_slot_commitment_matches_exported_plaintext` connect decrypted wallet plaintext and recipient material to the same abstract note commitment relation used by spend authorization and public output slots. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_decrypts_selected_output_to_statement_commitment` composes that decrypt/plaintext boundary with the selected native tx-leaf observer row under the ciphertext privacy game: the selected summary decrypts under recipient material, the plaintext-derived commitment equals the public commitment, and that commitment is the exact one indexed in the public shape, statement preimage, and proof-binding message while public metadata, batch/timing leakage, and raw-wire indistinguishability remain explicit. `gen_note_ciphertext_wire_vectors` emits schema-v7 representative parser cases with expected wire lengths, chain/DA wrong-version rejection, expected DA projection bytes, projected DA parser summaries, an alternate same-summary/different-wire chain fixture, and decrypt-admission cases for valid decrypt, metadata mismatch rejection, wrong-recipient rejection, and KEM/note/memo malleation rejection; the production wallet test checks those vectors against `synthetic_crypto::note_encryption::NoteCiphertext::from_bytes`, `wallet::NoteCiphertext::from_chain_bytes`, `wallet::NoteCiphertext::from_da_bytes`, `wallet::NoteCiphertext::to_da_bytes`, `ciphertext_hash_bytes`, and concrete wallet decrypt results. The independent `note_ciphertext_chain_and_da_parsers_match_independent_byte_oracle_on_mutation_corpus` gate compares production chain/DA parser summaries to a separate fixed-container, compact-length, exact-size, padding, version, suite, and KEM-length byte oracle over valid, malformed, truncated, trailing, noncanonical compact, overrun, deterministic-noise, and mutated-valid ciphertext wires. Focused Rust regressions also check wallet note-ciphertext version admission across chain, DA, and crypto-format ingress plus chain/DA encoding, the no-panic truncated crypto parser, wallet chain/DA parser canonicality rules, node/archive RPC ciphertext decoding through the strict DA parser, synthetic-crypto decrypt rejection for wrong recipient, wrong secret key, version tamper, KEM tamper, note-payload tamper, and memo-payload tamper, wallet decrypt rejection for metadata mismatches, wrong root key, and KEM/note/memo payload malleation, full-view decryption to NoteData/input/output witness commitment equality, tx-builder equality from recipient-decrypted ciphertext to outgoing disclosure, transaction bundle, decoded native tx-leaf public commitments, decoded native tx-leaf public ciphertext hashes recomputed from decoded note DA bytes, and native tx-leaf verification, plus same-plaintext same-recipient encryption freshness over the wallet `RngCore` path.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for wallet note-ciphertext parser grammar, active-version admission, chain-to-DA parser-equivalence fixtures, decrypt-admission vectors, plaintext-to-commitment witness binding, native tx-leaf commitment consistency, and an independent chain/DA parser mutation-oracle corpus. Privacy, concrete hash/commitment implementation equivalence, KEM/AEAD security, and complete wallet/native equivalence remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening note-ciphertext wire theorems or proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production crypto, chain, DA, chain-to-DA projected note-ciphertext parsing, or wallet decrypt admission away from the Lean executable grammar must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing production chain or DA note-ciphertext parser acceptance away from the independent fixed-container/compact-length/exact-size byte oracle must fail the mutation corpus gate.
The synthetic-crypto parser must reject truncated KEM/payload prefixes without panicking and reject trailing bytes.
The wallet chain parser must reject memo overruns, unsupported ciphertext versions, nonzero fixed-container padding, noncanonical compact KEM lengths, and trailing bytes.

The wallet DA parser and node/archive ciphertext decoder must reject memo overruns, unsupported ciphertext versions, nonzero fixed-container padding, truncated KEM bytes, and trailing bytes instead of accepting malformed public DA ciphertexts.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p wallet from_da_bytes_rejects_ --lib -- --nocapture && cargo test -p wallet decode_ciphertext_entries_ --lib -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The wallet note-ciphertext boundary must reject unsupported versions across chain, DA, and crypto-format ingress and refuse to encode unsupported versions to chain or DA bytes.
The synthetic-crypto note decrypt wrapper must reject wrong recipients, wrong secret keys, version tampering, KEM ciphertext malleation, note payload malleation, and memo payload malleation.

The wallet note decrypt wrapper must reject version, crypto-suite, and diversifier metadata mismatches before crypto and reject wrong root keys plus KEM/note/memo payload malleation as decryption failures.
Full-view wallet decryption must export NoteData whose commitment equals the plaintext-derived input/output witness commitment.

Wallet transaction construction must keep recipient-decrypted note commitments equal to outgoing disclosure commitments, bundle commitments, decoded native tx-leaf commitments, decoded native tx-leaf ciphertext hashes recomputed from decoded note DA bytes, and a verifying native tx-leaf artifact.
Same-plaintext same-recipient wallet encryption must consume fresh KEM randomness, producing different KEM ciphertexts and chain wire bytes while preserving decrypt correctness.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate note-ciphertext wire vectors and run the Rust conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks representative parser grammar for crypto note ciphertext bytes, wallet chain note ciphertext containers, and wallet DA sidecar note ciphertext containers, plus generic accepted-summary version/suite/KEM facts, byte-bounded fixed chain-wire length and structural DA-preimage projection for the modeled parsers, explicit selected-row projected-DA-to-public-ciphertext-hash predicate binding, metadata-gated decrypt admission over an explicit crypto-authentication predicate, and abstract plaintext-to-note-data commitment binding through the spend-authorization commitment relation. Concrete note-commitment input order and asset-id field admission are covered by `formal.transaction-note-commitment-inputs`; the selected-row hash predicate is a binding surface, not a Lean proof of BLAKE3 or hash implementation security. This node does not prove ML-KEM security or implementation equivalence, AEAD confidentiality, wallet key custody, Poseidon2/BLAKE3 hash security or full implementation equivalence, nullifier derivation, BLAKE3 ciphertext-hash security or implementation equivalence, DA availability, full DA-root inclusion, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean privacy observer model, the full public observer view of a shielded transaction consists of verifier-facing public inputs, ciphertext bytes, ciphertext summaries parsed from chain wire bytes, block height, and action index. The public metadata view removes raw ciphertext bytes while retaining public inputs, parsed summaries, and placement. The batch/timing view separately exposes active-output count, public ciphertext count, parsed-summary count, block height, and action index. Changing private witnesses, prover randomness, local action metadata, local address metadata, local wallet bookkeeping metadata, or local network metadata cannot change those projections; equal public inputs, equal parsed summaries, and equal placement imply equal public metadata leakage without requiring equal ciphertext bytes; valid observer-chain worlds with equal public inputs and placement imply equal batch/timing leakage. Parsed summaries have the same length as the public chain ciphertext byte list, accepted parsed summaries have the expected chain suite/KEM shape, valid observer-chain surfaces tie parsed ciphertext count to the public active-output count, equal public inputs, chain ciphertext wire bytes, and placement imply equal full allowed leakage, accepted native tx-leaf output-slot binding composes with that observer surface under the canonical deployed verifier boundary, an active native tx-leaf output slot forces nonempty observer ciphertext bytes and summaries, that active slot's public active-output rank is in bounds for the observer ciphertext lists, that rank selects a concrete observer wire row that parses to the same chain-format summary, the selected active slot exposes direct public-shape/statement/proof-binding vector indices for commitment and ciphertext-hash fields, byte-bounded selected observer wire rows have the fixed production chain ciphertext wire length and project to fixed-length DA preimage bytes, selected projected DA bytes bind to the public ciphertext-hash row under an explicit hash-match predicate, and the selected active-output ciphertext boundary carries the native ciphertext-hash, ciphertext-payload-hash, and output-count equality gates with those observer and statement/proof facts. The selected active-output public-metadata boundary additionally packages tx-leaf/action binding, canonical statement output-slot binding, left/right valid observer-chain surfaces, same public metadata leakage, and observer ciphertext-count equality without requiring raw ciphertext equality. Observer-visible wallet ciphertext archive publication is accepted only when archive indices are contiguous from zero and each row is below the commitment leaf count.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_private_witness`, `public_metadata_view_ignores_private_witness`, `batch_timing_view_ignores_private_witness`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_prover_randomness`, `public_metadata_view_ignores_prover_randomness`, `batch_timing_view_ignores_prover_randomness`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_ignores_private_witness_and_randomness`, `public_metadata_view_ignores_private_witness_and_randomness`, and `batch_timing_view_ignores_private_witness_and_randomness` prove that note values, asset ids, note randomness, spend secret seeds, plaintext seeds, memo seeds, and prover randomness are outside the observer, public-metadata, and batch/timing projections. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_public_metadata_leakage_of_public_summaries_and_placement` defines the deterministic public-metadata equality boundary for privacy games whose ciphertext wires may differ under a crypto assumption. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_batch_timing_leakage_of_valid_public_inputs_and_placement` defines the explicit batch/timing leakage boundary: valid observer-chain worlds with equal public inputs and placement have equal active-output count, public ciphertext count, parsed-summary count, block height, and action index. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.parsed_chain_ciphertext_summaries_length` proves parser-derived summaries align with the public chain ciphertext byte list, while `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.parsed_chain_ciphertext_summaries_have_chain_format`, `parsed_chain_ciphertext_summary_at_rank`, `valid_observer_chain_surface_ciphertext_at_rank`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.observer_view_summaries_have_chain_format` prove accepted observer summaries have the expected chain suite/KEM shape and that any in-bounds observer ciphertext rank selects a public wire row parsed to the same chain-format summary. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.valid_observer_chain_surface_ciphertext_count` proves valid observer-chain surfaces have exactly one parsed ciphertext summary per active public output; `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.output_slot_active_flag_count_nonzero` proves an indexed active public output slot forces a nonzero active-output count; `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.output_slot_active_rank_lt_count` proves that slot's public active-output rank is below the active-output count; and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_preserves_active_output_count`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_public_valid_observer_surfaces_ciphertext_count`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_valid_observer_chain_surfaces` lift that count boundary through same-public-input and same-leakage worlds. `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_public_chain_wire_and_placement`, `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_of_public_wire_and_placement`, and `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer.same_allowed_leakage_iff_observer_view_eq` define the stronger full-leakage equality boundary future ZK, ciphertext confidentiality, wallet metadata, local network metadata, and timing privacy games must refine when ciphertext bytes are also equal. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_output_slot_bound_to_observer_surface`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_slot_forces_nonempty_observer_ciphertexts`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_slot_has_observer_rank`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_slot_has_parsed_observer_wire_at_rank`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_slot_has_indexed_statement_observer_wire`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_slot_has_indexed_statement_observer_wire_fixed_chain_shape`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_ciphertext_boundary_facts`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_ciphertext_boundary_has_projected_da_bytes`, `native_tx_leaf_active_output_ciphertext_boundary_binds_projected_da_hash`, and `native_tx_leaf_output_slot_same_chain_wire_preserves_allowed_leakage` compose that boundary with the accepted native tx-leaf/action output-slot binding theorem, so output commitment/ciphertext slots bound to the canonical statement vectors also determine the observer count/format surface, active outputs force nonempty observer ciphertext bytes/summaries, active output ranks are in bounds for those observer lists and select parsed public chain wire rows, selected active slots expose direct shape/statement/proof-binding vector indices for commitments and ciphertext hashes, byte-bounded selected wire rows have the fixed production chain ciphertext wire length and fixed-length DA projection bytes, a selected-row hash-match predicate explicitly binds those projected DA bytes to the public ciphertext-hash row, native ciphertext-hash, ciphertext-payload-hash, and output-count equality gates are carried with the selected row, and same-chain-wire leakage equality is preserved. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_active_output_public_metadata_boundary` then packages the deterministic public-metadata boundary for an active native output row: tx-leaf/action binding, canonical statement output-slot facts, left/right valid observer-chain surfaces, same public metadata leakage, native ciphertext equality gates, and observer ciphertext-count equality are available without requiring raw ciphertext equality. The observer boundary consumes the raw-wire split public metadata certificate as a single theorem object that separates intentionally public chain metadata and ciphertext summaries from hidden witness, randomness, wallet, action, address, and local network metadata. `Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextArchiveBoundary.accepts_iff_no_first_rejection`, `first_rejection_from_none_contiguous`, `first_rejection_from_none_rows_below_leaf_count`, and `accepted_ciphertext_archive_publication_binds_contiguous_indices_below_leaf_count` model the wallet ciphertext archive publication gate: accepted archive rows are contiguous from index 0 and strictly below `leaf_count`, while gap and leaf-count rejection cases fail closed. `gen_ciphertext_archive_boundary_vectors` checks those cases against `hegemon_walletCiphertexts`, including an accepted prefix below `leaf_count`, archive gaps, and rows beyond `leaf_count`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production leakage-boundary model. It defines the observer projection used by future privacy games, composes native tx-leaf output-slot binding into that surface, and now proves combined private-witness/prover-randomness/local action/address/wallet-bookkeeping/network metadata exclusion for observer, public metadata, batch/timing, and valid-chain-surface projections. It does not prove cryptographic zero-knowledge or ciphertext confidentiality. The wallet ciphertext archive publication gate is now Lean/vector checked for contiguous indices and leaf-count bounds.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the privacy observer boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the observer module in the Lean root and claims ledger.
PQ connection info and relay config sentinel metadata must not change consensus-carrying proto wire bytes or decoded consensus payload projection.
Changing native wallet ciphertext archive publication to accept gaps or rows beyond the commitment leaf count must fail generated-vector conformance.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves only the formal observer projection, public metadata projection that excludes raw ciphertext bytes, explicit batch/timing projection for active-output count, ciphertext count, parsed-summary count, block height, and action index, parser-derived chain-wire summary boundary, accepted-summary suite/KEM format boundary, byte-bounded fixed chain-wire length and DA-preimage projection boundary, explicit selected-row projected-DA-to-public-ciphertext-hash predicate binding, public active-output ciphertext-count/nonemptiness/rank/parsed-row/indexed-vector boundary, selected-row native ciphertext equality-gate packaging, active-output public-metadata boundary packaging, native tx-leaf output-slot-to-observer composition under the explicit deployed verifier soundness assumption, public-metadata equality boundary, batch/timing equality boundary, full allowed-leakage equality boundary when chain wires are equal, and combined local action/address/network metadata exclusion from observer/public/batch projections and valid observer-chain surfaces. It does not prove public ciphertext-hash equality/security for selected wire bytes without that predicate, BLAKE3 security, full DA-root inclusion, simulator-based zero-knowledge, proof-system privacy, ML-KEM/AEAD confidentiality, ciphertext indistinguishability, wallet metadata hygiene, timing privacy, remote topology/network privacy, hash security, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean ciphertext privacy-game boundary, two valid observer-chain worlds with equal public inputs, equal parsed ciphertext summaries, and equal placement preserve public ciphertext shape, active-output count, ciphertext summary count, accepted chain-format summary facts, public metadata leakage, and explicit batch/timing leakage even when their raw ciphertext bytes are not equal; modeled unlinkability reduces without new assumptions to equal public shape, equal public metadata, equal batch/timing leakage, and the explicit raw-wire indistinguishability premise; those public metadata and batch/timing projections remain stable under independent left/right private-witness, prover-randomness, local-action metadata, local address metadata, local wallet bookkeeping metadata, and local network metadata resampling; the open-assumption package carries the deterministic public boundary, the resampling boundary, the raw-wire-excluded public-leakage package, the same-wire full-leakage discharge, and the exact raw-wire/proof-system/wallet/timing/network assumptions in one theorem; every active native tx-leaf output slot composed with that privacy game selects the same parsed public summary and same statement/proof-bound commitment/ciphertext-hash metadata in both worlds, and that active-output public metadata boundary remains true after independent secret/prover-randomness/local-action and local-network metadata resampling for selected and all active rows; every active row also exposes fixed chain wire lengths and projected DA bytes for both worlds while carrying explicit ML-KEM, AEAD, KDF, RNG, and raw-wire indistinguishability assumptions; every active row that successfully decrypts under recipient material reconstructs the exact plaintext-derived commitment carried by the public shape, statement preimage, and proof-binding message while its projected DA preimages satisfy the explicit ciphertext-hash predicate; accepted materialized sidecar publication composes with that all-active projected-DA and open-assumption decrypt boundary while carrying sidecar availability/size agreement, wire replay DA-row binding, tx-leaf ciphertext publication, and statement/proof ciphertext-vector binding; the PQ transport residual certificate composes accepted PQ handshake transport/wrapper facts with the native ciphertext privacy boundary while exposing ML-KEM, AEAD, KDF, OS RNG, parser/wire exactness, raw-wire, and privacy-policy assumptions as fields; the seed-hardened PQ residual certificate additionally requires the accepted KEM transcript/KDF certificate to expose OS-RNG seed source/length, supplied-seed ML-KEM consumption, transcript-as-HKDF-salt, and ordered KEM-secret IKM before the privacy residuals are used; the materialized-sidecar PQ certificate composes that seed-hardened residual certificate with the accepted materialized sidecar publication facts and all-active open-assumption decrypt-to-DA observer boundary on one witness; if their public chain wire bytes are exactly equal then they already have the same full allowed leakage, while the only remaining cryptographic obligation for different same-shape wires is the explicit `wireIndistinguishable` premise. Production wallet encryption must also fail closed against accidental deterministic same-plaintext same-recipient encryption by consuming fresh KEM randomness, emitting different chain/DA wire bytes under distinct RNG streams, preserving the Lean-modeled public ciphertext summary shape across those re-encryptions, matching the Lean wallet output-batch public-shape table, and keeping wallet-local bookkeeping plus address-cursor metadata out of the public ciphertext projection, while production sidecar materialization must fail closed against arrival-time-only metadata drifting into the semantic observer projection, and PQ connection/relay metadata must stay out of consensus-carrying wire payload projection. Observer-visible wallet ciphertext archive publication is accepted only when archive indices are contiguous from zero and each row is below the commitment leaf count.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean structure `Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextPrivacy.CiphertextPrivacyGame` is intentionally not a cryptographic proof. It packages the observer-chain validity predicates from `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer`, equality of public inputs, equality of parsed ciphertext summaries, equal block/action placement, and one explicit proposition `wireIndistinguishable` plus its proof. The theorems `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_public_ciphertext_shape`, `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_active_output_count`, `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_ciphertext_summary_count`, and `ciphertext_privacy_game_summaries_have_chain_format` prove that this boundary preserves all modeled public ciphertext-shape facts before any cryptographic assumption is used. `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_public_metadata_leakage` additionally proves that the non-wire public metadata projection is identical for the two worlds; this is the deterministic privacy-game fact needed when raw ciphertext wires differ but are covered by the crypto premise. `ciphertext_privacy_game_unlinkability_reduces_to_raw_wire_indistinguishability` packages the no-new-assumptions reduction from modeled unlinkability to equal public shape, equal public metadata, equal batch/timing leakage, and the explicit raw-wire indistinguishability premise, while `ciphertext_privacy_game_raw_wire_excluded_public_leakage_facts` records that public metadata and batch/timing leakage do not require raw wire equality and that same raw wire discharges full observer leakage. `ciphertext_privacy_game_boundary_facts` packages those deterministic facts with first-class proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching policy, and network metadata policy assumptions, and proves full observer, public metadata, and batch/timing projections ignore private witnesses and prover randomness once public wire data is fixed. `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_action_metadata_boundary_facts` and `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_action_metadata_open_assumption_boundary_facts` lift the same game across independently changed local action metadata while preserving the explicit open assumptions; `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_address_metadata_boundary_facts` and `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_address_metadata_open_assumption_boundary_facts` do the same for wallet-local address metadata such as address cursors. `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_network_metadata_boundary_facts` and `ciphertext_privacy_game_local_network_metadata_open_assumption_boundary_facts` do the same for local PQ connection and relay metadata such as peer tags, connection epochs, counters, and gossip-hop tags. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_active_output_ciphertext_privacy_game_public_metadata_boundary` composes that public-metadata theorem with an active native tx-leaf output slot: both worlds select public observer rows at the same active-output rank, those rows parse to the same public summary, and the same shape/statement/proof-binding commitment and ciphertext-hash indices plus native ciphertext equality gates are carried across the game. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_active_output_slot_selects_same_public_summary` packages the same selected-row boundary together with preserved public metadata leakage, preserved batch/timing leakage, and the explicit `wireIndistinguishable` obligation, and `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_select_same_public_boundary` quantifies that package over every active output slot. The active-output resampling theorems prove that the selected/all-active native public metadata row package survives independent left/right private-witness, prover-randomness, local-action metadata, and combined secret-plus-local resampling while carrying the explicit proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_selected_output_wire_da_commitment_boundary` composes that selected-row package with the wallet chain-wire boundary for both worlds: each selected wire is byte-bounded, parses to the same public summary, projects to fixed-length DA bytes, and carries the same public shape, statement, proof-binding, ciphertext-hash, payload-hash, and output-count gates while ML-KEM, AEAD, KDF, RNG, and raw-wire indistinguishability remain explicit assumptions; `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_projected_da_boundary` lifts that projected-DA package to all active output slots. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_decrypt_to_statement_commitment_and_projected_da_hash` lifts the selected decrypt/projected-DA theorem to every active output slot: any active row that successfully decrypts under matching recipient material reconstructs the statement/proof-bound plaintext commitment and carries explicit left/right projected-DA hash predicates. `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_open_assumption_decrypt_da_boundary` packages that all-active decrypt boundary with `ciphertext_privacy_game_open_assumption_boundary_facts`, so deterministic observer leakage, secret-resampling stability, raw-wire-excluded public leakage, same-wire discharge, and proof-system/wallet/timing/network assumptions travel with every active output row. `Hegemon.Privacy.PqTransportPrivacyBoundary.accepted_pq_handshake_native_ciphertext_privacy_boundary_residual_certificate` then packages the accepted PQ handshake transport/wrapper completion facts with the native ciphertext privacy certificate and names the residual ML-KEM, AEAD, KDF, OS RNG, parser/wire exactness, raw-wire, and privacy-policy assumptions without adding runtime work. `Hegemon.Privacy.PqTransportPrivacyBoundary.accepted_pq_kem_transcript_kdf_certificate_excludes_implementation_seed_misuse` extracts the implementation boundary from the accepted PQ KEM transcript/KDF certificate: both encapsulation seeds are OS-RNG-sourced 32-byte seeds consumed by ML-KEM, the public transcript enters HKDF as salt, and KEM shared secrets enter IKM in handshake order. `accepted_pq_kem_certificate_native_ciphertext_privacy_boundary_seed_hardened_residual_certificate` composes that seed/KDF boundary with the native ciphertext privacy residual certificate, so implementation seed misuse is no longer a privacy residual; primitive security, OS RNG quality, parser/wire exactness, raw-wire indistinguishability, and policy assumptions remain explicit. `accepted_pq_kem_materialized_sidecar_ciphertext_privacy_boundary_seed_hardened_residual_certificate` then packages that seed-hardened PQ residual certificate with `MaterializedSidecarDaBlobPublicationFacts`, sidecar availability/size and wire-replay DA bindings, tx-leaf ciphertext publication, statement/proof ciphertext vectors, the native open-assumption boundary, and all-active decrypt-to-DA observer facts on the same materialized witness. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeSidecarObserverSurface.materialized_sidecar_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_projected_da_boundary` composes the all-active projected-DA package with `Hegemon.Native.MaterializedSidecarDaBlobPublication.MaterializedSidecarDaBlobPublicationFacts`, and `materialized_sidecar_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_open_assumption_decrypt_da_boundary` carries the all-active open-assumption decrypt package through the same materialized sidecar publication surface, preserving sidecar ciphertext availability, size agreement, wire replay DA-row binding, tx-leaf ciphertext hash/payload-hash publication, and statement/proof ciphertext-vector equality. The production regression `materialized_sidecar_observer_projection_ignores_received_time` builds two sidecar transfers that differ only in arrival time, requires their raw pending hashes to differ, and requires their semantic hashes, materialized ciphertexts, replay keys, and consensus transaction observer fields to match. `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_batch_timing_leakage` proves that the explicit batch/timing projection, including active-output count, public ciphertext count, parsed-summary count, block height, and action index, is also identical under the same valid same-public-input and same-placement premises. `ciphertext_privacy_game_only_open_crypto_obligation` exposes the exact remaining cryptographic premise, and `same_wire_ciphertext_privacy_game_implies_same_allowed_leakage` connects the boundary back to the stronger observer same-leakage theorem when no wire privacy assumption is needed because the public wires are identical. The production regression `encrypt_same_plaintext_to_same_address_uses_fresh_kem_randomness` builds one note and recipient, encrypts it twice through the wallet `RngCore` path with distinct RNG streams, requires different ML-KEM ciphertexts and different chain/DA wire bytes and ciphertext hashes, and confirms both ciphertexts decrypt to the same plaintext. `reencryption_preserves_public_ciphertext_summary_shape` checks that those different raw chain/DA wires preserve the Lean-modeled public summary fields: version, crypto suite, diversifier, KEM length, note payload length, memo payload length, and fixed wire lengths after chain and DA parsing. `local_wallet_bookkeeping_does_not_change_public_ciphertext_projection` checks that sync height/hash, genesis hash, ciphertext cursor, tracked-note metadata, pending status, pending-recipient memo, and submission timestamp bookkeeping do not change public ciphertext summary, chain wire, DA bytes, or ciphertext hash. `local_address_metadata_does_not_change_public_ciphertext_projection` advances and persists `next_address_index` through address reservation and reopen, then checks the same production chain ciphertext bytes, DA bytes, and ciphertext hash for the already-created output. The Lean wallet output-batch vectors now also bind newly built change-output public metadata: production computes transfer, stablecoin, burn, and consolidation output projections through the same `change_output_address` helper used by construction and checks every change output uses the cursor-independent primary diversifier even when local address metadata seeds differ. `ciphertext_privacy_game_secret_resampling_boundary_facts` packages the same boundary into an explicit unlinkability-style resampling theorem: after the privacy-game public fields are fixed, independently replacing the left and right private witnesses, prover randomness, and local metadata, including local network metadata, preserves the public metadata projection and the explicit batch/timing projection, while the raw-wire indistinguishability, proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions remain first-class fields rather than hidden conclusions. `ciphertext_privacy_game_open_assumption_boundary_facts` closes the review gap by packaging the deterministic public boundary, the secret-resampling boundary, the raw-wire-excluded public-leakage package, the same-wire full-leakage theorem, and the exact remaining raw-wire, proof-system, wallet, timing, and network assumptions into one theorem-backed boundary object. `Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextPrivacy.accepted_ciphertext_privacy_game_public_metadata_privacy_certificate` and `raw_wire_split_public_metadata_privacy_certificate` package intentionally public metadata separately from private witness, prover randomness, local action/address/wallet bookkeeping/network metadata, proving the public metadata and batch/timing projections remain stable under those local resamplings while raw-wire/ZK/wallet/timing/network assumptions remain explicit. `Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextArchiveBoundary.accepts_iff_no_first_rejection`, `first_rejection_from_none_contiguous`, `first_rejection_from_none_rows_below_leaf_count`, and `accepted_ciphertext_archive_publication_binds_contiguous_indices_below_leaf_count` model the wallet ciphertext archive publication gate: accepted archive rows are contiguous from index 0 and strictly below `leaf_count`, while gap and leaf-count rejection cases fail closed. `gen_ciphertext_archive_boundary_vectors` checks those cases against `hegemon_walletCiphertexts`, including an accepted prefix below `leaf_count`, archive gaps, and rows beyond `leaf_count`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a research privacy-game boundary with explicit cryptographic, proof-system, wallet metadata, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions plus production wallet randomness/bookkeeping/address-metadata regressions, PQ connection/relay metadata projection regression, and native mineable candidate-selection conformance. The secret-resampling and open-assumption package theorems strengthen deterministic unlinkability coverage, the seed-hardened PQ residual certificate removes KEM seed-source misuse from the residual by consuming the accepted KEM transcript/KDF certificate, and the materialized-sidecar PQ certificate carries those residuals through the accepted sidecar publication witness. This node still does not claim to mechanize ML-KEM, AEAD, RNG, global miner fairness, or simulator-based zero-knowledge security. The wallet ciphertext archive publication gate is now Lean/vector checked for contiguous indices and leaf-count bounds.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the ciphertext privacy-game boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Wallet note encryption must not deterministically identify repeated plaintexts to the same recipient; distinct RNG streams must produce different KEM ciphertexts and chain wire bytes while decrypting to the same plaintext.
Wallet note re-encryption must preserve the Lean-modeled public ciphertext summary shape while raw chain and DA wire bytes change under distinct RNG streams.
Wallet-local sync, cursor, tracked-note, and pending-submission bookkeeping must not alter public ciphertext summary, chain wire bytes, DA bytes, or ciphertext hash.
Wallet-local address cursor metadata must not alter public ciphertext summary, chain wire bytes, DA bytes, or ciphertext hash for an already-created output.

Wallet transfer, stablecoin, burn, and consolidation output-batch counts and change-output public diversifier policy must match the Lean public-shape table and stay within the two-output public bound.
Changing only sidecar pending-action arrival time must not alter semantic identity, materialized ciphertexts, replay keys, or consensus transaction observer fields.
Native block-template candidate selection must follow the Lean ordered mineable-transfer count and first-matching-candidate table, including zero-transfer rejection and unmineable sidecar exclusion.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the ciphertext privacy-game module plus the wallet re-encryption, output-batch, bookkeeping, and address-metadata regressions.
PQ connection info and relay config sentinel metadata must not alter consensus-carrying proto wire bytes or decoded payload projection and must not appear in those byte projections.

The PQ privacy residual certificate must consume the accepted KEM transcript/KDF certificate and expose OS-RNG seed source/length, supplied-seed ML-KEM consumption, transcript-as-HKDF-salt, and ordered KEM-secret IKM facts before primitive residual assumptions are used; the materialized-sidecar PQ certificate must also carry accepted sidecar publication and all-active observer decrypt/DA facts through that residual boundary.
Changing native wallet ciphertext archive publication to accept gaps or rows beyond the commitment leaf count must fail generated-vector conformance.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a Lean privacy-game boundary around valid observer-chain public shape, public metadata leakage excluding raw ciphertext bytes, explicit batch/timing leakage, same-shape ciphertext wires, independent left/right private-witness, prover-randomness, local-action metadata, local address metadata, local wallet bookkeeping metadata, and local network metadata resampling stability for public metadata and batch/timing projections, combined all-local-metadata open-assumption stability for action/address/network metadata in one theorem, same-wire discharge to full allowed-leakage equality, selected/all-active native tx-leaf public metadata row-package stability under independent secret/prover-randomness/local metadata resampling, all-active native tx-leaf public metadata and projected-DA boundaries under the explicit deployed-verifier and ML-KEM/AEAD/KDF/RNG assumptions, materialized sidecar DA publication composition into that all-active projected-DA boundary, selected-row decrypt-to-commitment reconstruction under the explicit wallet crypto-authentication predicate, and packaged assumption boundaries for raw-wire indistinguishability, proof-system zero knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching policy, network metadata policy, PQ transport parser/wire exactness, and PQ KEM OS-RNG seed-source/transcript-KDF implementation binding; it also production-checks that wallet note encryption consumes fresh KEM randomness for repeated same-plaintext same-recipient encryption, that wallet transfer/stablecoin/burn/consolidation output-batch counts match the Lean public-shape table, that wallet-local bookkeeping and address-cursor metadata do not affect public ciphertext projection, and that sidecar arrival-time metadata does not affect semantic/materialized observer fields. It explicitly leaves `wireIndistinguishable` as the cryptographic premise for raw ciphertext bytes and explicitly treats batch/timing as leakage, not hidden information. It does not prove ML-KEM IND-CCA security, AEAD confidentiality, KDF security, OS RNG quality, PQ transport parser/wire implementation equivalence, simulator-based zero knowledge, proof-system privacy, remaining wallet metadata beyond the modeled bookkeeping/address/output-count shape surfaces, remote topology/network timing privacy, timing privacy, ciphertext-hash security, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier-state transition, zero nullifiers cannot be staged or imported, successful staging prevents staging the same nullifier again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same nullifier.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_restaging` prove the anti-double-spend state behavior over the executable nullifier kernel. `gen_shielded_vectors` emits Lean-derived nullifier examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-shielded-pool` conformance test against them. Focused independent Rust oracles now also check protocol `NullifierState` transitions across can-stage/stage/import, zero, spent, pending, restage, and reimport cases, and consensus `NullifierSet` commitment behavior against a sorted unique preimage oracle with duplicate and duplicate-suffix batch-extend rejection staying atomic. The native node uses those shared helpers for mempool staging, block validation, state replay, and author preview.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-shielded-pool nullifier stage/import behavior, with focused independent protocol transition and consensus sorted-unique commitment oracles.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier stage/import behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing protocol NullifierState can-stage, stage, import, zero, pending, spent, restage, or reimport semantics away from the independent transition oracle must fail the focused production test.
Changing consensus NullifierSet commitment ordering, duplicate rejection, or atomic batch-extend rejection semantics away from the sorted unique oracle must fail the focused production test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks nullifier stage/import behavior and now adds focused implementation-equivalence evidence for protocol nullifier transitions plus consensus sorted-unique nullifier commitments. It does not prove full transaction balance conservation, note commitment correctness, nullifier hash security, or proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean fork-choice transition, a candidate tip is selected over the current tip exactly when it has greater work, or equal work with greater height, or equal work and height with a lexicographically lower hash; the selected tip is always either the current or candidate tip and never lowers cumulative work.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.higher_work_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.lower_work_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_higher_height_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_lower_height_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_height_uses_hash_order`, `Hegemon.Consensus.same_tip_not_better`, `Hegemon.Consensus.selectBest_selection_facts`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.selectBest_work_not_lower` prove deterministic two-tip ordering and selected-tip monotonicity over the executable fork-choice kernel. `gen_consensus_vectors` emits Lean-derived fork-choice examples with selected-source, selected-tip, and no-lower-work fields, and the formal-core gate runs both the production `consensus::fork_choice` helper conformance test and the native `native_meta_better_than` wrapper conformance test against them. `PowConsensus` and native block import both call the helper/wrapper path, and the blueprint binds native announced-block fork-choice to the side-branch persistence or canonical reorg publication split. The existing TLA+ model remains the bounded aggregate-work/finality abstraction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the deterministic fork-choice ordering helper, with the TLA+ finality model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the fork-choice ordering theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production fork-choice ordering away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing the native import fork-choice wrapper, selected source, selected tip, or no-lower-work behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `ForkChoiceInvariant` or `FinalityInvariant` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the PoW model.
Native announced-block import must bind fork-choice comparison before either canonical reorg publication or noncanonical side-branch persistence can run.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

falsification_cases.description: Native announced-block fork-choice publication order constraints must reference fork-choice Lean theorems listed by the claims ledger; removing those theorem refs or pointing at an unclaimed theorem must fail the blueprint gate.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves deterministic two-tip ordering, selected-tip source/facts, and no-lower-work selection only. It does not prove PoW admission arithmetic, SHA-256 header-hash implementation equivalence, every network partition, timestamp edge case, fork-choice convergence/liveness, or long-range adversarial mining schedule.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW admission transition, a block candidate is rejected when checked u64 next-height, pow_bits, timestamp, compact target, hash threshold, claimed cumulative Work48, or Work48 overflow violate the specified arithmetic policy; otherwise the expected cumulative work is accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_zero_mantissa`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_shifted_zero_target`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_large_exponent`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_rejects_max`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_accepts_predecessor`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_parent_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_median_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_future_skew`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_pow_bits`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_insufficient_work`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_accepts_valid` prove core facts about the executable PoW admission table. `gen_pow_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_admission` helper used by `PowConsensus` plus the `consensus-light-client` compact-target, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work helpers used by native import and bridge verification. Focused Rust regressions cover checked height overflow in both `evaluate_pow_admission` and `verify_pow_header`, plus generic consensus Work48 cumulative-work overflow. The blueprint now requires `PowConsensus::apply_block` to propagate `evaluate_pow_admission` before proof verification or node insertion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW checked next-height, target, timestamp, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work admission semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the PoW admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production consensus or light-client PoW arithmetic away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Existing PoW rule tests must continue rejecting target, timestamp, and admission-arithmetic violations.
Generic consensus admission and bridge/native header verification must reject a u64::MAX parent height instead of accepting through saturating next-height arithmetic.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p consensus pow_admission_rejects_height_overflow -- --nocapture && cargo test -p consensus-light-client pow_header_rejects_height_overflow -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: Generic consensus admission must reject cumulative work that exceeds the fixed 48-byte Work domain rather than storing an unbounded BigUint.
Generic PoW block application must propagate PoW admission before proof verification or node insertion.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks arithmetic admission over already-computed PoW hashes. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, retarget economics, network orphan policy, or long-range finality. Header preimage serialization is covered separately by consensus.header-preimage-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW retarget helper, the retarget constants match the release parameters, actual timespans are clamped to the 1/4x..4x window, a zero previous target stays zero, expected-timespan retargeting preserves the target, and nonzero retargeting never returns zero after the clamp.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetConstants_match_consensus`, `Hegemon.Consensus.adjustedTimespan_clamps_fast_blocks`, `Hegemon.Consensus.adjustedTimespan_keeps_expected_timespan`, `Hegemon.Consensus.adjustedTimespan_clamps_slow_blocks`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetTarget_zero_previous_target`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetTarget_keeps_expected_timespan`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetTarget_fast_timespan_is_clamped`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetTarget_slow_timespan_is_clamped`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetTarget_minimum_nonzero_target` pin the executable target-adjustment rule. `gen_pow_vectors` emits Lean-derived retarget examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `consensus::reward::adjusted_timespan` and `consensus::reward::retarget_target`, the helpers used by `PowConsensus::expected_pow_bits` at retarget boundaries.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the executable retarget target-adjustment helper. Compact re-encoding is covered by consensus.pow-retarget-compact-bits; mining economics remains out of scope.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the retarget constants, clamp, zero-target, expected-timespan, or nonzero-output theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production adjusted-timespan clamping or retarget target scaling away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate PoW vectors containing retarget cases and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable retarget target scaling and clamping after compact target decoding. Compact target re-encoding is covered separately by consensus.pow-retarget-compact-bits. It does not prove timestamp honesty, hash-rate economics, network liveness, or long-range adversarial mining schedules.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW compact retarget helper, target_to_compact returns the production compact encoding for zero, one, three-byte, and release easy targets; retargetBits rejects invalid previous pow_bits and otherwise composes compact decoding, clamped target retargeting, and compact re-encoding deterministically.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.targetToCompact_zero`, `Hegemon.Consensus.targetToCompact_easy_roundtrip`, `Hegemon.Consensus.targetToCompact_roundtrip_easy_target`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetBits_expected_timespan_keeps_bits`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetBits_rejects_invalid_previous_bits` pin executable compact re-encoding and retarget-bit composition facts. `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_shifted_zero_target` pins the hardened compact decode rejection for nonzero-mantissa encodings that shift to target zero. `gen_pow_vectors` emits compact roundtrip and retarget-bits examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `compact_to_target`, `target_to_compact`, `consensus::reward::adjusted_timespan`, and `consensus::reward::retarget_target`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for compact target encoding and retarget-bit composition. Retarget-boundary scheduling is covered by consensus.pow-retarget-schedule; mining economics remains out of scope.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening compact target encoding, retarget-bits, or shifted-zero-target rejection theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.

The release formal-core wrapper must generate PoW vectors containing compact roundtrip and retarget-bits cases and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable compact target encoding and retarget-bit composition. nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW retarget schedule helper, height zero uses genesis bits; non-boundary heights and early boundary candidates inherit parent pow_bits; true retarget boundaries require the RETARGET_WINDOW-1 anchor; missing history rejects; and supplied parent/anchor timestamps are converted through saturating timespan, retargetBits, and compact previous-bit validation.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetAnchorSteps_genesis_none`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetAnchorSteps_non_boundary_none`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetAnchorSteps_early_boundary_none`, `Hegemon.Consensus.retargetAnchorSteps_boundary_requires_window`, `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_genesis_uses_genesis`, `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_non_boundary_inherits_parent`, `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_missing_history_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_expected_timespan_keeps_bits`, `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_invalid_previous_bits_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.expectedPowBitsSchedule_reversed_timestamp_saturates` pin representative facts about the executable pow_bits schedule. `gen_pow_vectors` emits schedule examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `pow_retarget_anchor_steps` and `evaluate_pow_bits_schedule` helpers now used by `PowConsensus::expected_pow_bits`.

The release formal-core wrapper must generate PoW vectors containing pow_bits schedule cases and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable retarget-boundary scheduling and timestamp-span conversion after parent lookup. nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header grammar, the signing preimage is the ASCII domain `block` followed by the fixed consensus header fields in the specified little-endian and byte-array order, and is independent of signature, BFT bitmap, and PoW seal payloads; the full header preimage appends signature length/payload, optional bitmap tag/length/payload, and optional PoW seal tag/nonce/pow_bits.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.signingPreimage_independent_of_auth_payloads`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.sample_signing_preimage_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.sample_pow_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.bft_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.unsigned_full_header_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.pow_header_has_pow_tag`, `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.bft_header_has_bitmap_tag`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.Header.unsigned_header_has_absent_auth_tags` prove executable facts about the header preimage grammar. `gen_header_vectors` emits Lean-derived exact byte preimages, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `BlockHeader::signing_preimage_v1` and `BlockHeader::full_header_preimage_v1`, the helpers used by miner signature verification and PoW work hashing.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus header signing and full-header work-hash preimage grammar. SHA-256 security and implementation equivalence remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the header preimage theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production header preimage serialization away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks consensus header preimage byte grammar only. It does not prove SHA-256 collision resistance, SHA-256 implementation equivalence, ML-DSA unforgeability, semantic correctness of header field values, retarget economics, network orphan policy, or long-range finality.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW miner-identity transition, a header can be accepted only if it does not carry a BFT signature bitmap, its validator-set commitment resolves to a registered miner key, its signature payload has exactly the ML-DSA-65 signature byte length, signature byte parsing succeeds, and signature verification succeeds.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_signature_bitmap`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_unregistered`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_bad_signature_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_bad_signature_bytes`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powMinerIdentity_rejects_failed_signature_verification` prove core facts about the executable PoW miner-identity admission table. `gen_miner_identity_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_miner_identity` helper used by `PowConsensus::verify_pow_miner_signature` after real ML-DSA parsing and verification. The blueprint now requires that wrapper to propagate the evaluator result and requires `PowConsensus::apply_block` to propagate miner-signature verification before commitment checks, PoW admission, proof verification, or node insertion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW miner identity and signature admission ordering. ML-DSA cryptographic security and implementation correctness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the miner-identity theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production miner-identity admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Existing PoW rule tests must continue rejecting unregistered miners, malformed miner signatures, and forbidden BFT signature bitmaps.
Generic PoW block application must propagate miner identity verification before commitment checks, PoW admission, proof verification, or node insertion.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks miner identity and signature admission ordering only. Header signing-hash byte grammar is covered separately by consensus.header-preimage-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean version-policy transition, initial version bindings are allowed, activation events add bindings at and after their activation height, retirement events remove bindings at and after their retirement height, retirement wins over same-height activation, duplicate initial bindings are deduplicated, and block admission reports the first unsupported transaction version in transaction order.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_initial_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_unknown_initial`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_before_activation`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_accepts_at_activation`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_accepts_before_retirement`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_rejects_at_retirement`, `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_retirement_wins_same_height`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.versionPolicy_reports_first_unsupported_in_tx_order` prove representative executable facts about the version schedule admission table. `gen_version_policy_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `VersionSchedule::validate_versions` helper used by both `PowConsensus::apply_block` and `BftConsensus::apply_block`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus version admission. Protocol governance and per-version proof soundness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the version-policy theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production version-schedule admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The existing consensus integration test must continue rejecting unsupported versions before activation and accepting them at activation.
PoW and BFT block import must propagate version-policy admission before proof verification, voting-state mutation, or fork/node insertion.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks schedule admission semantics only. It does not prove social upgrade governance, protocol manifest correctness, proof-system soundness for each version, transaction-proof backend security, or full native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header-MMR opening-shape transition, valid openings must have a matching context, in-range leaf index, peak count equal to the leaf-count peak decomposition, sibling count equal to the selected peak height, and path orientation bits derived from the leaf's local index.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.peak_ranges_six`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.valid_shape_accepts`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.singleton_shape_accepts`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.four_leaf_left_orientation`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.four_leaf_right_orientation`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_context_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_leaf_out_of_range`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_empty_leaf_set`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_peak_count_mismatch`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmr.rejects_sibling_count_mismatch` prove representative facts about the MMR opening shape table. `gen_bridge_header_mmr_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `evaluate_header_mmr_opening_shape`, the production helper used by `verify_header_mmr_opening` and the long-range verifier's shared MMR context path.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header-MMR transcript transition, parent hash preimages must bind the exact node domain, u32 little-endian level, and ordered left/right child hashes; root hash preimages must bind the exact root domain, u64 little-endian leaf count, u32 little-endian peak count, and ordered peak hash sequence.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.parent_domain_bytes`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.root_domain_bytes`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.sample_parent_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.sample_parent_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.max_level_parent_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.empty_root_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.empty_root_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.two_peak_root_length`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.two_peak_root_hex`, `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.reversed_two_peak_root_hex`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.HeaderMmrTranscript.root_order_binds_peak_sequence` prove representative byte-layout facts about the parent/root transcript grammar. `gen_bridge_header_mmr_transcript_vectors` emits Lean-derived parent and root preimage cases, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `header_mmr_parent_preimage_v1` and `header_mmr_root_preimage_v1`, the production helpers hashed by header-MMR construction and verification.
