nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean bridge long-range proof-shape transition, valid proofs must bind the verifier hash, message count, header/MMR lengths, checked trusted-checkpoint sample-start arithmetic, trusted/message/tip height ordering, message opening leaf, selected message source, FlyClient sample heights/opening leaves, minimum confirmations, minimum tip work, and claimed output before long-range bridge outputs are accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.valid_shape_accepts`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_bad_verifier_hash`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_tip_mmr_len_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_trusted_height_overflow`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_tip_not_after_message`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_not_after_trusted`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_opening_leaf_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_index_oob`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_source_chain_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_message_source_height_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_sample_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_sample_height_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_sample_opening_leaf_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_under_confirmed`, `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_insufficient_tip_work`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.LongRange.rejects_output_mismatch` prove representative executable facts about the long-range bridge admission table. `gen_bridge_long_range_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks those vectors against `evaluate_long_range_proof_shape`, the production helper called by `verify_hegemon_long_range_proof_inner` before bridge outputs are accepted. The focused `long_range_proof_wire_decoders_match_independent_raw_oracle` regression separately checks the production long-range proof full and guest wire decoders against an independent raw-byte oracle over canonical wires, trailing/truncated input, noncanonical compact payload/message/sample lengths, over-cap message/payload/MMR/sample vectors, output-tail tamper where full decode succeeds but guest decode rejects, and deterministic mutations of valid wire bytes.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for long-range bridge proof structural admission plus an independent raw-wire decoder oracle corpus. Cryptographic MMR/hash binding and full FlyClient sampling soundness remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the long-range bridge proof-shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production long-range bridge proof structural admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing production long-range bridge proof full or guest wire decoder behavior away from the independent raw-byte oracle must fail the focused mutation corpus gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate long-range bridge vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks cheap long-range bridge proof structural admission over already-computed hashes, work values, sample indices, and message metadata, and adds independent raw-wire oracle evidence for the production full/guest proof decoders. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, PoW hash security, MMR hash binding, bridge message-root hashing, FlyClient sampling soundness, external-chain verifier behavior, or complete long-range light-client soundness. Header MMR opening shape and path orientation are covered separately by bridge.header-mmr-opening-shape. Header MMR parent/root transcript bytes are covered separately by bridge.header-mmr-transcript-binding. FlyClient transcript/index derivation is covered separately by bridge.flyclient-sampling-transcript. Checkpoint output canonical/journal bytes are covered separately by bridge.checkpoint-output-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean proof-policy transition, empty blocks must not carry proof payloads; non-empty product-path blocks must use self-contained aggregation with transaction artifacts, transaction validity claims, a proven batch, and a route-compatible aggregation payload; recursive blocks must not carry legacy commitment-proof bytes or receipt-root payloads and must carry a block artifact. For the executable TxValidityClaim matching transition, accepted provided claims must exactly match verified backend artifact receipts and statement bindings.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.empty_clean_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.empty_with_payload_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nonempty_requires_tx_artifacts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nonempty_rejects_tx_artifact_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nonempty_rejects_inline_required`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nonempty_requires_proven_batch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.recursive_rejects_commitment_proof_bytes`, `Hegemon.Consensus.recursive_requires_block_artifact`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.recursive_complete_accepts` prove core facts about the executable proof-policy decision table. `Hegemon.Transaction.TxValidityClaimMatching.accepted_tx_validity_claim_fields_match_verified_artifact` proves accepted claim matching exposes exact equality of receipt statement hash, proof digest, public-input digest, verifier profile, binding statement hash, anchor/root, fee, and circuit version, with companion rejection/precedence theorems. `gen_proof_policy_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples for empty blocks, missing transaction artifacts, count mismatch, legacy InlineRequired, missing proven batches, missing transaction validity claims, legacy inline batches, recursive commitment-proof bytes, recursive receipt-root payloads, missing recursive artifacts, complete recursive blocks, and receipt-root payload requirements. `gen_tx_validity_claim_matching_vectors` emits exact-match and field-mismatch examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `evaluate_block_proof_policy` helper and the production TxValidityClaim match helper called by `ParallelProofVerifier` before heavy proof verification can rely on provided claims.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the consensus proof payload shape and provided-claim/verified-artifact matching enforced before heavy artifact verification.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the proof-policy theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production proof-policy helper away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Existing self-contained proof mode tests must continue rejecting missing or malformed aggregation payloads.
Changing production TxValidityClaim matching away from the Lean executable exact-match table must fail generated-vector conformance.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks proof payload shape/admission and deterministic provided TxValidityClaim exact matching against verified backend artifacts only. It does not prove proven-batch binding, aggregation V5 header admission, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, tx leaf proof soundness, DA root derivation, receipt-root security, native backend security, or complete block-state equivalence. Proven-batch binding is covered separately by consensus.proven-batch-binding. Aggregation V5 header admission is covered separately by consensus.aggregation-v5-header-admission.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native tx-leaf admission transition, accepted inputs must have a present proof envelope, tx_leaf artifact kind, native verifier-profile agreement on the envelope and receipt, artifact bytes within the configured size cap, a matching expected artifact hash when one is supplied, and matching cached receipt plus public transaction view when a cache entry is reused; accepted uncached inputs require backend verification, while accepted cache hits may return the cached verified record.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.accepts_iff_admission_preconditions` proves that the executable acceptance decision is exactly the native tx-leaf admission predicate. The companion theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.valid_uncached_requires_backend_verification`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.valid_cache_hit_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.missing_envelope_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.wrong_artifact_kind_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.envelope_profile_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.oversized_artifact_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.receipt_profile_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.expected_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.missing_expected_hash_skips_hash_check`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.cache_receipt_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.cache_transaction_mismatch_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.NativeTxLeafAdmission.exact_size_limit_accepts` pin representative accept/reject facts. `gen_native_tx_leaf_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_native_tx_leaf_admission` helper now called by native tx-leaf verification before backend verification or cache return.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native tx-leaf envelope/profile/size/hash/cache receipt and transaction-view admission before backend proof verification. Scope excludes backend cryptographic soundness and BLAKE3 implementation equivalence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native tx-leaf admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native tx-leaf admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Reusing a cached native tx-leaf artifact for a different public transaction view must fail the focused cache replay regression.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native tx-leaf admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native tx-leaf envelope/profile/size/expected-hash/cache receipt and transaction-view admission only. It does not prove native tx-leaf artifact parsing, statement-hash preimage binding, native backend proof soundness, BLAKE3 artifact-hash security or implementation equivalence, cache implementation liveness, or complete block proof validity. Native tx-leaf artifact wire grammar and transaction statement-hash binding are covered separately.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean DA-root byte model, transaction ciphertext blobs are serialized as transaction-count, per-transaction ciphertext-count, per-ciphertext length-prefix, and ciphertext bytes in block order; DA params reject zero chunk size or sample count; shard counts are data shards plus ceil(data/2) parity shards bounded by 255 total shards; chunk proof paths are capped at 8 siblings and page proof paths at 32 siblings; leaf preimages are `da-leaf || u32le(index) || data`; node preimages are `da-node || left48 || right48`; and Merkle proof steps orient current/sibling bytes by node-index parity.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.empty_block_blob_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.sample_blob_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.empty_ciphertext_blob_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.leaf_zero_preimage_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.leaf_max_index_preimage_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.node_preimage_hex`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.node_preimage_order_binds_children`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_empty_blob`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_partial_third_blob`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_max_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_rejects_zero_chunk`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_rejects_zero_sample`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.shard_count_rejects_too_many`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.chunk_proof_path_len_max_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.chunk_proof_path_len_over_cap_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.page_proof_path_len_max_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.page_proof_path_len_over_cap_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.even_merkle_step_uses_current_left`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.DaRoot.odd_merkle_step_uses_sibling_left` pin the executable byte grammar, shard-count table, and bounded proof-path admission. `gen_da_root_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `consensus::build_da_blob`, `state_da::chunk_count_for_blob`, `state_da::encode_da_blob`, proof-path cap helpers, and the `state_da` DA preimage helpers used by leaf/node hashing and proof verification. A focused state-da oracle independently rebuilds da-leaf/da-node preimages, chunk/page Merkle levels, duplicate-last odd padding, production chunk and page proof paths, and proof replay for empty, single-page, multipage, and odd-leaf cases while rejecting index, sibling-orientation, page-root, and page-sibling mutations; a separate regression requires over-cap chunk/page proof paths to fail before hash replay.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the deterministic DA bytes and shard-count arithmetic used before DA-root hashing. Scope excludes Reed-Solomon correctness, BLAKE3 security, and sampling probability.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the DA-root byte/shard theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production DA blob, shard-count, or Merkle preimage helpers away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate DA-root vectors and run the production conformance test.
Changing state-da leaf/node preimages, chunk/page Merkle orientation, duplicate-last padding, proof paths, or proof replay away from the independent oracle must fail the focused production test.
Removing fail-fast bounded admission for over-cap chunk or page Merkle proof paths must fail the focused production test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks deterministic DA blob bytes, shard-count admission, bounded proof-path admission, leaf/node preimage bytes, and Merkle proof-step orientation, and adds focused state-da implementation-equivalence evidence for chunk/page proof paths and replay. It does not prove Reed-Solomon erasure-code correctness, BLAKE3 security or implementation equivalence, DA sampling probability, chunk network availability, ciphertext sidecar availability, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean proven-batch binding transition, accepted proven batches must have a route-compatible proof kind, tx_count equal to the block transaction count, a statement commitment matching the expected block commitment, a DA root matching the block/body-derived DA root, a nonzero DA chunk count equal to the recomputed block/body DA encoding chunk count, recursive-block mode must carry a block-artifact envelope, any present block-artifact envelope must have matching kind and verifier profile, and recursive-block mode must not carry a receipt-root payload.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.accepts_iff_binding_preconditions` proves that the executable acceptance decision is exactly the proven-batch binding predicate. The companion theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.valid_recursive_block_v2_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.valid_receipt_root_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_incompatible_recursive_route`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_incompatible_receipt_route`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_tx_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_statement_commitment_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_da_root_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_da_chunk_count_zero`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_da_chunk_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_artifact_kind_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_artifact_profile_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_recursive_block_receipt_root_payload`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.receipt_root_skips_block_artifact_checks`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.recursive_v1_route_is_compatible`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.tx_leaf_is_not_block_artifact_route` pin representative accept/reject facts about the table. `gen_proven_batch_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_proven_batch_binding` helper called by `validate_proven_batch_binding` before heavy proof verification; production recomputes the DA encoding once, derives both root and expected chunk count from it, and rejects producer-supplied chunk-count drift before recursive or receipt-root proof work. `Hegemon.Consensus.ProvenBatchBinding.rejects_missing_recursive_block_artifact` and the generated negative case now force recursive-block mode to fail at the binding surface when `block_artifact` is absent instead of relying on a later proof-policy layer.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for proven-batch binding before recursive block or receipt-root proof verification. Scope excludes proof-system and DA hash soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the proven-batch binding theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production proven-batch binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate proven-batch binding vectors and run the production conformance test.
RecursiveBlock proven-batch binding must reject a missing block_artifact before heavy proof verification.
Proven-batch binding must reject a producer-supplied DA chunk count that differs from the recomputed DA encoding chunk count even when the DA root matches.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks cheap proven-batch binding only. It does not prove DA erasure-code soundness, BLAKE3 security, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, transaction leaf proof soundness, aggregation V5 header admission, native backend security, or complete block proof validity. DA byte grammar and shard-count binding are covered separately by consensus.da-root-byte-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean recursive-block admission transition, accepted artifact inputs must have an envelope kind equal to the expected recursive_block_v1 or recursive_block_v2 kind, a matching verifier profile, artifact bytes within the fixed versioned cap before decode, successful artifact decoding, a recursive header artifact-version matching that kind, public tx_count equal to the block tx_count, public tx_statements_commitment equal to the expected block commitment, and public replay agreement with the expected block semantic tuple when replay is required. The generic recursive_block_v1/v2 registry ArtifactVerifier path must reject direct verification with requires_semantic_replay because it lacks verified tx records and semantic replay inputs. For the shipped recursive_block_v2 verifier surface, accepted pre-proof inputs must have the expected public tuple, fixed padded proof width, exact versioned artifact byte cap agreement, supported tx count, exact derived header, canonical proof-prefix decoding, zero proof padding, successful proof relation projection, and canonical proof length within the projected proof-width cap before cryptographic child proof verification is invoked.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.artifact_accepts_iff_preconditions` proves that the executable recursive-block admission decision is exactly the precondition predicate, `artifact_accepts_implies_bytes_within_cap` proves acceptance implies the versioned artifact byte cap, and `recursive_block_artifact_accepts_implies_bounded_request_facts` projects accepted recursive artifact bytes into the reusable bounded-request resource table consumed by the pre-heavy DoS certificate. Companion theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.valid_v2_artifact_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.valid_v1_artifact_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.wrong_kind_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.profile_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.artifact_too_large_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.artifact_too_large_precedes_decode_failure`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.decode_failed_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.header_version_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.tx_count_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.statement_commitment_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.public_replay_mismatch_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.kind_precedes_decode_failure` pin representative accept/reject and precedence facts. The Lean v1 fixture now uses the corrected fixed serializer width of 699,452 bytes, and consensus/protocol caps are derived or set to that width. `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.direct_v1_requires_semantic_replay` and `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.direct_v2_requires_semantic_replay` pin the fail-closed generic registry rule. `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockV2VerifierSurface.surface_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `full_verifier_accepts_iff_surface_and_crypto`, and `full_accepts_implies_surface_accepts` prove that the executable recursive_block_v2 pre-proof surface is exact and that full verifier acceptance can only happen after the surface accepts. `gen_recursive_block_admission_vectors` and `gen_recursive_block_v2_verifier_surface_vectors` emit Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_recursive_block_artifact_admission` helper, the direct `RecursiveBlockVerifier` rejection in `consensus/src/proof.rs`, and the production `verify_block_recursive_v2_surface` helper in `circuits/block-recursion/src/tree_v2.rs` before heavy recursive child proof verification. The gate also runs `recursive_block_v1_fixed_wire_cap_and_parser_match_oracle`, which compares production V1 cap/decode/header/count/commitment admission labels with an independent raw-byte oracle over valid fixed-width bytes, wrong kind/profile precedence, oversized-before-trailing precedence, empty/minimum/truncated decode failures, invalid profile/relation/proof-length header failures, header-version mismatch, tx-count mismatch, and statement-commitment mismatch. `recursive_block_v2_raw_fixed_width_oracle_matches_parser_boundaries` compares the production V2 fixed-width parser, serializer preservation, truncation/trailing rejection, header/proof/public boundary mutations, unsupported tx_count rejection, public/header mismatch, and pre-proof verifier-surface rejection order against an independent header/proof/public byte oracle. `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockV2VerifierSurface.versioned_cap_mismatch_rejects` and the generated `versioned-cap-mismatch` vector force the Rust v2 verifier surface to reject if the derived header/proof/public tuple size drifts from the versioned artifact cap before proof decode/projection or child verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for recursive-block artifact envelope/decode/header/count/statement/public-replay admission before heavy recursive verification, direct registry-verifier semantic-replay rejection, corrected V1 fixed-width cap binding, independent V1 and V2 raw-byte parser/admission oracles, and the recursive_block_v2 pre-proof verifier surface. Scope excludes recursive proof cryptographic soundness and native tx-leaf soundness. The v2 verifier-surface vector set includes versioned-cap mismatch rejection before child proof verification.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the recursive-block admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production recursive-block admission or direct registry-verifier rejection away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Oversized recursive_block_v1/v2 artifacts must reject on the cheap artifact-size gate before deserialization or recursive proof verification.
Changing the recursive_block_v1 fixed wire cap, parser decode behavior, or cheap admission ordering away from the independent raw-byte oracle must fail the focused corpus gate.
Changing production recursive_block_v2 pre-proof verifier-surface rejection or acceptance away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing the recursive_block_v2 fixed-width parser, serializer preservation, boundary mutation handling, or pre-proof surface rejection order away from the independent raw-byte oracle must fail the focused corpus gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate recursive-block admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks recursive-block envelope kind, verifier profile, artifact byte caps before decode, artifact decoding, recursive header version, tx-count, statement-commitment, public-replay admission, direct registry-verifier semantic-replay rejection, and recursive_block_v2 pre-proof verifier-surface admission, and adds independent mutation-oracle evidence for both recursive_block_v1 and recursive_block_v2 fixed-width parser/admission gates. It does not prove native tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, public replay semantic correctness beyond the separate recursive-public-replay field-propagation claim, BLAKE3 security or implementation equivalence, aggregation STARK soundness, child proof verification soundness, complete Rust parser implementation correctness, or complete block proof validity. Proven-batch binding, native tx-leaf admission, native tx-leaf artifact wire grammar, and aggregation V5 header admission are covered separately.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean recursive public replay transition, accepted v1/v2 replay inputs must have contiguous tx indices, and the resulting public tuple must carry the tx count, tx statements commitment, state roots, kernel roots, nullifier root, DA root, tree commitments, and version-specific message-root behavior exactly as specified; non-contiguous tx indices are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.accepts_iff_contiguous` proves that replay acceptance is exactly contiguous tx-index admission. Companion theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.zero_based_contiguous_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.nonzero_start_contiguous_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.gap_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.duplicate_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.decreasing_rejects`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.valid_v1_public_fields_match_semantic`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.valid_v2_public_fields_match_semantic`, `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.v1_public_byte_length`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursivePublicReplay.v2_public_byte_length` pin representative accept/reject, field propagation, and serialized public-length facts. `gen_recursive_public_replay_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `public_replay_v1` and `public_replay_v2` helpers in `block-recursion`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for recursive public replay tx-index ordering, semantic-field propagation, version-specific message-root behavior, and public tuple lengths. Scope excludes hash security/equivalence and recursive proof cryptographic soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the recursive public replay theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production recursive public replay field propagation or tx-index admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate recursive public replay vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks recursive public replay tx-index ordering, semantic-field propagation, version-specific message-root behavior, and public tuple lengths only. It does not prove BLAKE3/fold_digest48 security or implementation equivalence, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive semantic-input source binding except through the separate consensus.recursive-semantic-inputs source-mapping claim, aggregation STARK soundness, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean recursive semantic-input derivation, accepted inputs must satisfy nonempty block, nullifier-count, nonzero-nullifier, duplicate-nullifier, and DA-encoding preconditions; the resulting semantic tuple must source each field from the expected statement commitment, parent and applied commitment-tree roots, kernel roots of those roots, nonzero-nullifier root, block DA root under header DA params, header message root, and parent/applied recursive tree-state commitments exactly as specified.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveSemanticInputs.semantic_accepts_iff_preconditions` proves that semantic-input acceptance is exactly the executable admission predicate. Companion theorems `valid_semantic_derivation_accepts`, `empty_block_rejects`, `excessive_nullifiers_rejects`, `zero_nullifier_rejects`, `missing_nonzero_nullifier_rejects`, `duplicate_nullifier_rejects`, `da_encoding_rejects`, `valid_semantic_fields_match_sources`, and `rejection_precedes_da_encoding` pin representative acceptance, rejection, field-source, and precedence facts. `gen_recursive_semantic_input_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `recursive_block_semantic_inputs_from_block` in consensus.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for recursive semantic-input admission, field-source mapping, and rejection precedence. Scope excludes cryptographic hash/security equivalence and recursive proof soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the recursive semantic-input theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production recursive semantic-input field sourcing or rejection ordering away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate recursive semantic-input vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks recursive semantic-input admission, field-source mapping, and rejection precedence only. It does not prove BLAKE3, DA erasure-code, Merkle tree hash, kernel-root hash, nullifier-root hash, or recursive-state-commitment cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, native tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, consensus block-header validity, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean statement-anchor admission predicate, accepted nonempty block verification must have one statement binding per transaction and every binding anchor must be known in the parent commitment-tree root history before any same-block commitments are applied. Binding-count mismatch rejects before unknown-anchor checks.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.StatementAnchorAdmission.accepts_iff_statement_anchor_preconditions` proves that statement-anchor admission accepts exactly when the binding count equals the transaction count and all anchors are known. Companion theorems `valid_accepts`, `empty_statement_anchor_admission_accepts`, `binding_count_mismatch_rejects`, `unknown_anchor_rejects`, `binding_count_mismatch_precedes_unknown_anchor`, `same_block_anchor_rejects`, `accepted_implies_all_anchors_known`, and `accepted_implies_binding_count_matches` pin representative acceptance, rejection, precedence, and accepted-fact behavior. `gen_statement_anchor_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `validate_statement_anchor_history` in consensus.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for parent-history statement-anchor admission. Scope excludes Merkle/hash cryptographic soundness and complete Rust commitment-tree refinement.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the statement-anchor admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production statement-anchor count, parent-history membership, rejection precedence, or same-block-created-root rejection behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

The release formal-core wrapper must require the production verifier to call the statement-anchor admission helper, propagate its result, and dominate recursive semantic derivation, artifact verification, and final tree transition.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks verifier-side statement-anchor admission only: statement-binding count agreement, parent root-history anchor membership, same-block-created-anchor rejection, and rejection precedence. It does not prove note commitment correctness, Merkle or BLAKE3/Poseidon2 security or implementation equivalence, root-history persistence, transaction proof cryptographic soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, nullifier uniqueness, DA soundness, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean block tree-transition predicate, accepted transitions must have proof starting root equal to the parent commitment-tree root, commitment application must succeed, proof ending root must equal the applied commitment-tree root, and accepted execution must return that applied root. Starting-root mismatch must reject before commitment application, and commitment application failure must reject before ending-root comparison.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.TreeTransition.tree_transition_accepts_iff_preconditions` proves that transition acceptance is exactly starting-root agreement plus successful commitment application plus ending-root agreement. Companion theorems `valid_transition_accepts`, `accepted_transition_returns_applied_root`, `starting_root_mismatch_rejects`, `apply_failure_rejects`, `ending_root_mismatch_rejects`, `starting_root_mismatch_precedes_apply_failure`, `apply_failure_precedes_ending_root_mismatch`, and `empty_valid_transition_accepts` pin representative acceptance, return-root, rejection, and precedence facts. `gen_tree_transition_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `verify_and_apply_tree_transition_without_anchors` in consensus.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus commitment-tree transition control flow. Scope excludes Merkle/hash cryptographic soundness and complete block proof validity.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the block tree-transition theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production tree-transition start-root, commitment-application, end-root, or rejection-precedence behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate block tree-transition vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks consensus commitment-tree transition control flow only: starting-root agreement, commitment application success/failure, ending-root agreement, accepted applied-root return, and rejection precedence. It does not prove note commitment correctness, BLAKE3 or Poseidon2 security or implementation equivalence, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, DA soundness, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean receipt-root admission transitions, accepted payload inputs must have receipt-root leaf_count equal to the block tx_count, receipt payload length equal to tx_count, present tx-validity claim receipts, payload receipts matching the claims, and present transaction artifacts; accepted artifact inputs must have receipt_root envelope kind, native verifier-profile agreement, artifact bytes within the tx-count-dependent size cap, present transaction artifacts, and tx-artifact count equal to the block tx_count; accepted statement and verified-metadata bindings must have matching derived statement commitment and verified leaf count.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.ReceiptRootAdmission.payload_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ReceiptRootAdmission.artifact_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Consensus.ReceiptRootAdmission.statement_accepts_iff_preconditions`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.ReceiptRootAdmission.verified_metadata_accepts_iff_preconditions` prove that each executable receipt-root admission decision is exactly its precondition predicate. Companion theorems pin representative accept/reject facts for payload leaf-count mismatch, payload receipt-count mismatch, missing tx-validity claims, payload receipt mismatch, missing transaction artifacts, wrong artifact kind, verifier-profile mismatch, artifact size overflow, tx-artifact count mismatch, statement-commitment mismatch, verified leaf-count mismatch, and exact-size-limit acceptance. `gen_receipt_root_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production helpers now called by `ParallelProofVerifier` and `ReceiptRootVerifier` around native receipt-root verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for receipt-root payload/envelope/size/count/statement/metadata admission around backend verification. Scope excludes native backend cryptographic soundness and BLAKE3 implementation equivalence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the receipt-root admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production receipt-root admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate receipt-root admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks receipt-root payload, envelope, size, tx-artifact count, statement-commitment, and verified-metadata admission only. It does not prove native tx-leaf proof soundness, native receipt-root byte parsing, native receipt-root cryptographic fold soundness, backend transcript challenge derivation, BLAKE3 security or implementation equivalence, or complete block proof validity. Native tx-leaf admission, native receipt-root wire grammar, and proven-batch binding are covered separately.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean aggregation V5 header transition, accepted payload headers must use version/format 5, public-values encoding 2, serialized tree_arity equal to the configured merge fan-in, a 48-byte statement commitment matching the expected block commitment, nonzero child counts bounded by fan-in, subtree tx-count equal to the expected block tx-count, exact tree-level/root-level bounds, a binary-minimum merge fan-in, route-compatible leaf/merge fan-in, and packed public-value length equal to inner_public_inputs_len.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.valid_leaf_singleton_header_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.valid_merge_header_accepts`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_bad_version`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_bad_proof_format`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_bad_public_values_encoding`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_tree_arity_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_statement_commitment_length`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_statement_commitment_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_child_count_above_fan_in`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_subtree_tx_count_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_tree_levels_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_root_level_out_of_range`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_leaf_fan_in_above_configured`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_merge_fan_in_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.rejects_inner_public_inputs_len_mismatch`, `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.merge_fan_in_floor_is_binary_minimum`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.AggregationV5.tree_levels_use_binary_merge_floor` prove representative facts about the executable header admission table. `gen_aggregation_v5_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `validate_header` path in `consensus::aggregation::v5`. The production helper rejects serialized tree_arity mismatches before representative child proof decoding or STARK batch verification, and the merge fan-in helper floors at 2 so tree-level computation cannot be configured into a non-progressing merge loop. The formal-core gate now also runs an aggregation V5 envelope mutation corpus: raw pass-through payloads, HGA0 compressed payloads, compression version mismatch, invalid declared uncompressed lengths, missing/garbage zstd payloads, zstd length mismatch, postcard truncation/trailing bytes, malformed random bytes, and mutated valid raw/compressed payloads are compared against an independent marker/version/length/zstd/postcard/header oracle before heavy proof work.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for aggregation V5 payload header admission and a deterministic envelope/postcard parser-drift corpus before heavy proof verification. Scope excludes proof-system soundness and does not prove zstd/postcard internals.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the aggregation V5 header theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production aggregation V5 header admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production aggregation V5 HGA0/zstd envelope decode, exact-postcard payload consumption, or cheap header admission away from the independent parser/header oracle over malformed and mutated byte streams must fail the corpus gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate aggregation V5 vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks cheap aggregation V5 header admission only, including serialized tree_arity-to-configured-merge-fan-in agreement before heavy proof work. It also checks parser drift for the HGA0/zstd envelope plus exact-postcard V5 payload boundary against an independent oracle. It does not prove postcard or zstd implementation correctness, representative child proof decoding, transaction proof soundness, aggregation STARK soundness, packed public-value cryptographic binding, or complete block proof validity.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean supply-accounting transition, supply digest increases and decreases are checked against the 128-bit bound, underflow and overflow return rejection, no-coinbase native blocks leave supply unchanged, and native coinbase blocks advance supply by the checked subsidy-plus-fee amount.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.increaseSupplyDigest_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.increaseSupplyDigest_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.decreaseSupplyDigest_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.decreaseSupplyDigest_rejects_underflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nativeSupplyDelta_without_coinbase`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nativeSupplyDelta_with_coinbase`, `Hegemon.Consensus.advanceNativeSupplyDigest_checked`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.advanceNativeSupplyDigest_rejects_supply_overflow` prove the executable accounting kernel. `gen_supply_vectors` emits Lean-derived consensus and native examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `CoinbaseData::net_native_delta`, `consensus::reward::update_supply_digest`, and native `advance_native_supply_digest`. The native node now rejects supply overflow instead of using saturating addition on import, replay, and mined-block metadata; mining-template construction must fall back to an empty parent-root template when checked supply advancement rejects pending actions. Native block replay must also match `expected_native_supply_from_parts` as `Some(expected_supply)` or return `SupplyDeltaInvalid` before block commitment admission.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus and native supply accounting behavior. Scope is executable accounting only.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the supply-accounting theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing consensus coinbase native delta or supply digest update behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing native no-coinbase, coinbase fee, or checked supply overflow behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Disconnecting checked native supply advancement from mining-template fallback, or constructing a header from invalid pending-action roots after supply rejection, must fail the blueprint gate.
Native block replay must fail closed with SupplyDeltaInvalid when checked expected-supply reconstruction overflows, before block commitment admission can accept the replay.
Native work preparation must drop pending actions and bind the parent supply digest when pending-action supply advancement would overflow.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable supply arithmetic and native coinbase supply-digest behavior. It does not prove PoW admission arithmetic, full block proof-system soundness, monetary policy economics beyond the current subsidy helper, or complete wallet reward construction correctness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean block-subsidy schedule, the monetary constants equal the tokenomics inputs, height zero mints no subsidy, positive heights use a capped halving epoch, the first epoch pays the initial subsidy, the first and second halving boundaries divide that subsidy by powers of two, and the capped shift extinguishes the subsidy permanently.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.monetaryConstants_match_tokenomics`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_zero_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_initial_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_first_epoch_last_block`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_first_halving_boundary`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_second_halving_boundary`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_extinct_at_capped_shift`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_capped_shift_stable_after_extinction`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_uses_capped_halving_epoch` pin the executable schedule. `gen_supply_vectors` emits Lean-derived constants and boundary cases, and the formal-core gate checks them against `consensus::reward::block_subsidy`, the compiled consensus constants, and the native supply-vector parser.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the executable block-subsidy schedule. Scope is the schedule helper, not the economics of the schedule.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the subsidy-schedule theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production monetary constants, halving epoch calculation, or block subsidy boundary behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate supply vectors containing monetary constants and subsidy boundary cases, then run the consensus and native conformance tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the executable block_subsidy schedule and monetary constants. It does not prove monetary economics, timestamp honesty, retarget economics, wallet reward construction, encrypted coinbase note construction, proof-system soundness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean claimed supply-chain replay transition, if an accepted chain prefix returns a final supply digest, then replaying the same ordered supply deltas from genesis returns exactly that final digest; mismatched claimed supply, burn underflow, and supply overflow reject. Accepted native transaction-chain deltas, canonical no-theft/public-field boundary facts, and accepted native coinbase accounting can feed valid claimed-supply certificates when explicit fee/native-delta and subsidy-plus-fee bindings identify the production block construction fields with the authorized deltas; the cross-layer coinbase/canonical certificates carry those facts together for one-coinbase and no-coinbase block surfaces.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.SupplyInvariant.validated_chain_matches_expected_deltas` proves that accepted claimed supply-chain replay matches the expected delta replay. Companion theorems `valid_step_claims_expected_supply`, `valid_step_claims_expected_supply_with_bound_fees`, `claimed_supply_mismatch_rejects`, and `supply_delta_failure_rejects_step` prove one-step claimed supply binding and rejection behavior. `accepted_transaction_chain_native_delta_feeds_valid_claimed_supply_step` packages accepted native transaction-chain delta authorization into the claimed-supply step under an explicit fee/native-delta binding, and `canonical_no_theft_boundary_native_delta_feeds_valid_claimed_supply_step` does the same for canonical no-theft/public-field boundary facts. `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseSupplyConservation.accepted_one_coinbase_accounting_binds_observed_amount`, `accepted_one_coinbase_accounting_yields_supply_certificate`, and `accepted_no_coinbase_accounting_yields_supply_certificate` package accepted native coinbase accounting with claimed-supply replay, proving one-coinbase observed amount equals checked subsidy plus fees and no-coinbase surfaces carry zero modeled mint. `gen_supply_invariant_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, counterfeit-claim, underflow, and overflow chain cases, and the formal-core gate checks them against `consensus::reward::validate_supply_chain`. PoW validation now calls the shared expected supply transition helper through a local `pow.rs` wrapper; the blueprint requires the `let Some(expected_supply) = ... else { return InvalidCoinbase }` shape, the immediate comparison to `block.header.supply_digest`, and dominance before PoW admission, proof verification, or node insertion. `Hegemon.Consensus.SupplyInvariant.accepted_transaction_chain_yields_supply_conservation_certificate` and `canonical_no_theft_boundary_yields_production_supply_conservation_certificate` package accepted transaction-chain native deltas, claimed-supply replay, explicit fee/native-delta binding, and canonical no-theft/public-field facts into production supply conservation certificates. The new `accepted_one_coinbase_accounting_and_canonical_boundary_yields_supply_integrity_certificate` and `accepted_no_coinbase_accounting_and_canonical_boundary_yields_supply_integrity_certificate` theorems compose those coinbase facts with the canonical no-theft/public-balance certificate so reward accounting, native public-delta binding, per-asset conservation, and claimed-supply replay are carried together.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for accepted-chain supply replay. Scope is supply-digest replay, not proof-system soundness or privacy.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the supply-chain invariant theorem or rejection facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production supply-chain replay away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate supply-chain invariant vectors and run the production conformance test.

Generic PoW block application must fail closed on expected-supply reconstruction failure and compare the checked expected supply to the header supply digest before PoW admission, proof verification, or node insertion.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks accepted-chain supply replay and claimed supply-digest matching. It does not prove transaction proof soundness, note privacy, cryptographic hash equivalence, monetary policy economics beyond the current subsidy/delta helpers, network finality, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native coinbase-accounting admission transition, a block is accepted only when it has at most one coinbase action, no-coinbase blocks are accepted without summing fees, and a present coinbase amount equals the checked block subsidy plus checked shielded-transfer fee total. Accepted one-coinbase accounting can be packaged with claimed-supply replay so the observed coinbase amount is the checked subsidy-plus-fee amount at the supply-conservation boundary.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.no_coinbase_accepts`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.multiple_coinbase_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.fee_total_overflow_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.reward_overflow_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.coinbase_amount_missing_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.amount_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.matching_amount_accepts`, and `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.multiple_precedes_fee_total_overflow` prove the executable admission table. `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseSupplyConservation.accepted_one_coinbase_accounting_binds_observed_amount`, `accepted_one_coinbase_accounting_yields_supply_certificate`, and `accepted_no_coinbase_accounting_yields_supply_certificate` compose accepted accounting with claimed-supply replay. `gen_coinbase_accounting_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_coinbase_accounting_admission`, the helper used by native block import and mining validation. The same two cross-layer supply-integrity theorems compose accounting admission with canonical no-theft/public-balance conservation so accepted block reward accounting can be consumed at the production supply boundary without reopening lower-level balance proofs.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native coinbase accounting admission. Scope is block reward admission policy, not encrypted note construction or monetary economics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the coinbase-accounting admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production duplicate-coinbase, no-coinbase fee-burn, fee-total overflow, reward overflow, missing amount, reward mismatch, or valid reward behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused native tests must reject multiple coinbase actions, reward mismatch, and overflowing fee totals when a block claims coinbase fees.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate coinbase-accounting admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native coinbase accounting admission only. It does not prove encrypted coinbase note construction, coinbase ciphertext hash binding, transfer proof soundness, monetary policy economics beyond the current subsidy helper, full action payload validation, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native coinbase action payload-admission transition, decoded coinbase payloads are accepted only when the amount is nonzero, the decoded note commitment matches the action commitment, that commitment is nonzero, the encrypted note bytes are within the native ciphertext cap, and the public ciphertext hash and size metadata match the decoded encrypted note. The companion CoinbaseActionPayloadScaleWire transition accepts raw MintCoinbaseArgs SCALE route payload bytes only when fixed fields and canonical compact prefixes match, every byte is consumed, and canonical re-encoding equals the supplied wire.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseActionPayloadAdmission.accepts_iff_payload_preconditions` proves the complete executable payload predicate, with companion theorems for amount-zero, commitment mismatch, zero commitment, ciphertext-too-large, ciphertext-hash mismatch, ciphertext-size mismatch, and rejection precedence. `gen_coinbase_action_payload_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_coinbase_action_payload_admission`, the helper used by native mempool and block action validation. `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseActionPayloadScaleWire.coinbase_action_payload_scale_wire_acceptance_exposes_facts`, `accepted_coinbase_action_payload_scale_wire_total_length`, and `accepted_coinbase_action_payload_scale_wire_exact_decode` prove the raw MintCoinbaseArgs SCALE wire surface; `gen_coinbase_action_payload_scale_wire_vectors` emits valid short-KEM and zero-KEM fixtures plus empty, short, trailing, KEM length overrun, and noncanonical compact-prefix rejection cases checked against production `MintCoinbaseArgs::encode` and `decode_scale_exact::<MintCoinbaseArgs>`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for decoded native coinbase reward-note payload admission. Scope is payload/public metadata binding after route/scope admission.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the coinbase action payload-admission theorem or companion rejection proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production zero amount, commitment mismatch, zero commitment, ciphertext oversize, ciphertext hash mismatch, ciphertext size mismatch, or valid payload behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production MintCoinbaseArgs SCALE field order, exact-decode acceptance, canonical re-encoding, or modeled malformed-byte rejection away from the Lean wire vectors must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Focused native tests must reject zero or semantically mismatched coinbase commitments, public-seed commitment tampering, ciphertext hash mismatches, ciphertext size mismatches, and oversized coinbase ciphertexts.
Mutating a coinbase note public seed without recomputing the public note commitment must fail native block-action validation before state mutation.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate coinbase action payload-admission and SCALE wire vectors and run the production conformance tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks decoded native coinbase payload/public metadata admission plus representative raw MintCoinbaseArgs SCALE route-payload conformance. It does not prove encrypted note confidentiality, ciphertext hash cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, reward accounting, complete SCALE decoder correctness for all arbitrary bytes, transfer proof soundness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native resource-budget admission predicates, pending-action mempool admission accepts only when saturated pending bytes plus candidate bytes are within the configured cap, and staged proof replacement accepts only when saturated staged bytes minus the replaced proof bytes plus the new proof bytes are within the configured cap.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.mempool_accepts_iff_not_over_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.staged_proof_accepts_iff_not_over_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.mempool_over_limit_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.staged_proof_over_limit_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.mempool_saturated_overflow_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.staged_proof_replacement_subtracts_existing`, and `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission.staged_proof_saturated_overflow_rejects` prove the executable saturated byte-budget behavior. `gen_resource_budget_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_mempool_byte_budget_admission` and `evaluate_native_staged_proof_byte_budget_admission`, the helpers used by action staging and `da_submitProofs`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native mempool and staged-proof byte-budget admission. Scope is resource-budget arithmetic, not RPC exposure safety or sidecar availability.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the resource-budget admission theorems or saturated arithmetic examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production exact-limit, over-limit, replacement, existing-overcount, or saturated total behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused native tests must reject mempool and staged-proof byte-budget overflow and accept replacement of an existing staged proof inside the cap.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate resource-budget admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native mempool and staged-proof byte-budget admission only. It does not prove RPC authentication, public exposure safety for unsafe methods, sled durability, proof byte parsing, native tx-leaf proof soundness, DA sidecar availability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native RPC admission predicates, RPC policy resolution accepts only safe/unsafe/auto/empty tokens with auto and empty resolving to safe for external listeners and unsafe for local listeners; safe policy rejects unsafe methods and hides them from rpc_methods; timestamp queries accept only non-inverted, non-overflowing ranges within the row cap; base64/hex byte strings accept only within raw and decoded caps; and JSON-RPC batches accept only when nonempty and within the batch cap. Blueprint implementation bindings additionally require timestamp, block-row, and wallet archive materialization helpers to propagate storage/key/value failures before returning JSON rows, and require mining thread parameters to pass through a bounded parser before starting mining.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_accepts_valid_tokens`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_rejects_invalid`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_auto_external_safe`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_auto_local_unsafe`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.safe_policy_rejects_unsafe_methods`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.unsafe_policy_allows_every_method`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.safe_method_list_hides_unsafe_methods`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.timestamp_range_accepts_iff_requested_within_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.hex_byte_parse_accepts_iff_caps_hold`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.base64_byte_parse_accepts_iff_caps_hold`, and `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.batch_accepts_iff_nonempty_within_limit` prove the executable RPC admission behavior. `gen_rpc_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `rpc_method_policy`, `dispatch_rpc_method`, `native_rpc_methods`, `block_timestamps`, `parse_bytes_value`, and `rpc_handler` behavior. Separate blueprint implementation bindings require the production block metadata, timestamp, commitment archive, and ciphertext archive materializers to be called by the exported RPC paths with propagated results, require mining thread parsing before `start_mining`, and focused regressions corrupt sled rows or malformed explicit parameters to prove those paths fail closed.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native JSON-RPC admission. Scope is exposed RPC policy/range/cap gating, not RPC authentication or full handler semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the RPC admission theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production RPC policy resolution, unsafe-method gating, method-list filtering, timestamp range bounds, byte string cap behavior, or batch-size admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused native tests must keep unsafe RPC methods disabled under safe policy, with external auto resolving safe and local auto resolving unsafe.
Focused native tests must reject unbounded timestamp ranges, oversized raw or decoded byte strings, and oversized JSON-RPC batches.
Focused native tests must reject malformed commitment archive keys/values/gaps and malformed ciphertext archive keys/values instead of returning shortened or junk wallet pages.

Focused native tests must reject malformed explicit chain RPC block hashes, block records stored under mismatched hash keys, missing canonical timestamp height indexes inside the best range, and canonical timestamp records with wrong embedded height or work hash instead of falling back to latest or returning forged rows.
Focused native tests must accept omitted or valid unsafe start-mining thread parameters and reject malformed, zero, or over-limit explicit thread parameters before starting mining.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate RPC admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native JSON-RPC admission for method policy, unsafe-method exposure, timestamp ranges, byte parser caps, and batch size only. Blueprint bindings and regressions additionally cover selected handler materialization and numeric-parameter fail-closed wiring. It does not prove RPC authentication, host firewall posture, plaintext control-plane safety, JSON parser correctness, base64/hex parser correctness beyond cap behavior, full handler semantics, mining liveness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean legacy network secure-channel predicates, the KDF preimage is the fixed secure-channel-v2 domain followed by one of three distinct i2r/r2i/aad labels and the exact offer, acceptance, confirmation, responder-secret, and initiator-secret byte sequences; initiator and responder roles choose opposite send/receive directional key slots; AES-GCM nonces are four zero bytes followed by the big-endian u64 counter; counter overflow rejects before encrypt/decrypt can advance; the modeled channel state transition increments only the send counter on successful protect and only the receive counter on successful open, using the matching directional key slot and nonce in each case; and modeled receive admission rejects authentication failure, stale duplicate, future-gap, slot-mismatched, or overflowed observations while preserving receive state before the next valid frame is admitted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Network.SecureChannel.directional_labels_distinct`, `aad_label_distinct_from_i2r`, `aad_label_distinct_from_r2i`, `send_recv_slots_distinct`, `key_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `sample_i2r_r2i_preimages_distinct`, `nonce_from_counter_zero`, `nonce_from_counter_max`, `nextCounter_accepts_below_max`, and `nextCounter_rejects_u64_max` prove the executable key-schedule and nonce helper behavior. The channel-state theorems `initial_state_counters_zero`, `protectFrame_accepts_below_max`, `openFrame_accepts_below_max`, `protectFrame_rejects_send_overflow`, `openFrame_rejects_recv_overflow`, `protectFrame_direction_and_counter`, and `openFrame_direction_and_counter` lift those helpers into send/receive state transitions with direction-separated slots and monotone per-direction counters. The observed-wire admission theorems `rejectedOpenFrameAdmission_preserves_state`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_accepts_current_authenticated`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_recv_overflow`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_slot_mismatch`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_nonce_mismatch`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_authentication_failure`, `duplicate_after_first_open_rejected_preserves_receive_state`, `future_before_current_rejected_preserves_receive_state`, and `current_after_future_failure_accepts` prove that failed duplicate, future, malformed, or unauthenticated opens preserve receiver state before recovery. `gen_network_secure_channel_vectors` emits Lean-derived KDF preimage, role, nonce, and schema-v2 open-admission examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `network` helpers used by `SecureChannel`; focused Rust regressions prove production AES-GCM decrypt failures do not consume `recv_nonce`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the legacy network secure-channel key schedule, role mapping, nonce layout, counter-overflow gate, explicit send/receive channel-state transition theorem, and failed-open receive-state preservation. Scope is KDF/nonce/control logic, not primitive cryptographic security or OS RNG quality.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the secure-channel key-schedule and nonce theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production KDF domain, direction labels, key preimage input ordering, role-to-key mapping, nonce bytes, counter overflow behavior, or modeled failed-open receive-admission behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused SecureChannel tests must prove corrupted and future AES-GCM frames reject without advancing the receive nonce, and that the current frame can still be accepted afterward.
Focused network adversarial tests must keep reconnects from reusing the same first key/nonce pair and must keep opposite directions from sharing the same AES-GCM key at nonce zero.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate secure-channel vectors and run the production conformance test.
Active P2P handshakes must propagate secure-channel construction, and encrypted send/receive must propagate channel encryption/decryption before frame publication or decode.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks legacy network secure-channel KDF preimage labels/order, role-to-directional-key mapping, nonce layout, counter overflow rejection, pure channel-state send/receive counter transitions, and failed-open receive-state preservation. Blueprint bindings additionally require the active P2P handshake and encrypted frame path to propagate secure-channel construction and encrypt/decrypt helpers. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, AES-GCM security, ML-KEM/ML-DSA security, OS RNG quality, full PQ Noise handshake correctness, network liveness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PQ Noise predicates, the session HKDF salt is the transcript hash, the IKM is shared_secret_1 followed by shared_secret_2, i2r/r2i/aad info labels are distinct for both concrete and role-selected send/receive slots, initiator and responder roles choose opposite send/receive key slots, AES-GCM nonces are four zero bytes followed by the big-endian u64 counter, counter overflow rejects before encrypt/decrypt advances, InitHello/RespHello/Finish signatures hash the exact domain-separated message fields with transcript hashes included only in RespHello and Finish, and the modeled session channel state increments only the send counter on successful protect and only the receive counter on successful open, using the matching directional key slot and nonce in each case. The modeled observed-wire receive admission accepts only the current receive slot and nonce; stale duplicate, future-gap, indexed stale replay, slot-mismatched, or overflowed observations reject while preserving receive state. Under explicit transcript, ML-KEM, ML-DSA, HKDF, and AEAD assumptions, accepted authenticated PQ handshakes establish PQ-native direction-separated channel facts, initial PQ Noise protect/open transitions use role-correct slots, AAD separated from directional labels, zero counters, direct transport-completion facts for opposite local/peer roles, zero initial counters, matched send/receive slots, matched slot infos, and AAD separation on both sides, and wrapper-completion facts for opposite role flags, zero initial byte counters, and first-frame payload-plus-tag wire accounting. The first local protected frame is admitted by the peer receive path under the matching directional slot and nonce; the legacy secure-channel comparison surface remains separately stated. The indexed peer-frame theorem family additionally proves that, for every modeled frame index below the accepted sequence bound and below u64 counter overflow, local protect and peer open use the same directional slot and nonce, carry opposite counters unchanged, advance the matching send/receive counter by one, and keep session AAD distinct from directional key info. The failed-open replay theorem additionally proves duplicate-after-first, future-before-current, and stale-after-three observations preserve the receive counter before the next valid frame is admitted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Network.PqNoise.hkdf_infos_distinct`, `aad_info_distinct_from_i2r`, `aad_info_distinct_from_r2i`, `aad_info_distinct_from_send`, `aad_info_distinct_from_recv`, `sample_hkdf_ikm_is_ordered_shared_secrets`, `hkdf_salt_is_transcript_hash`, `hkdf_ikm_orders_kem_shared_secrets`, `send_recv_slots_distinct`, `nonce_from_counter_zero`, `nonce_from_counter_max`, `nextCounter_accepts_below_max`, `nextCounter_rejects_u64_max`, `init_hello_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `resp_hello_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `finish_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `init_hello_signing_preimage_fields`, `resp_hello_signing_preimage_fields`, `finish_signing_preimage_fields`, `os_rng_mlkem_seed_source`, `os_rng_mlkem_seed_length`, `sample_resp_preimage_binds_transcript_hash`, and `sample_finish_preimage_binds_transcript_hash` prove the executable helper logic. The channel-state theorems `initial_state_counters_zero`, `protectFrame_accepts_below_max`, `openFrame_accepts_below_max`, `protectFrame_rejects_send_overflow`, `openFrame_rejects_recv_overflow`, `protectFrame_direction_and_counter`, and `openFrame_direction_and_counter` lift those helpers into send/receive state transitions with direction-separated slots and monotone per-direction counters. The observed-wire admission theorems `openFrameWithObservedWire_accepts_current_below_max`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_slot_mismatch`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_nonce_mismatch`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejection_preserves_state`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_stale_duplicate_after_first`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_future_gap_before_first`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_rejects_stale_nonce_one_at_three`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_next_frame_after_duplicate_rejects_accepts`, `openFrameWithObservedWire_current_frame_after_future_rejects_accepts`, and `openFrameWithObservedWire_current_frame_after_stale_three_rejects_accepts` prove that failed duplicate, future, or indexed stale opens preserve receiver state before the next valid frame is admitted. `Hegemon.Network.PqNoiseHandshakeChannel.accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_establishes_pq_channel_facts` and `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_initial_pq_protect_open_facts` compose those facts directly with the PQ Noise channel state that production `NoiseCipher` mirrors, while `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_transport_completion_facts` packages the corresponding local/peer role, zero-counter, matched-slot, matched-info, and AAD-separation facts that a completed transport session must expose. `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_wrapper_completion_facts` adds the wrapper-observable role flags, zero byte counters, and first-frame payload-plus-AEAD-tag wire accounting on top of that transport surface. `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_secret_rng_transcript_kdf_certificate` packages the accepted handshake with responder/initiator 32-byte OS-RNG seed-source facts, supplied-seed consumption by ML-KEM encapsulation, transcript-as-HKDF-salt, KEM-secret IKM ordering, separated KDF labels, and exact signing preimage fields. `pq_send_slot_matches_peer_recv`, `pq_recv_slot_matches_peer_send`, and `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_initial_pq_peer_frame_admission_facts` prove the first local protected frame is accepted by the peer open path under the matching receive slot and zero nonce. `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_establishes_channel_facts` and `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_initial_protect_open_facts` keep the legacy secure-channel comparison theorem. All handshake-to-channel theorems keep transcript binding, ML-KEM agreement, ML-DSA authentication, HKDF, and AEAD soundness explicit. `gen_pq_noise_vectors` emits Lean-derived KEM RNG-source, HKDF-input, role, nonce, signing-preimage, bounded initiator/responder frame-sequence examples, duplicate/future/stale-after-three replay-admission examples, real transport-completion examples, role-misbind examples, and schema-v7 wrapper-completion examples; the formal-core gate checks the helper cases against production `SessionKeys`, `NoiseCipher`, `PqHandshake`, and the OS-RNG seed helper, checks the frame-sequence cases against production `SecureSession::send` and `SecureSession::recv`, raw emitted ciphertext bytes, raw open/decrypt acceptance, and failed-open receive-counter preservation, checks `PqTransport::upgrade_outbound` plus `PqTransport::upgrade_inbound` completion outputs for expected role flags, remote peer IDs, zero initial byte counters, matched directional slots, and the first encrypted frame, and checks the shipped `network::pq_transport` plus `NativePqTransport` wrappers for the same completion outputs.
