Blueprint bindings additionally require both pq-noise and legacy network handshake encapsulation sites to call `random_encapsulation_seed` before `encapsulate_with_seed`, and require those handshake sites to call the supplied-seed encapsulation helper. `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_indexed_pq_peer_frame_admission_facts` packages an arbitrary modeled frame index, local receive counter, and peer send counter; `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_bounded_pq_peer_frame_sequence_facts` derives the concrete protect/open facts for any frame in a bounded sequence below `PqNoise.u64Max`. `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_failed_open_replay_admission_facts` packages duplicate-after-first and future-before-current receive-state preservation, `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_indexed_stale_replay_admission_facts` packages stale-after-three receive-state preservation, and `Hegemon.Network.PqNoiseHandshakeChannel.accepted_pq_v5_os_rng_transcript_kdf_directional_replay_channel_safety_certificate` composes replay safety with OS-RNG seed-source exclusion facts, accepted transcript-KDF, transport-completion, indexed frame-admission, role/slot, AAD, and directional channel facts. The v6 role-binding certificate and generated role-misbind vectors prove same-role endpoints reject each other's directional frames and preserve receive counters, binding production `session_key_slots` and `NoiseCipher` behavior to the Lean send/receive slot model. The v7 indexed-stale certificate `accepted_pq_v7_os_rng_transcript_kdf_directional_replay_role_binding_indexed_stale_channel_safety_certificate` composes that role-binding certificate with stale replay admission after multiple valid frames, and `pq-noise/src/noise.rs` adds a focused production regression for the same recorded-frame replay shape.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PQ Noise HKDF labels/input ordering, role mapping, nonce layout, counter-overflow gate, handshake signing preimage grammar, explicit send/receive channel-state transition theorem, observed-wire failed-open receive-state preservation, first peer-frame slot/nonce admission, bounded production SecureSession frame-path conformance, real PqTransport outbound/inbound completion-output conformance, shipped network/native wrapper completion-output conformance, KEM OS-RNG seed-source cases, supplied-seed encapsulation tests, production call-order bindings for both pq-noise and legacy network encapsulation sites, PQ-native assumption-explicit handshake-to-channel composition, and legacy secure-channel comparison. Scope is byte/control/source logic and assumption packaging, not primitive cryptographic security, OS RNG quality, transport liveness, future wrapper integrations, or complete native-node equivalence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the PQ Noise HKDF, role, nonce, or signing-preimage theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production PQ Noise HKDF label text, shared-secret ordering, role-to-key mapping, nonce bytes, counter overflow behavior, signing preimage field order, failed-open receive-counter preservation, or bounded SecureSession active-frame send/open behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production PqTransport outbound/inbound completion outputs away from the Lean transport-completion facts for role flags, remote peer IDs, zero initial byte counters, matched directional slots, or first-frame admission must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing shipped network::pq_transport or NativePqTransport wrapper completion outputs away from the Lean wrapper-completion facts for role flags, remote peer IDs, zero initial byte counters, matched directional slots, or first-frame accounting must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused PQ Noise tests must prove duplicate, future, and multi-frame stale replay failed opens do not advance the receive nonce before the next valid frame is admitted.
Focused PQ Noise tests must keep ML-KEM response and finish ciphertexts from being reproducible from public transcript-derived seeds.
Focused PQ Noise tests must prove the supplied seed helper produces the same ciphertext/shared-secret pair as direct ML-KEM encapsulation with that seed.
Focused legacy network tests must prove the supplied seed helper produces the same ciphertext/shared-secret pair as direct ML-KEM encapsulation with that seed.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate PQ Noise vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks PQ Noise session HKDF input labels/order, role-to-directional-key mapping, nonce layout, counter overflow rejection, handshake signing preimage grammar, pure channel-state send/receive counter transitions, failed duplicate/future/indexed-stale open receive-state preservation, first peer-frame slot/nonce admission, bounded production active-frame byte-path conformance, direct production PqTransport outbound/inbound completion-output conformance, shipped network/native wrapper completion-output conformance, KEM OS-RNG seed-source cases, production supplied-seed encapsulation helper consumption, a PQ-native assumption-explicit handshake-to-channel composition surface, and the legacy secure-channel comparison surface. It does not prove SHA-256 or HKDF implementation equivalence, AES-GCM security, ML-KEM/ML-DSA security, OS RNG quality, broader KEM ciphertext correctness beyond supplied-seed consumption, timeout/scheduler liveness, future wrapper integrations, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native sync admission, raw-ingress, block-range publication, and response-import predicates, response ranges are empty when the max-block cap is zero or the requested start is beyond the capped end, otherwise they serve only from the requested start through the minimum of requested end, local best height, and max response window; every accepted response range exposes generic bounded-request item-count facts for the served block count. Representative raw sync wire bytes are Lean-owned for canonical request and empty-response messages plus trailing-byte, missing-marker, and unknown-variant rejection, and production must drive those bytes through `decode_sync_message` before applying the existing request-range or response-import helpers. Published sync block ranges additionally accept only when an admitted range produced a matching served count, matching first and last heights, contiguous heights, a verified previous-parent anchor for non-genesis starts, contiguous served parent hashes, verified canonical rows, and verified non-genesis action bodies. Missing-block requests are empty when max-blocks is zero or the peer is not ahead, otherwise they request from local best plus one through the minimum of peer best and the capped window; inbound response block counts accept iff they are within the configured maximum. Accepted inbound response imports expose count-before-sort/import admission, deterministic sorted response heights, import-attempt and imported-block counts bounded by the response, stop-on-first-error behavior, and continuation requests only when the accepted response was nonempty and the peer remains ahead. Blueprint implementation bindings require inbound response-count admission to call the generic bounded-request helper with the sync response count as item count, require native_sync_loop to route response blocks through the admission/sort helper before progress tracking or import, require that helper to call the count gate before sort, require admitted sync response ranges to materialize canonical block rows through a propagated helper before any block is pushed into a peer response, require non-genesis sync rows to decode their native action bodies and verify the decoded action root before that materializer returns, and require `NativeNode::block_range` to evaluate the sync block-range publication table before returning rows.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems in Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission prove the executable native sync range and count-admission behavior plus bounded-request item-count facts for accepted outbound response ranges. Hegemon.Native.SyncRawIngress adds a representative byte-level ingress corpus over the shared `HNW1` network marker and postcard sync enum body: a canonical request, canonical empty response, request trailing byte, missing marker, and unsupported variant. The generated Rust gate feeds those bytes to production `decode_sync_message`, checks canonical request/response bytes against `encode_sync_message`, and then routes decoded requests to `native_sync_response_range` and decoded responses to `admit_and_sort_native_sync_response_blocks` plus `NativeSyncResponseImportProgress`. Hegemon.Native.SyncBlockRangePublicationAdmission adds the peer-visible outbound publication table: accepted publication carries admitted-range, served-count, first/last-height, height-continuity, previous-parent-anchor, parent-continuity, canonical-row verification, and action-body verification facts, with generated vectors for each rejection and precedence case. Hegemon.Native.SyncResponseImport adds the inbound response-import control-flow proof: accepted imports have response counts within the cap, impossible outcome traces longer than the response reject, import attempts and imported counts are bounded, first import error stops the suffix, sorted heights are deterministic, and follow-up requests require both a nonempty response and a still-ahead peer. gen_sync_admission_vectors, gen_sync_raw_ingress_vectors, gen_sync_block_range_publication_admission_vectors, and gen_sync_response_import_vectors emit Lean-derived normal, capped, empty, zero-cap, saturating-u64, exact-limit, over-limit, raw-byte, served-row drift, previous-parent drift, unverified-row, unsorted, error-stop, empty-ahead, peer-caught-up, and impossible-outcome examples; formal-core checks them against production helpers used by NativeNode::block_range, request_missing_blocks, outbound block-range publication admission, raw sync wire decode, native_sync_loop response handling, admit_and_sort_native_sync_response_blocks, and NativeSyncResponseImportProgress. Production inbound response-count admission constructs a generic bounded-request input with zero raw/decoded/per-item/aggregate/work dimensions and the sync response count as item count; the admitted response path now calls admit_and_sort_native_sync_response_blocks before allocating import progress or invoking import_announced_block, and the helper calls evaluate_native_sync_response_count_admission before blocks.sort_by_key. Production outbound block_range serving uses the same capped response-range helper before canonical row materialization and response-row publication, then evaluates `evaluate_native_sync_block_range_publication_admission` before returning blocks; focused regressions corrupt local canonical indexes and canonical action bodies to prove an admitted range errors instead of returning a truncated prefix, mismatched row, or forged body commitment.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native sync response range, raw sync request/empty-response ingress bytes, missing-block request range, zero-cap fail-closed behavior, saturating u64 edges, inbound response block-count rejection, bounded-request item-count facts for accepted outbound response ranges, and served block_range publication facts. Additional implementation bindings and regressions require overwide block_range serving to remain capped before response publication, non-genesis canonical sync rows to decode action bodies and verify decoded action roots before publication, and the final peer-visible range to pass the served-count/height/previous-parent/parent/verification table. Scope is representative raw-byte ingress, semantic sync admission, and canonical row materialization after decode, not full postcard parser correctness, full block validity, or network liveness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native sync admission theorems or concrete examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native sync response-range, missing-request, zero-cap, saturating, or response-count behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production sync wire decode, canonical request/empty-response encoding, request-range handoff, or empty-response import handoff away from the Lean-owned raw-byte corpus must fail generated-vector conformance.

Changing production outbound sync block-range publication admission away from the Lean executable served-count, height, previous-parent, parent, canonical-row, or body-verification table must fail generated-vector conformance.
The production publication input helper must reject truncated served ranges and unverified non-genesis body rows before peer-visible block_range publication.
Focused native tests must reject oversized inbound sync responses and keep zero-cap range/request helpers fail-closed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node native_sync_admission_rejects_oversized_responses --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node native_sync_ranges_fail_closed_when_cap_zero --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: Inbound sync response-count admission must be derived from the generic bounded-request item-count gate.
Overwide outbound sync block_range serving must remain capped to the bounded-request item-count surface before canonical rows are published into a peer response.
Focused native tests must reject admitted sync response ranges when canonical height rows or block records are missing or point at mismatched metadata.
Focused native tests must reject admitted sync response ranges when canonical non-genesis block action bytes do not decode to the committed action root.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native sync admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

Changing production inbound sync response import ordering, sort behavior, stop-on-error progress, over-limit rejection, or continuation-request behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native sync semantic admission after bounded message decode, binds representative raw sync request/empty-response/trailing/missing-marker/unknown-variant bytes to production decode and admission handoff, binds inbound response-count admission to the generic bounded-request item-count helper, proves accepted outbound response ranges expose bounded-request item-count facts, and production-binds inbound response import count/sort/progress/continuation control flow. Production bindings require capped non-genesis canonical sync row materialization to decode action bodies and verify decoded action roots before response publication. It does not prove full postcard parser correctness for arbitrary byte strings, SCALE codec correctness, PQ Noise transport security, full PoW/header validity beyond the metadata projection helper, fork-choice convergence, peer honesty, network liveness, storage durability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native mineable-action admission and ordered-selection predicates, a candidate artifact action is accepted for block-template construction only when it is the selected same-block candidate for the canonical ordered mineable-transfer count; a sidecar transfer action is accepted only when its local staged ciphertext metadata is present and size metadata agrees; non-candidate, non-sidecar actions are unaffected by sidecar metadata.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MineableActionAdmission.accepts_iff_mineable_preconditions` proves the complete executable block-template action admission predicate, with companion theorems for selected candidate acceptance, valid sidecar transfer acceptance, plain action acceptance, unselected candidate rejection, missing sidecar ciphertext metadata, missing sidecar size metadata, sidecar size mismatch, and candidate-before-sidecar precedence. The selection theorems `selected_candidate_none_when_no_mineable_transfers`, `first_matching_candidate_head_matches`, `first_matching_candidate_skips_nonmatching_head`, `selection_accepts_candidate_iff_selected`, `selection_accepts_transfer_iff_mineable`, and `selection_accepts_plain_action` prove the ordered candidate-selection table over a canonical action list: zero mineable transfers select no candidate, transfers are accepted iff mineable, plain actions pass, and only the first candidate with tx_count equal to the mineable transfer count is selected. `gen_mineable_action_admission_vectors` emits schema-v2 Lean-derived accept/reject and selection examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `evaluate_native_mineable_action_admission` plus `select_mineable_actions` before `prepare_work` and mined-block import recovery. The generated selection vectors cover zero-transfer rejection, one- and two-transfer candidate matching, unmineable sidecar exclusion from the transfer count, and canonical-order first matching candidate selection.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native block-template action admission and ordered candidate selection. Scope is the cheap local selection gate before mining work, not recursive proof soundness or global miner fairness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native mineable-action admission theorems or concrete examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native mineable-action admission or ordered candidate selection away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing production `select_mineable_actions` so candidate selection uses insertion order, arrival metadata, or a nonmatching transfer count must fail the schema-v2 generated-vector conformance step.
Native work preparation must drop stale or mismatched sidecar transfers before candidate artifact selection, while preserving matching staged sidecar transfers.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node prepare_work_drops_sidecar_transfer_ --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node prepare_work_keeps_sidecar_transfer_with_matching_staged_ciphertext --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must generate native mineable-action admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native block-template action admission and ordered same-block candidate selection only. It does not prove SCALE decoder correctness, ciphertext hash security or implementation equivalence, pending-action reload completeness, sled/BTreeMap consistency, DA durability, native tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, global miner fairness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native miner-identity transition, a non-genesis native block can be accepted or projected from storage only when its ML-DSA public-key length and parsing are valid, its 48-byte miner commitment matches that public key, its ML-DSA signature length and parsing are valid, and signature verification succeeds over the native signature message; genesis is exempt.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MinerIdentity.accepts_iff_native_miner_identity_preconditions` proves the executable native miner-identity table, with concrete theorem facts for valid non-genesis admission, genesis-without-identity admission, public-key length and parse failures, miner-commitment mismatch, signature length and parse failures, signature-verification failure, and rejection precedence. `gen_native_miner_identity_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_miner_identity_admission` helper. The blueprint also requires newly mined native blocks to call `sign_native_block_meta` before PoW metadata verification, atomic commit, state publication, or broadcast; requires storage/projection callers to route through `verify_native_block_meta_projection` before trusting startup canonical metadata, serving canonical sync rows, or exporting bridge witness light-client material; requires `verify_native_pow_meta` to propagate `verify_native_miner_identity` before light-client PoW verification; and requires the signing and verification paths to share `native_miner_signature_message`, whose Rust regressions bind nonce and work hash.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native self-contained ML-DSA miner side-auth admission. Miner registry governance and ML-DSA security remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening native miner-identity theorems or proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native miner-identity admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing native mined-block signing before PoW verification, commit, state publication, or broadcast must fail the blueprint gate.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

falsification_cases.description: Removing native miner-identity verification before native PoW metadata verification must fail the blueprint gate.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

falsification_cases.description: Startup, sync serving, and bridge witness export must reject unsigned non-genesis native metadata decoded from storage.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native self-contained miner identity admission and binds the production signing/verification/projection call order. It does not prove ML-DSA unforgeability or implementation equivalence, miner registration or stake governance, OS RNG quality for local identity generation, migration of legacy unsigned non-genesis dev-chain metadata, sled durability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean mined-work admission transition, native local mined-block import accepts work as fresh only when the current best hash equals the work parent hash and the current best height has a checked u64 next height equal to the work height; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MinedWorkAdmission.accepts_iff_mined_work_preconditions` proves the complete executable mined-work freshness predicate, with concrete theorem facts for valid admission, parent-hash mismatch, height mismatch, u64 height overflow rejection, parent-before-height rejection precedence, and accepting height u64::MAX only when the parent is u64::MAX - 1. `gen_mined_work_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_mined_work_admission` helper called by `import_mined_block` before pending-action replay, body commitment admission, supply accounting, and PoW metadata verification. The blueprint also gates local mined-block write order: `verify_native_pow_meta` must run before the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, or broadcast; `commit_mined_block_atomically` must commit the block record, height index, best pointer, canonical state/replay/ciphertext indexes, and pending-action removals before `publish_mined_state` or broadcast; and regressions assert invalid local seals leave pending actions, commitment state, and supply untouched while a mined action block reloads from canonical sled state after reopening.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native local mined-work freshness admission. Scope excludes work-template construction, pending-action replay, body commitment replay, supply accounting, and PoW threshold verification.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the mined-work admission theorem or concrete overflow/rejection facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production mined-work freshness admission table away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
Native local mined-work import must reject a best tip at u64::MAX instead of saturating and accepting same-height work.
Native local mined-block import must reject an invalid mined seal before mutating pending actions, commitment state, or supply state.

The blueprint gate must reject the mined-work admission claim if local mined-block PoW metadata verification drifts after the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, or block broadcast.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: The blueprint gate must reject mined import wiring if the atomic mined-block sled commit drifts after in-memory state publication or broadcast.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: A mined block containing an action must reload the best header, height index, commitment state, archived ciphertext, and empty pending-action tree from sled after reopening.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native mined-work admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native local mined-work freshness admission, and the blueprint/regression gate enforces that final local PoW metadata verification precedes the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, and broadcast, and that the atomic commit precedes in-memory state publication plus broadcast. Mined import now commits the block record, height index, best pointer, canonical state/replay/ciphertext indexes, and pending-action removals in one sled transaction before publishing the next in-memory state. Nonwinning announced side-branch block-record reload is covered separately by the native block replay-refinement implementation-binding gate. It does not prove mining work template construction, pending-action replay, body commitment replay, action payload validation, supply accounting, PoW hash-threshold validity beyond the production verifier call, wall-clock correctness, fork-choice, sled transaction implementation correctness, filesystem/fsync durability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean storage-durability admission transition, critical native storage mutations may be treated as publishable or complete only when the operation is supported, the storage transaction/write step accepted, and the explicit durability flush step accepted; unsupported-operation rejection precedes transaction rejection, and transaction rejection precedes flush-failure reporting.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.StorageDurabilityAdmission.accepts_iff_storage_durability_preconditions` proves the complete executable predicate, and concrete theorems pin valid acceptance, unsupported-operation rejection precedence, durability-flush rejection, and transaction-before-flush rejection precedence. `gen_storage_durability_admission_vectors` emits schema-v2 Lean-derived mined-block, canonical-reorg, canonical-index-repair, noncanonical-record, pending-action, ciphertext-sidecar, proof-sidecar, genesis-bootstrap, genesis-marker-repair, startup staged-ciphertext repair, startup staged-proof repair, startup pending-action repair, shutdown-flush, transaction-rejected, flush-failed, and unsupported-operation examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_storage_durability_admission` helper and the native storage-operation label parser. The production `flush_native_db_durability_barrier` performs a database flush and maps failures through the same rejection table; `flush_native_durability_barrier` routes initialized-node paths to that helper with a typed operation, and graceful shutdown now routes SIGTERM/Ctrl-C process exit through the same typed shutdown-flush operation. Blueprint bindings require mined-block import to propagate the barrier before prepared-work cleanup, in-memory publication, or broadcast; canonical reorg to propagate it before publication; canonical-index repair and nonwinning side-branch persistence to propagate it before completion; pending-action and sidecar staging to propagate it before in-memory publication; genesis bootstrap/marker repair to propagate it before startup succeeds; startup staged-sidecar and pending-action quarantine cleanup to propagate the DB durability barrier before the repaired startup state can be returned for publication; and graceful process shutdown to stop mining and flush the DB through the same storage-durability gate before completion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the explicit native storage durability barrier. Scope is the admission table and production wiring around supported storage-operation labels, sled transaction/write success, plus explicit database flush; it does not prove sled or operating-system crash semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the storage-durability admission theorems or concrete precedence facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production storage-durability admission table or accepted storage-operation label set away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.

The blueprint gate must reject storage durability if mined-block import, canonical reorg, canonical-index repair, noncanonical block-record persistence, pending-action staging, sidecar staging, startup staged-sidecar repair, startup pending-action repair, or genesis bootstrap/repair stop propagating the explicit durability barrier or move publication before it.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Committed mined blocks, canonical reorgs, startup canonical-index repairs, nonwinning side-branch records, and startup sidecar/pending-action repairs must reload from durable storage after reopen.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node mined_action_block_commit_reloads_canonical_sled_state reorg_action_block_commit_reloads_canonical_sled_state startup_canonical_index_repair_rebuilds_archive_atomically nonwinning_announced_side_branch_record_reloads_without_canonicalizing load_staged_sizes_ load_staged_proofs_ pending_action_startup --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must generate native storage-durability admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the explicit storage durability admission table and supported storage-operation label set, and binds production critical mutation paths to propagate the typed database flush barrier before publication or success, including startup genesis bootstrap/marker repair, startup staged-sidecar repair, startup pending-action quarantine cleanup, and user-facing pending-action/sidecar staging. It reduces the filesystem durability residual by making the barrier explicit and gated, but it does not prove sled transaction implementation correctness, operating-system fsync semantics, disk firmware honesty, crash-consistency below sled, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean atomic-commit manifest transition, native mined-block commits, canonical reorg commits, canonical-index repairs, and noncanonical block-record persistence accept only when their declared row-family write, clear, and removal counts match the kind-specific source counts before storage mutation. Canonical reorg acceptance additionally requires the replacement chain and its block-record/height-index write-set projection to pass the Lean-modeled structural admission table before canonical replay, index planning, or commit.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.AtomicCommitManifestAdmission.accepts_iff_atomic_commit_manifest_preconditions` proves the executable manifest predicate, with concrete valid-kind and mismatch rejection theorems for mined plan length, block-record writes, height-index writes, best-pointer writes, canonical-index clears, pending-tree clears, pending removals/writes, commitment writes, nullifier writes, bridge replay writes, ciphertext index/archive writes, and staged ciphertext removals. The companion theorem `Hegemon.Native.CanonicalReorgChainAdmission.accepts_iff_canonical_reorg_chain_preconditions` proves the canonical reorg replacement-chain gate over structural chain predicates, best-tip agreement, and block-record/height-index projection equality. `gen_atomic_commit_manifest_admission_vectors` and `gen_canonical_reorg_chain_admission_vectors` emit Lean-derived cases checked against production helpers. The native commit helpers build manifests from the same action/planning/index data they use for persistence and must propagate the gates before noncanonical record persistence, canonical reorg replay/planning, or any atomic sled transaction. Focused Rust regressions additionally require mined commit row derivation to reject ciphertext hash/size cardinality drift before sled mutation, and require canonical reorg commits to revalidate pending sidecar actions with surviving staged maps while atomically removing staged ciphertext rows consumed by the new canonical chain.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a bounded implementation-equivalence hardening slice for native atomic commit manifests and canonical reorg chain/write-set projection. It proves the declared row-family manifest gate, the replacement-chain admission table, and production ordering, not sled internals or OS durability.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the atomic commit manifest theorem or its concrete mismatch theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing native atomic commit manifest admission behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
Changing canonical reorg replacement-chain or write-set projection admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.

The blueprint gate must reject native commit paths that stop calling or propagating the manifest gate before noncanonical block-record persistence or sled transaction execution, or canonical reorg paths that stop admitting the replacement chain before replay/planning/commit/publication.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Canonical reorg replacement-chain admission must reject mismatched block-record, height-index, or best-tip projections before reorg commit.
Committed mined blocks, canonical reorgs, startup canonical-index repairs, and nonwinning side-branch records must reload from durable storage after reopen.
Mined-block atomic commit must reject ciphertext hash/size count drift before any sled transaction can publish partial block, ciphertext, pending-action, or staged-sidecar effects.

Canonical reorg must preserve valid pending sidecar actions when their staged ciphertext survives the new canonical chain, and must remove consumed staged ciphertext rows in the same atomic reorg commit.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native atomic-commit manifest vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the declared native atomic commit manifest table plus canonical reorg replacement-chain/write-set projection admission, and binds production commit/reorg helpers to propagate those checks before persistence, replay/planning, transaction execution, or publication. It does not prove sled transaction implementation correctness, filesystem durability, OS fsync semantics, disk firmware behavior, row byte serialization correctness beyond existing codec gates, raw action replay equivalence, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean work-template admission transition, native mining template construction emits a work height only when the current best height has a checked u64 successor and the cumulative Work48 value can be advanced for the configured PoW bits; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.WorkTemplateAdmission.accepts_iff_work_template_preconditions` proves the complete executable template-admission predicate, with concrete theorem facts for valid admission, u64 height overflow rejection, cumulative-work overflow rejection, height-before-work rejection precedence, and accepting height u64::MAX only when the parent is u64::MAX - 1. `gen_work_template_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_work_template_admission` helper called by `prepare_work` before header preimage construction. Direct regressions cover the former `saturating_add(1)` height edge and the former `cumulative_work_after(...).unwrap_or(best.cumulative_work)` fallback.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native mining work-template admission. Scope excludes pending-action preview, body replay, supply accounting, PoW seal liveness, and full native-node equivalence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the work-template admission theorem or concrete overflow/rejection facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production work-template admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
A native best tip at u64::MAX must not produce a same-height work template.
A best tip whose Work48 value cannot be advanced must not produce a work template with unchanged cumulative work.

A native miner configured with HEGEMON_MINER_ADDRESS must synthesize a wallet-decryptable shielded coinbase before previewing roots, computing supply, or hashing the work template, and must ignore staged coinbase recipients for local mining.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native work-template admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native mining work-template admission only. It does not prove pending-action preview correctness, action validity, header preimage hash security, PoW seal search or liveness, import-time body replay, supply accounting, storage durability, fork-choice convergence, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean recursive-artifact context admission transition, native recursive artifact verification constructs a consensus block header context only when the current best height has a checked u64 successor; otherwise max-height overflow rejects before backend verification context construction.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.RecursiveArtifactContextAdmission.accepts_iff_recursive_artifact_context_preconditions` proves the complete executable context-admission predicate, with concrete theorem facts for valid admission, u64 height overflow rejection, and accepting height u64::MAX only when the parent is u64::MAX - 1. `gen_recursive_artifact_context_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_recursive_artifact_context_admission` helper called by native recursive artifact verification before constructing the consensus block header passed to `ParallelProofVerifier`. A direct regression covers the former `saturating_add(1)` height edge.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native recursive artifact context admission. Scope excludes tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, body replay, and full native-node equivalence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the recursive-artifact context admission theorem or concrete overflow facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production recursive-artifact context admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
A native best tip at u64::MAX must not produce a same-height recursive artifact verifier context.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate native recursive-artifact context admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native recursive artifact context height admission only. It does not prove tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, DA-root binding, statement-commitment hash security, body replay, supply accounting, storage durability, fork-choice convergence, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native sidecar upload admission predicates and raw JSON projection table, ciphertext/proof upload requests accept only when representative da_submitCiphertexts/da_submitProofs request bytes pass strict JSON shape decoding, the required upload array is present, request-count caps hold, byte fields decode within bounds, staged sidecar insertions accept only when capacity remains or the item replaces an existing staged entry, proof metadata accepts only when binding_hash is present and valid and proof is present, and decoded proof bytes accept only when nonempty, within the native tx-leaf artifact cap, and whose decoded proof binding hash equals the upload key.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.ciphertext_request_accepts_iff_not_over_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.proof_request_accepts_iff_not_over_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.ciphertext_capacity_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.proof_capacity_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.proof_metadata_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.proof_decoded_accepts_iff_preconditions`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission.proof_binding_hash_mismatch_rejects`, and companion request/capacity/proof precedence examples prove the executable sidecar upload admission behavior. `gen_sidecar_upload_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, including `proof_binding_hash_mismatch`, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_native_*_sidecar_*_admission` helpers used by `da_submitCiphertexts` and `da_submitProofs`. `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadRawJsonProjection.raw_sidecar_upload_case_matches_iff_expected_rejection` and companion raw-case theorems add representative request-byte coverage for valid ciphertext upload, malformed JSON, unknown fields, missing/non-array upload fields, too-many arrays before byte parsing, invalid ciphertext/proof bytes, missing/invalid proof metadata, and empty proof bytes. `gen_sidecar_upload_raw_json_projection_vectors` drives those bytes through production serde_json, strict deny-unknown-field request decoding, byte parsing, and the same admission helpers. The production handlers now preflight all batch items into prepared rows, write accepted ciphertext/proof sidecar rows with one sled transaction, and publish the staged in-memory map only after the storage-durability barrier, so a later invalid item cannot leave hidden sidecar storage. Persisted staged ciphertext reload and staged proof reload are represented by the separate `native.staged-ciphertext-reload` and `native.staged-proof-reload` nodes.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native DA sidecar upload admission. Persisted staged ciphertext reload is tracked separately by native.staged-ciphertext-reload. Scope is upload admission policy, not parser correctness, RPC exposure safety, or sidecar availability.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the sidecar upload admission theorems or precedence examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production request-count, capacity, proof metadata, decoded proof-byte, or decoded proof binding-hash/key equality behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production DA sidecar upload raw JSON request decoding, unknown-field rejection, required-array handling, byte parsing, or first-failure admission order away from the Lean raw projection fixtures must fail generated-vector conformance.

Focused native tests must reject oversized sidecar upload batches, invalid proof metadata, empty sidecar proofs, proof binding-hash/key mismatches, and mixed valid/invalid upload batches without publishing memory or sled side effects, while accepting replacements at capacity.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node sidecar_upload --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node submit_ciphertexts_rejects_ --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node submit_proofs_rejects_ --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node mixed_batch_atomically --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must generate sidecar upload admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native DA sidecar upload admission plus representative raw JSON request projection, including strict request-shape decoding, decoded proof binding-hash equality with the upload key, and all-or-nothing production staging for upload batches. Persisted staged ciphertext reload is covered by native.staged-ciphertext-reload, and persisted staged proof reload is covered by native.staged-proof-reload. It does not prove RPC authentication, public exposure safety for unsafe methods, complete serde_json/base64/hex parser correctness, ciphertext hash derivation/security, sled durability below the accepted transaction, DA sidecar availability, native tx-leaf proof soundness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the Lean pre-heavy-work resource surface, if the resource-budget, RPC admission, sidecar upload, mineable-action, action-request projection, transfer-payload, bridge action resource, candidate-artifact, tx-leaf artifact parser/resource projection, receipt-root schedule/resource projection, pending/block action parser-resource, and sync response parser-resource surfaces are all accepted, then the combined surface exposes the concrete resource facts needed before expensive work: pending-action and staged-proof byte totals are within caps, timestamp rows and RPC batches are within caps, hex/base64 decoded-byte inputs satisfy raw and decoded caps, submit-action JSON/kernel-envelope/route/nullifier/public-args/exact-payload projection predicates hold, sidecar upload request/capacity/proof predicates satisfy their preconditions, mineable candidate/sidecar action predicates satisfy their preconditions, transfer proofs and inline ciphertexts are in cap, bridge receipt and bridge message payload bytes are in cap before payload-hash binding or receipt decode/verification, recursive candidate proofs and tx counts are in cap, tx-leaf artifact parser shape facts and raw/decoded/item/proof/aggregate/work caps hold, receipt-root expected leaves, leaf/fold counts, fold-shape schedule facts, and raw/decoded/item/max-item/aggregate/work caps hold, accepted pending/block action parser rows and sync response rows carry generic bounded-request cap facts, and the reusable bounded-request table rejects the first raw-byte, decoded-byte, item-count, per-item-byte, aggregate-byte, or work-unit over-cap condition deterministically. The same accepted pre-heavy surface can be paired with accepted codec-canonical publication facts so cheap resource bounds and exact canonical byte publication are exposed before sync import or pending-action publication reaches heavier semantic/proof work.
nodes.informal_argument: The theorem `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyWorkResourceBoundSurface.accepted_preheavy_resource_bound_surface_exposes_bounds` composes accepted facts from `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission`, and `Hegemon.Native.MineableActionAdmission`. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyWorkResourceBoundSurface.action_request_projection_accepts_implies_preheavy_bounds` derives the strict submit-action projection preconditions from accepted action-request projection, including JSON decode acceptance, absent kernel-envelope fields, supported route, scoped/bounded/hex-valid nullifiers, bounded/base64-decoded public args, and exact route-payload decoding. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.ReceiptRoot.receipt_root_schedule_accepts_implies_facts` exposes nonzero expected leaves, exact leaf/fold-count agreement, and exact fold-shape schedule acceptance from the receipt-root structural gate. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.ReceiptRoot.accepted_receipt_root_resource_exposes_bounds` projects accepted receipt-root artifact bytes into reusable bounded-request facts for raw bytes, decoded bytes, dynamic item count, max item bytes, aggregate bytes, and work units. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.TxLeafArtifactProjectionRefinement.accepted_native_tx_leaf_artifact_resource_exposes_bounds` projects accepted tx-leaf artifact bytes into reusable bounded-request facts for raw bytes, decoded bytes, dynamic item count, STARK proof bytes, aggregate bytes, and work units. The theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.RecursiveBlockAdmission.recursive_block_artifact_accepts_implies_bounded_request_facts` projects accepted recursive block artifact bytes into the same reusable bounded-request table from the existing pre-decode artifact byte cap. The strengthened theorem `accepted_preheavy_public_input_parser_admission_bounds_verification_paths` adds those action-request bounds, accepted transfer-payload bounds, bridge action resource facts, candidate-artifact bounds, recursive block artifact resource facts, tx-leaf artifact byte-shape/resource facts, and receipt-root schedule/resource facts while carrying parser correctness and benchmark caps as explicit assumptions. `Hegemon.Native.BridgeActionResourceAdmission.accepted_bridge_action_resource_exposes_bounds` and `accepted_inbound_bridge_payload_resource_bounds_receipt_before_verify` add the bridge action resource projection, proving accepted bridge public_args, proof-receipt, message-payload, aggregate, item-count, and work-unit caps before receipt verification. `Hegemon.Resource.BoundedRequestAdmission.accepts_iff_resource_preconditions`, `accepted_bounded_request_exposes_all_caps`, and the companion rejection theorems add the reusable raw/decoded/item/work cap table for remaining parser families. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionByteParserRefinement.accepted_pending_action_byte_parser_with_resource_bounds_refines_wire_replay_rows` composes that generic table with accepted exact pending-action decode, block-action count binding, pending reload, and accepted wire replay, proving the resource item count matches declared block-action count, decoded payload count, and wire replay row count. `Hegemon.Native.SyncBlockReplayPublication.accepted_sync_block_response_with_resource_bounds_binds_raw_canonical_publication_from_decoded_rows` composes the same table with accepted bounded sync decode/count, exact block-action decode, wire replay projection, and raw canonical publication, proving the bounded-request item count matches both admitted response count and synced raw block batch length. Production `decode_block_actions` rejects over-cap declared/payload action counts, over-cap per-action action bytes, aggregate-byte overflow, and aggregate-byte over-cap before SCALE action decode; production recursive block artifact admission rejects oversized recursive_block_v1/v2 artifacts before deserialize; production inbound sync response-count admission calls `evaluate_native_bounded_request_admission` before sorting or importing response blocks; production inbound bridge payload validation now calls the bridge resource projection before payload-hash binding, nested receipt decode, or receipt verification; production tx-leaf and receipt-root vector conformance checks the Lean resource projections against decoded `superneo-hegemon` artifacts. Together these theorems and regressions add concrete consensus-facing parser families to the pre-heavy-work DoS boundary. `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyWorkResourceBoundSurface.accepted_preheavy_dos_certificate_covers_all_public_input_cost_classes` packages the accepted DoS certificate into an explicit 22-family public-input cost-class coverage certificate. `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyCodecCanonicalPublication.accepted_preheavy_codec_canonical_publication_certificate` is the cross-surface certificate: it reuses the accepted pre-heavy DoS certificate and the accepted codec-canonical publication certificate, keeping parser correctness and benchmark caps explicit while proving the accepted byte path is both bounded and canonical before heavy work. `accepted_preheavy_bounded_codec_canonical_publication_certificate` further requires concrete PendingAction, MintCoinbaseArgs, ShieldedTransferInlineArgs, ShieldedTransferSidecarArgs, OutboundBridgeArgsV1, InboundBridgeArgsV1, and BridgeVerifierRegistrationV1 SCALE wire witnesses, PendingAction exact-decode adapter equality, and metadata ordering on that same pre-heavy bounded/canonical review surface. The staged-proof pre-heavy vectors and theorem force `da_submitProofs` to run metadata admission, then staged proof byte-budget admission, then decoded proof binding admission, so over-budget uploads reject before native tx-leaf artifact binding decode, proof hashing, capacity admission, or staging. The generic staged-proof ordering theorems `staged_proof_upload_metadata_rejects_before_budget`, `staged_proof_upload_budget_rejects_before_decoded`, and `staged_proof_upload_decoded_rejects_after_budget_accepts` strengthen the previous sample vectors into reusable proof facts.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a Lean composition over existing theorem-backed admission surfaces, now including strict action-request projection facts, transfer payload, candidate artifact, tx-leaf artifact parser/resource bounds, receipt-root schedule/resource facts, and pending/block action parser resource caps. The block-action cap slice is backed by a production pre-decode helper and Rust regression; tx-leaf and receipt-root resource projections are backed by generated superneo-hegemon vectors.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the pre-heavy-work resource composition theorem must fail the Lean formal gate and claim theorem audit.
falsification_cases.gate: bash scripts/check_lean_formal.sh && cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Removing any of the admission-surface dependencies must fail the blueprint dependency and edge checks.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: The reusable bounded-request vector generator must remain executable and emit parseable JSON examples for exact-limit acceptance and over-limit rejection precedence.
falsification_cases.gate: cd formal/lean && lake exe gen_bounded_request_admission_vectors | python3 -m json.tool >/dev/null

falsification_cases.description: Changing staged proof upload ordering so decoded proof binding/hash work runs before the staged byte-budget gate must fail generated-vector conformance.
The reusable bounded-request table must remain matched by the production native bounded-request helper.
Bridge action public_args, proof-receipt, message-payload, aggregate, item-count, and work-unit projections must remain matched by the production helper.

Accepted native tx-leaf artifact raw-byte, decoded-byte, item-count, proof-byte, aggregate-byte, and work-unit resource projections must remain matched by production decoded superneo-hegemon artifacts.

Accepted native receipt-root artifact raw-byte, decoded-byte, item-count, max-item-byte, aggregate-byte, and work-unit resource projections must remain matched by production decoded superneo-hegemon artifacts.
Oversized inbound bridge proof receipts and bridge message payloads must reject before payload-hash binding, nested receipt decode, or receipt verification.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node bridge_inbound_proof_receipt_resource_rejects_before_receipt_decode_or_verify --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node bridge_inbound_message_payload_resource_rejects_before_receipt_verify --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: Native block-action decode must reject over-cap action counts, per-action payloads, and aggregate bytes before SCALE action decode.
Legacy block-circuit commitment proof decoding must reject empty or over-cap proof bytes before bincode and must reject trailing bytes before proof verification.
Inbound sync response-count admission must use the generic bounded-request item-count gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the pre-heavy-work resource module in the Lean formal gate and theorem audit.

The pre-heavy resource/DoS certificate and codec-canonical publication/non-malleability certificate must build as one Lean surface carrying sync exact-decode, pending/block-action exactness, decoded/published row equality, canonical publication, and explicit parser/cost assumptions together.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves Lean composition over accepted admission predicates and the reusable bounded-request decision table only. It exposes resource and precondition facts before expensive proof, sidecar, RPC, block-template, action-request projection, transfer-proof, recursive-artifact, tx-leaf parser/resource projection, and receipt-root parser/resource/folding work, while keeping parser correctness, per-family production binding, and benchmark caps explicit. It does not prove JSON parser correctness, RPC authentication, unsafe-method exposure safety, base64/hex parser correctness beyond modeled cap behavior, DA availability, sled durability, proof-system soundness, native receipt-root cryptographic fold soundness, mining liveness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native pending-action reload predicate, a persisted pending action is accepted into the reloaded local mempool only when its key is well formed, its embedded tx_hash equals the 32-byte sled key, the production pending_action_hash recomputation equals the embedded tx_hash, and no equal action hash has already been loaded; otherwise startup reload rejects with fixed malformed-key, key/hash-mismatch, recomputed-hash-mismatch, then duplicate precedence. Accepted reload can now be carried into the byte parser and publication refinement packages together with exact decode, block-action declared-count binding, action-hash, wire replay, and canonical publication facts.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.accepts_iff_pending_action_reload_preconditions` proves the executable key/hash acceptance predicate, while `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.malformed_action_key_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.key_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.recomputed_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.duplicate_pending_action_rejects`, and the companion precedence theorems pin the reject order. `gen_pending_action_reload_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_pending_action_reload`, the helper used by `load_pending_actions`. Blueprint bindings and focused regressions additionally require persisted local mempool entries to be semantically unique after removing arrival time from their hash preimage, require semantically re-admitting those loaded entries before they can feed mining selection, and require canonical reorg pending resurrection to re-admit existing pending actions before orphaned old-chain actions can be persisted. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionByteParserRefinement.accepted_pending_action_byte_parser_refines_wire_replay_rows` derives projected replay row-count equality from exact pending/block-action decode, accepted pending reload, accepted wire replay, and the production count binding `wireProjection.actionCount = blockActionDecode.declaredTxCount`. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionBytePublicationRefinement.accepted_pending_action_bytes_bind_tx_leaf_publication` then carries that accepted parser/reload surface through action-hash admission, raw canonical publication, and native tx-leaf/canonical statement artifact facts.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native pending-action startup key/hash reload. Semantic startup re-admission and canonical reorg pending resurrection are covered by Rust implementation bindings and focused regressions; sled atomicity, filesystem durability, SCALE decode equivalence, and pending_action_hash cryptographic security remain outside Lean.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the pending-action reload theorems or precedence examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production key-shape, key/hash, recomputed-hash, duplicate, or rejection-precedence behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Focused native tests must reject persisted pending-action entries with malformed keys, sled-key/embedded-hash drift, or recomputed pending_action_hash drift before reloaded actions can feed mining selection.
Persisted pending-action reload must reject entries whose semantic action hash is duplicated after arrival time is removed from the preimage.
Focused native tests must reject semantically invalid persisted pending actions after canonical state, bridge replay, staged ciphertext, and staged proof reload but before NativeState publication.
Canonical reorg pending resurrection must semantically re-admit existing pending actions before orphaned old-chain actions so duplicate orphaned nullifiers are quarantined before reorg persistence.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate pending-action reload vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native pending-action startup key/hash reload behavior. Blueprint bindings and regressions additionally require startup to reject persisted arrival-time-only semantic duplicates and semantically re-admit loaded pending actions against the loaded canonical state, bridge replay set, staged ciphertext metadata, staged proof cache, and mempool resource budgets before NativeState publication; they also require canonical reorg pending resurrection to revalidate the final pending set before atomic reorg persistence or in-memory publication. It does not prove sled atomicity, filesystem durability, I/O error behavior, SCALE decode implementation equivalence, BLAKE3 pending_action_hash or pending_action_semantic_hash derivation/security or implementation equivalence, tx leaf proof soundness, mining selection fairness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native staged-ciphertext reload predicate, a persisted ciphertext sidecar entry is accepted for restored staged-size metadata only when its key is well formed, its raw ciphertext length is within the configured cap, the production ciphertext hash equals the 48-byte key, and staged-entry capacity remains; otherwise the entry is rejected with fixed malformed-key, oversize, hash-mismatch, then capacity precedence.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.StagedCiphertextReload.accepts_iff_staged_ciphertext_reload_preconditions` proves the executable acceptance predicate, while `Hegemon.Native.StagedCiphertextReload.malformed_ciphertext_key_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedCiphertextReload.oversized_ciphertext_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedCiphertextReload.ciphertext_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedCiphertextReload.staged_ciphertext_capacity_reached_rejects`, and the companion precedence theorems pin the drop order. `gen_staged_ciphertext_reload_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_staged_ciphertext_reload`, the helper used by `load_staged_sizes_with_limits` before persisted ciphertext metadata can feed mining selection.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for per-entry native staged ciphertext startup reload. Scope is the deterministic admission/drop table over observed persisted entries; sled atomicity, filesystem durability, and ciphertext-hash cryptographic security remain outside Lean.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the staged-ciphertext reload theorems or precedence examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production key-shape, byte-cap, hash-match, capacity, or rejection-precedence behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused native tests must reload staged ciphertext metadata only from hash-bound raw sidecar bytes and must purge hash-mismatched, oversized, and over-capacity persisted cache entries.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate staged-ciphertext reload vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native staged-ciphertext startup reload per-entry admission/drop behavior only. It does not prove sled atomicity, filesystem durability, I/O error behavior, base64/hex parser correctness, ciphertext hash derivation/security or implementation equivalence, DA sidecar availability, native tx-leaf proof soundness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native staged-proof reload predicate, a persisted proof sidecar entry is accepted into the restored proposer-local proof cache only when its key is well formed, its proof bytes are nonempty, its proof length is within the configured cap, staged-proof count capacity remains, aggregate staged-proof byte capacity remains, and the decoded native tx-leaf public fields recompute the same binding hash as the persisted key; otherwise the entry is dropped with fixed malformed-key, empty-proof, oversize, staged-count-capacity, staged-byte-capacity, then proof-binding-hash-mismatch precedence.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.accepts_iff_staged_proof_reload_preconditions` proves the executable acceptance predicate, while `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.malformed_proof_key_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.empty_proof_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.oversized_proof_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.staged_proof_capacity_reached_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.staged_proof_byte_capacity_reached_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.StagedProofReload.proof_binding_hash_mismatch_rejects`, and the companion precedence theorems pin the drop order. `gen_staged_proof_reload_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_staged_proof_reload`, the helper used by `load_staged_proofs_with_limits` before persisted proof sidecars can feed proposer-local artifact coordination.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for per-entry native staged proof startup reload. Scope is the deterministic admission/drop table over observed persisted proof-cache entries, including native tx-leaf public-field binding-hash equality with the persisted key; sled atomicity, filesystem durability, full proof byte parser correctness, cryptographic binding-hash security, and native tx-leaf proof soundness remain outside Lean.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the staged-proof reload theorems or precedence examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production key-shape, nonempty-proof, byte-cap, count-capacity, byte-capacity, proof/key binding-hash equality, or rejection-precedence behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Focused native tests must reload staged proof sidecars only from well-formed nonempty in-budget entries whose decoded native tx-leaf public fields bind to the persisted key, and must purge malformed, empty, oversized, count-overflow, byte-overflow, and binding-mismatched persisted cache entries.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate staged-proof reload vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native staged-proof startup reload per-entry admission/drop behavior only. It does not prove sled atomicity, filesystem durability, I/O error behavior, base64/hex parser correctness, DA/proof sidecar availability, native tx-leaf proof soundness, proof byte parser correctness beyond size admission, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native candidate-artifact admission transition, accepted candidate actions must carry no shielded state deltas, must have route public_args that exact-decode as SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs, must have that route payload equal the candidate_artifact payload field, must have a decoded artifact payload, and that artifact must match the shipped recursive_block_v2 schema, nonzero/in-bound tx count, nonzero DA chunks, verifier profile, no custom proof-kind route, empty legacy commitment-proof bytes, absent receipt-root payload, present nonempty recursive proof bytes, and recursive proof size cap. The raw CandidateArtifact / SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs SCALE wire witnesses must expose fixed field widths, canonical compact prefixes, full byte consumption, canonical re-encoding, and representative parser rejection cases for the route-payload premise. Accepted candidate artifacts must also project declared bytes, proof bytes, receipt bytes, recursive proof bytes, tx count, and DA chunk count into the reusable bounded-request caps before acceptance.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.valid_candidate_artifact_accepts`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.state_deltas_present_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.route_payload_decode_failed_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.route_payload_artifact_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.artifact_missing_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.schema_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.tx_count_zero_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.tx_count_too_large_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.da_chunk_count_zero_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.wrong_proof_mode_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.wrong_proof_kind_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.custom_proof_kind_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.verifier_profile_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.commitment_proof_present_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.receipt_root_present_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.recursive_payload_missing_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.recursive_proof_empty_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.recursive_proof_too_large_rejects`, and `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactAdmission.state_deltas_precede_artifact_validation` prove the executable admission table. `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactResourceAdmission.accepted_candidate_artifact_after_resource_exposes_byte_caps` proves accepted candidate artifacts expose bounded-request facts for declared bytes, total decoded bytes, tx count, per-proof-like bytes, aggregate proof-like bytes, and DA chunk work units before candidate acceptance. `gen_candidate_artifact_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples for valid artifacts, malformed route payload bytes, route payload/artifact mismatch, custom proof-kind rejection, existing artifact-shape failures, and schema-v2 candidate-artifact resource projection cases; the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_candidate_artifact_admission`, `validate_candidate_artifact_resource_projection`, and the reusable bounded-request helper used by native candidate action and artifact validation. `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactScaleWire.candidate_artifact_scale_wire_acceptance_exposes_facts` and `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactScaleWire.accepted_candidate_artifact_scale_wire_exact_decode` prove the raw CandidateArtifact / SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs SCALE wire predicate exposes total length, full consumption, and canonical re-encoding facts. `gen_candidate_artifact_scale_wire_vectors` emits recursive-block-v2, receipt-root, custom proof-kind, trailing, truncated, invalid enum, noncanonical compact-prefix, recursive proof overrun, and receipt-count overrun examples; formal-core checks the raw bytes against production `CandidateArtifact::encode`, `SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs::encode`, `decode_scale_exact::<CandidateArtifact>`, and `decode_scale_exact::<SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs>`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native candidate-artifact admission, explicit custom proof-kind rejection, cheap candidate-artifact resource projection, and representative raw CandidateArtifact / SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs SCALE wire exact-decode conformance. Scope is decoded action/artifact shape, shipped-route admission, route-payload wire exactness, and bounded-request resource projection, not recursive proof cryptographic soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the candidate-artifact admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production candidate-artifact admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing production CandidateArtifact or SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs SCALE encoding/decoding away from the Lean raw-byte wire witnesses must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Focused native tests must reject state-delta smuggling, malformed route payload bytes, route payload/artifact mismatch, missing payloads, zero tx count, wrong recursive route/profile, and oversized recursive proofs.
Focused native tests must project candidate artifact byte/count/work dimensions into bounded-request admission before accepting artifacts.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate candidate-artifact admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native candidate-artifact action/artifact admission, representative raw CandidateArtifact / SubmitCandidateArtifactArgs SCALE wire exact-decode conformance, and cheap bounded-request resource projection for candidate artifact byte/count/work dimensions. It does not prove arbitrary SCALE decoder correctness for every byte string, verifier-profile digest construction, recursive proof cryptographic soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, DA soundness, statement commitment correctness, block tree-transition correctness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native candidate-artifact coupling transition, accepted native blocks must have zero decoded candidate artifacts when they contain zero shielded transfers, and must have exactly one decoded candidate artifact whose tx_count matches the shielded transfer count when they contain one or more shielded transfers.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.accepts_iff_coupling_preconditions` proves that the executable coupling predicate is exactly the transfer/candidate count precondition. Companion theorems `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.empty_block_accepts`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.matched_candidate_accepts`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.candidate_without_transfers_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.missing_candidate_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.multiple_candidates_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.candidate_tx_count_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.no_transfers_precedes_tx_count_mismatch`, and `Hegemon.Native.CandidateArtifactCouplingAdmission.candidate_count_precedes_tx_count_mismatch` pin representative accept/reject and precedence facts. `gen_candidate_artifact_coupling_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_candidate_artifact_coupling_admission`, the production helper called by `verify_native_block_artifacts_locked` before recursive block artifact verification.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native block-level shielded-transfer/candidate-artifact coupling before recursive proof verification. Scope is count and tx_count matching, not recursive proof soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the candidate-artifact coupling theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production candidate-artifact coupling admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Native block artifact verification must reject a shielded transfer block with no candidate, with multiple candidates, or with a candidate tx_count mismatch.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate candidate-artifact coupling vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native transfer/candidate-artifact coupling only.
