Removing the loaded bridge-replay reload handoff into raw-ingress canonical publication must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a raw-ingress/pending-action/bridge-mint-replay/canonical-publication composition over accepted production-bound predicates, including a named final canonical replay-set membership and duplicate-rejection publication record when structured imported-replay projection facts bind the freshly imported replay set to the canonical replay witness. It can also consume accepted startup bridge-replay reload projection facts under explicit loaded/projected/imported replay premises. The policy-publication certificate ties the abstract accepted receipt mint/replay policy to the raw-ingress final replay record, but it keeps byte-key/native-replay-key projection as an explicit premise. The production review certificate composes that policy-publication record with accepted inbound receipt admission, native backend review posture, explicit external PQ receipt verifier soundness, authorized asset delta, final canonical replay membership, and final duplicate rejection on the same witness. The release-disabled bridge theorems prove only fail-closed current-release behavior: when policy receipt verification reflects the disabled release RISC Zero verifier, accepted mint-replay policy publication is impossible. The canonical-publication-safe and final-replay-safe theorems do not depend on external receipt/PQ verifier soundness; the replay theorem and production review certificate carry that soundness as an explicit assumption. It does not prove full SCALE/JSON parser correctness for arbitrary bytes, external-chain receipt validity, disabled RISC Zero receipt soundness, future PQ bridge receipt soundness, BLAKE3/DA-root/action-hash/payload-hash security or implementation equivalence, DA availability or retention, recursive proof soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, sled transaction correctness, filesystem durability, byte-key/native-replay-key projection refinement, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean block replay-refinement transition, native replay/accounting acceptance implies the ordered action-stream effect table accepted, checked native supply advancement produced the claimed supply digest, and every block commitment-admission precondition holds. The accepted-chain lift threads parent supply, commitment leaf cursor, action-stream imported nullifier state, and imported bridge replay state across a list of accepted native replay blocks, proving that accepted ledger replay equals checked native supply advancement, carries the same leaf cursor as accepted block summaries, has canonical commitment-start plans for every accepted block, preserves final nullifier uniqueness, and preserves final consumed bridge replay-key uniqueness. `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` packages those five startup/replay facts into one theorem. The projection lift proves that production-style replay inputs derive parent supply, leaf cursor, spent-nullifier state, and consumed-bridge-replay state from an explicit carried ledger state, the direct projected ledger-state executor is extensionally equal to accepted projected replay, the raw decoded projected executor is extensionally equal to accepted replay over raw replay inputs before inheriting the same startup-equivalence facts, and the raw decoded ledger/tree executor inherits commitment-root progression. The canonical publication lift packages accepted block-index reload, canonical-state reload, canonical reorg-chain admission, atomic commit-manifest admission, storage durability admission, and accepted ledger/tree replay into one publication fact surface. The traced Lean transition has the same result as the untraced transition, and accepted replay has the canonical stage trace action-stream, supply, block-commitment, accepted. In particular, an accepted multi-action replay summary cannot carry a counterfeit state-root predicate, claimed supply, stale leaf cursor, stale spent-nullifier state, or stale consumed-bridge-replay state under the modeled production-derived predicates. Accepted pending-action byte publication additionally composes exact pending-action decode, pending-action reload key/hash admission, block-action declared-count binding, action-hash admission, wire replay projection, and raw canonical publication into the same fact surface.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.BlockReplayRefinement.accepts_iff_block_replay_preconditions` proves the composed executable predicate over ordered action-stream planning, native supply advancement, and block commitment admission. `accepted_claims_expected_supply`, `accepted_has_action_stream_effect`, `accepted_implies_commitment_preconditions`, and `accepted_forbids_counterfeit_state_root` extract the facts needed by the per-block no-counterfeiting boundary. The original accepted-chain theorems `accepted_native_replay_chain_no_counterfeiting`, `accepted_native_replay_chain_nullifier_preconditions`, `accepted_native_replay_chain_nullifiers_unique`, and `accepted_native_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` retain the supply/nullifier replay surface. The stronger ledger-chain theorems `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_supply_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_leaf_cursor_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_commitment_plans_canonical_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_nullifiers_unique_from`, `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_bridge_replays_unique_from`, and `accepted_native_ledger_replay_chain_startup_equivalence` lift that boundary across blocks while carrying supply, leaf cursor, spent nullifiers, and consumed bridge replay keys. The projection theorems `Hegemon.Native.BlockReplayInputProjection.replayInputFromProjection_projects_carried_state`, `accepted_projected_native_block_claims_carried_state`, `accepted_projected_native_ledger_replay_chain_carried_states`, `accepted_projected_native_ledger_replay_chain_startup_equivalence`, `projectedLedgerStateAfter_eq_validate_projected_replay`, `rawProjectedLedgerStateAfter_eq_validate_raw_replay`, and `accepted_projected_ledger_state_after_startup_equivalence` make the production state-to-replay-input projection explicit, prove the direct projected ledger-state executor equal to accepted projected replay, prove the raw decoded projected executor equal to accepted raw replay inputs, and then reuse the carried ledger replay theorem. `accepted_raw_projected_ledger_state_after_startup_equivalence` adds the raw-decoded native block surface: decoded carried supply, leaf cursor, spent-nullifier state, consumed bridge replay state, actions, and block commitment flags project into the existing projected replay executor and inherit supply equality, leaf-cursor equality, canonical commitment-plan preconditions, carried-state preconditions, final nullifier uniqueness, and final bridge replay-key uniqueness. `Hegemon.Native.CanonicalPublicationRefinement.accepted_canonical_publication_refines_ledger_tree_replay` composes accepted block-index reload, canonical-state reload, canonical reorg-chain admission, atomic commit-manifest admission, storage durability admission, and accepted ledger/tree replay into a named publication fact package. `accepted_raw_canonical_publication_refines_ledger_tree_replay` lifts that same publication package through raw decoded ledger/tree replay and preserves raw carried-state preconditions. `replayInputFromRawDecodedBlock_projects_decoded_fields`, `raw_decoded_blocks_projection_round_trips`, `valid_raw_projected_ledger_state_after_accepts`, `valid_raw_projected_ledger_state_after_startup_equivalence`, `stale_raw_projected_carried_state_rejects`, `valid_projected_ledger_state_after_accepts`, `valid_projected_ledger_state_after_startup_equivalence`, `stale_projected_carried_state_rejects`, `stale_leaf_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects`, `duplicate_bridge_replay_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects`, and `stale_bridge_replay_native_ledger_replay_chain_rejects` pin representative carried-state acceptance/rejection cases. `traced_result_matches_untraced` proves that the traced evaluator used for conformance has the same result as the executable transition, and `accepted_trace_is_canonical` pins the accepted-stage order. `valid_two_action_replay_accepts`, `duplicate_nullifier_replay_rejects`, and `cross_action_bridge_replay_duplicate_rejects` pin representative multi-action replay behavior. `gen_block_replay_refinement_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the `node/src/native/mod.rs` production helper used by mined-block import, announced-block import, and canonical replay; that helper now materializes inline/sidecar ciphertext bytes before constructing the action-stream steps, passes materialized ciphertext counts to the production ordered stream helper, and composes block-commitment plus checked native supply advancement over height, fee total, and coinbase presence while exposing the production stage trace for differential checking. The projection helper `native_block_replay_refinement_input_from_state` centralizes the Rust construction of `leaf_start` and `parent_supply` from the same native state passed to replay refinement. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionBytePublicationRefinement.accepted_pending_action_bytes_bind_raw_canonical_publication` then composes accepted exact pending-action decode, accepted block-action decode, pending-action reload key/hash checks, action-hash admission, action wire replay projection, and the raw canonical publication theorem so the byte-admission surface carries exact parser, reload, action-hash, wire projection, replayed supply, leaf-cursor, commitment-root, nullifier, and bridge replay facts together. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionBytePublicationRefinement.accepted_pending_action_bytes_bind_tx_leaf_publication` additionally composes that byte-admission/publication package with the native tx-leaf/canonical statement artifact theorem, binding accepted raw action bytes, replay/publication facts, and tx-leaf statement/proof artifact facts in one Lean surface.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a compositional Lean refinement boundary over existing production-derived predicates. It narrows the gap toward end-to-end no-counterfeiting but is not a complete native-node equivalence proof.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the block replay-refinement theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing the Rust composite replay/accounting helper used by mined-block import, announced-block import, and canonical replay away from the Lean executable spec or its staged transition trace must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing the production replay helper stage order without changing the final result must fail because Rust compares its production trace to Lean-generated `expected_trace` for every replay-refinement vector.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate block replay-refinement vectors and run the Rust conformance test over the production helper composition.
The blueprint gate must reject the block replay-refinement claim if mined-block import, announced-block import, or canonical replay stop calling the non-test production replay helper.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: The blueprint gate must reject the block replay-refinement claim if the replay helper no longer precedes the declared payload validation, state mutation, persistence, broadcast, or explicit-chain reorganization callees in the critical import/replay callers.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Native preview, memory replay, and block replay-refinement must reject sidecar actions whose ciphertext payloads are not materialized from canonical DA storage instead of self-fulfilling ciphertext counts from commitments.
Announced-block import must reject a forged body commitment through the replay-refinement gate before malformed action payload validation can mask the no-counterfeiting failure.
A nonwinning announced side branch must persist as a hash-addressed block record across reopen without replacing the canonical height index.

A restarted node must reject a multi-block side-branch reorg candidate that combines sidecar transfer nullifier rows with a disabled inbound bridge replay key before publishing canonical indexes, nullifiers, ciphertext indexes, or bridge replay rows.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the composed replay/accounting helper now called by native mined-block import, announced-block import, and explicit-chain canonical branch replay over ordered decoded action summaries, materialized inline/sidecar ciphertext counts, checked native supply advancement, and block commitment predicates. The blueprint/regression gate now also binds nonwinning announced side-branch persistence through a named block-record helper, checks reload without canonical-height replacement, and covers a combined disabled-inbound sidecar/nullifier/bridge-replay reorg candidate failing closed before publication. It does not prove SCALE decoder correctness, sled transaction implementation correctness, filesystem/fsync durability, commitment-tree/hash security, BLAKE3/Blake2 implementation equivalence, full raw-action native-node replay equivalence, tx-leaf proof soundness, recursive proof soundness, PoW threshold validity, positive inbound bridge mint publication while the release path is disabled, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean message-root transcript function, valid 48-byte message hashes are encoded as domain || len(count-bytes) || count || len(hash) || hash..., invalid hash lengths are rejected before the Rust root helper can be called, and ordered pairs produce different transcripts when reversed.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean `Hegemon.Bridge.MessageRoot` kernel fixes the byte grammar that feeds bridge `message_root` hashing. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits empty, single, ordered-pair, reversed-pair, and invalid hash-length cases, and the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test checks the Rust root preimage helper against those Lean bytes. The independent bridge reference vectors continue to rederive the BLAKE3 XOF-48 payload hash, canonical message hash, single-message root, and inbound replay key without importing production bridge helpers.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted for Lean-backed transcript grammar and independent BLAKE3 reference vectors. Larger bridge light-client and external-chain proof systems remain out of scope.

falsification_cases.description: Changing the Rust bridge message-root transcript domain, count bytes, length prefixes, or ordered hash sequence must fail the Lean-generated conformance test.
Changing payload bytes or expected payload hash must fail vector verification.
Replay keys must differ across source chain ids and nonces.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node covers bridge message-root transcript grammar, canonical vector derivations, and replay-key vector checks. It does not prove BLAKE3 cryptographic security or implementation equivalence, every bridge light-client proof system, or external-chain covenant behavior.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean replay-state transition, successful staging prevents staging the same key again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same key.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Bridge.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Bridge.import_prevents_restaging` prove the no-duplicate properties over the executable replay-state kernel. `gen_bridge_vectors` emits Lean-derived bridge encoding and replay examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-kernel` conformance test against them. Persisted native startup reload of consumed replay keys is separately theorem-backed by `native.bridge-replay-reload`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-kernel bridge encoding and replay helper behavior. Native persisted replay reload canonical binding is separately covered by native.bridge-replay-reload.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the replay-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production bridge encoding or replay helper behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The production native startup reload claim must remain present for persisted inbound replay state.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks protocol replay-state duplicate rejection. Persisted native bridge replay reload is covered by native.bridge-replay-reload. This node does not prove BLAKE3 replay-key derivation, bridge light-client validity, bridge receipt soundness, or full native-node equivalence beyond the node's use of the protocol-kernel replay helper.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native tx-leaf artifact parser, valid bounded artifact bytes are accepted with the expected summary, missing backend bytes default according to the tx version/default backend rule at the permissive decode layer, strict canonical admission rejects missing backend bytes, active input/output flags must be binary and match compact nullifier/commitment/ciphertext-hash counts, and malformed backend IDs, trailing bytes, over-cap counts, over-cap proof lengths, over-cap commitment rows/coefficients, and truncation are rejected.
nodes.informal_argument: Native tx-leaf artifacts are a proof-system trust boundary before consensus consumes receipt metadata. The Lean parser models the exact byte grammar and count caps used by the production decoder, while the strict parser models release verifier admission that requires the explicit proof-backend byte. `Hegemon.Native.TxLeafArtifact.valid_projection_counts_accept`, `input_count_mismatch_projection_rejects`, `output_commitment_count_mismatch_projection_rejects`, `output_ciphertext_count_mismatch_projection_rejects`, `nonbinary_input_flag_projection_rejects`, and `nonbinary_output_flag_projection_rejects` pin the active-count projection table. `gen_native_tx_leaf_artifact_vectors` emits parser, strict-canonical, and active-count expectations, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding, strict canonical decoding, canonical re-encoding, and active-count projection against those Lean-derived cases. The formal-core gate now also runs a deterministic raw-byte oracle corpus covering valid explicit backend bytes, missing-backend permissive defaulting, trailing bytes, truncation, bad-backend mutation, over-cap serialized/public/commitment counts, oversized declared STARK proof length, summary projection, and canonical re-encoding.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native tx-leaf artifact parser grammar, canonical re-encoding behavior, and a deterministic independent raw-byte parser/admission corpus.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native tx-leaf artifact parser theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native tx-leaf artifact decoding or canonical re-encoding away from the Lean executable parser must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production native tx-leaf artifact decode, strict canonical admission, summary projection, or canonical re-encode behavior away from the independent raw-byte oracle over malformed and mutated artifacts must fail the corpus gate.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native tx-leaf artifact byte grammar, count caps, active-count projection, permissive backend-byte defaulting at decode, strict canonical backend-byte admission for verification, trailing-byte rejection, and canonical re-encoding behavior, and adds independent mutation-oracle evidence for the production raw parser/admission gate. It does not prove lattice backend security, STARK proof soundness, receipt-root fold soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, complete Rust parser implementation correctness, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native receipt-root parser, structural gate, and resource projection, valid bounded artifact bytes parse with the expected summary, non-empty expected leaf sets must match artifact leaf count, fold_count must equal leaf_count - 1, each fold must carry exactly five challenges and exact 11 by 54 parent-row shape, malformed counts, trailing bytes, and truncation are rejected, and accepted artifacts expose bounded raw bytes, decoded bytes, dynamic item count, max item bytes, aggregate bytes, and work units through the reusable bounded-request table.
nodes.informal_argument: Native receipt-root artifacts are consumed after tx-leaf verification and before backend fold verification. The Lean parser models the bounded byte grammar, the Lean schedule predicate models the cheap production validator now called by both native receipt-root verifier entry points before cryptographic fold checks, and `Hegemon.Native.ReceiptRoot.accepted_receipt_root_resource_exposes_bounds` projects accepted receipt-root artifacts into reusable bounded-request facts for raw bytes, decoded bytes, dynamic item count, max item bytes, aggregate bytes, and work units. `gen_native_receipt_root_vectors` emits accepted parser/schedule/resource cases and rejected mismatch cases, and the superneo-hegemon conformance test checks production decoding, canonical re-encoding, structural-gate agreement, and resource projection agreement. The formal-core gate now also runs a deterministic arbitrary-byte mutation corpus that compares production parse/schedule behavior with an independent raw-byte oracle over empty, valid one-/two-leaf, wrong expected-count, trailing-byte, zero-leaf, over-cap leaf/fold/challenge/row/coeff counts, overstated bodies, truncation, deterministic noise, oversized envelopes, and mutated valid receipt-root bytes.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native receipt-root parser grammar, structural fold-schedule gating, bounded-request resource projection, and a deterministic independent-parser mutation corpus. Scope excludes cryptographic fold soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native receipt-root parser/schedule/resource-projection theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production native receipt-root decoding, canonical re-encoding, structural scheduling, or resource projection away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

Changing production receipt-root parse, canonical re-encode, or structural schedule behavior away from the independent raw-byte parser/schedule oracle over malformed and mutated byte streams must fail the corpus gate.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native receipt-root byte grammar, cheap structural fold-schedule gating, and bounded-request resource projection, and adds independent mutation-oracle evidence for the production raw parser/schedule gate. It does not prove lattice backend security, backend transcript challenge derivation, fold proof soundness, statement-digest collision resistance, complete Rust parser implementation correctness, or full native backend cryptographic acceptance.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable Lean native-backend review policy accepts a vector bundle only when the backend remains candidate-under-review / structural-candidate, claimed security metadata is not below the configured floor, case names are unique, every case kind is supported, positive cases omit expected-error evidence, negative cases include expected-error evidence, and all required tx-leaf and receipt-root valid/invalid cases are present. The independent reference verifier must then accept the committed valid artifacts and reject malformed companions for spec digest, parameter fingerprint, proof digest, STARK proof, fold-row, root-commitment, and trailing-byte failures.
nodes.informal_argument: Native proof artifacts are a trust boundary. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.NativeBackendReviewPolicy.accepts_iff_native_backend_review_preconditions` pins the review policy, companion theorems pin posture, security-floor, duplicate-name, unsupported-kind, malformed-expectation, and missing-case rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by `scripts/check_native_backend_review_policy_vectors.py`, and `native-backend-ref` remains the byte-level independent verifier for the actual bundle.

target_review.notes: The target is accepted as a vector and byte-grammar claim, not as a cryptographic proof of the candidate backend.

falsification_cases.description: The vector bundle includes invalid cases that must continue to be rejected by the reference verifier.

Lean-generated native-backend review-policy vectors must fail on unsupported posture, insufficient security-floor metadata, duplicate case names, unsupported kinds, malformed expected-error flags, and missing required cases.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks review-policy coverage for the native backend vector bundle and then runs the independent byte verifier. It does not certify the lattice backend's security assumptions, the Module-SIS reduction, backend transcript soundness, BLAKE3 collision resistance, or full proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable release RISC Zero verifier predicate accepts exactly when image id, journal decoding, and verifier-enabled preconditions all hold; the shipped release configuration sets verifier-enabled false, so no release receipt can be accepted after prechecks.
nodes.informal_argument: RISC Zero bridge receipts remain offline experimental artifacts because the verifier path can pull classical Groth16/BN254 code into the binary. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.Risc0ReleaseVerifier.release_build_never_accepts` proves the release-disabled predicate is fail-closed, companion theorems pin image/journal rejection precedence, and generated Lean vectors are checked against the production helper used by `verify_risc0_bridge_receipt`. The existing node regression still checks that message-binding tampering rejects before the disabled verifier path and that a bound RISC Zero receipt is not staged.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a fail-closed residual-risk node; it is not production-eligible bridge receipt acceptance.

falsification_cases.description: Inbound bridge message-binding tampering must be rejected by node tests.
Lean-generated release-verifier vectors must match the production RISC Zero release-verifier helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the fail-closed release decision table only. It explicitly does not permit inbound RISC Zero receipt acceptance on the release node and does not prove future PQ bridge receipt soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: Accepted commitment-tree membership handoff requires consensus root equality with the state-merkle root, provided-root equality with the state-merkle root, parent root-history membership, state-merkle authentication-path export, and transaction Merkle-path verifier acceptance. The release gate also gives finite production conformance evidence for the real handoff: consensus CommitmentTreeState append roots equal state-merkle append roots, state-merkle authentication paths verify in transaction-circuit MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node under the real Poseidon2 merkle_node combiner, wrong leaf, wrong position, pre-append root, or unavailable future leaf paths reject, and the opt-in consensus append-transition certificate is checked against Lean-generated mutation vectors while replaying to the same root.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeMembershipRefinement.membership_handoff_accepts_implies_facts` packages the abstract handoff predicate into named facts over the existing transaction Merkle-path kernel. The companion theorems prove the valid handoff and representative root mismatch, missing path, wrong leaf, and same-root changed-leaf/prior-root rejection cases; the latter also proves root/export checks still hold and the Merkle path itself rejects. The formal-core Rust regression `commitment_tree_append_roots_match_state_merkle_and_membership_paths_verify` then checks the production implementations against each other: consensus `CommitmentTreeState::append`, state-merkle `CommitmentTree::append` and `authentication_path`, and transaction-circuit `MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node` using the real `merkle_node` combiner. The same formal-core slice runs the append certificate regressions, and `Hegemon.Consensus.CommitmentTreeAppend` plus `GenerateCommitmentTreeAppendVectors` now model and emit append leaf-index/count progression, sibling orientation/default choice, bounded root-history retention, and zero-limit unbounded-history cases checked against `CommitmentTreeState::append_with_certificate` and real Merkle replay. The consensus append slice now also checks oversized batch-extension rejection is atomic before root, leaf-count, frontier, or history mutation. The state-merkle crate now has a deterministic independent oracle that recomputes default nodes, append roots, authentication paths, root history, stale-root rejection, full-tree rejection, path bounds, and oversized batch-extension rejection against the production tree.

target_review.notes: Accepted as production-bound conformance evidence for commitment-tree append roots, membership-path handoff, the Lean-vector-checked replayable append-transition certificate, and the focused state-merkle independent append/path oracle. Scope is finite consensus/state-merkle/transaction Merkle-path interoperability plus Lean handoff and append-vector facts, not complete implementation equivalence, Merkle hash security, or complete native-node refinement.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the membership handoff theorem or rejection theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.

Drift between consensus append roots, state-merkle path export, transaction Merkle-path verification, replayable append-transition certificates, or atomic oversized batch-extension rejection must fail the production conformance regression.

Drift between state-merkle append roots, root history, authentication paths, stale-root rejection, tree-full behavior, path bounds, or atomic oversized batch-extension rejection and the independent tree oracle must fail the focused production test.
Drift between the Lean executable append mutation model and the production append certificate must fail the generated-vector conformance gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must run the commitment-tree membership handoff regression.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and checks append-root, membership-path handoff, append-transition certificate replay, focused state-merkle append/path/root-history implementation equivalence, and atomic oversized batch-extension rejection across the production consensus, state-merkle, and transaction Merkle verifier implementations. It does not prove Poseidon2 collision resistance, note commitment correctness, arbitrary raw-byte action decoding, exhaustive tree equivalence for all depths/sequences, full block replay/reorg/startup equivalence, transaction proof soundness, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native action stream, acceptance determines the same planned commitment-start sequence and next leaf cursor accepted by the canonical action-plan application model, and an accepted consensus tree transition exposes the applied commitment-tree root. Accepted ledger/tree replay chains additionally thread the commitment-root cursor in lockstep with accepted ledger replay, so the final root is exactly the result of the accepted tree-transition chain while the corresponding ledger replay still proves supply replay, leaf-cursor replay, canonical commitment plans, nullifier uniqueness, and bridge replay uniqueness. Raw-decoded native ledger/tree replay projections are proved extensionally equal to accepted tree replay over the projected inputs and inherit the same root-cursor, replay, carried-state, nullifier, and bridge replay facts. Accepted canonical publication additionally carries those ledger/tree replay facts across accepted block-index reload, canonical-state reload, canonical reorg-chain admission, atomic commit-manifest admission, and storage durability admission. Accepted raw-ingress full-byte publication further binds canonical commitment rows to the exact ordered commitments and planned indexes decoded from accepted action rows, binds canonical replay-set rows to exact ordered decoded nullifiers plus exact ordered planned bridge replay keys, and, under accepted action-plan row-start gating over decoded commitment starts/counts, carries the Lean append-summary/count surface together with commitment-root and leaf-cursor publication over the same raw witness. The raw-ingress ledger-integrity publication theorem packages those field-projection, append-publication, replay-set, accepted ledger/tree replay, replayed supply, commitment-root, leaf-cursor, final nullifier uniqueness, final bridge replay uniqueness, exact canonical row, ordered content, and append-summary index/count facts into one review certificate over the same witness. The native publication path-family ledger-integrity theorem composes that raw-ingress certificate with mined commit, announced import, sync import, reorg replay, canonical-index rebuild, block_range, and startup repair row-equivalence content, so exact canonical rows and final replay-set uniqueness are reviewable beside startup/reorg/mined/import path evidence in one certificate.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeRefinement.accepted_stream_commitment_starts_from` proves that accepted action-stream replay determines a commitment-start schedule. `commitment_starts_plan_application_accepts` and `accepted_stream_canonical_plan_accepts` prove that replaying those counts and starts through the action-plan admission model accepts with the same next leaf cursor. `accepted_stream_tree_transition_refines_applied_root` then composes that plan fact with `Hegemon.Consensus.TreeTransition.accepted_transition_returns_applied_root` so an accepted tree transition publishes the applied root. `Hegemon.Native.AcceptedChain.accepted_native_ledger_tree_replay_chain_integrity` lifts that boundary to accepted ledger/tree replay chains: the modeled root cursor follows accepted tree transitions, the projected ledger replay is accepted, and the existing accepted-ledger replay theorem still gives supply replay, leaf-cursor replay, canonical commitment plans, nullifier uniqueness, and bridge replay uniqueness. `Hegemon.Native.BlockReplayInputProjection.accepted_raw_projected_ledger_tree_state_after_startup_equivalence` adds the raw-decoded native tree replay surface: decoded replay fields and decoded tree-transition inputs project into the ledger/tree replay model, the raw projected executor is tied to accepted tree replay, and accepted raw-projected tree replay exposes commitment-root progression, accepted ledger replay, supply equality, leaf-cursor equality, canonical commitment-plan preconditions, raw tree carried-state preconditions, final nullifier uniqueness, and final bridge replay-key uniqueness. `Hegemon.Native.CanonicalPublicationRefinement.accepted_canonical_publication_refines_ledger_tree_replay` composes accepted block-index reload, canonical-state reload, canonical reorg-chain admission, atomic commit-manifest admission, storage durability admission, and ledger/tree replay into one publication package carrying replayed supply, leaf cursor, commitment-root publication, canonical commitment plan, final nullifier uniqueness, and final bridge replay-key uniqueness. `accepted_raw_canonical_publication_refines_ledger_tree_replay` lifts the same package through raw decoded ledger/tree replay while preserving raw carried-state preconditions. The valid raw tree example pins the root cursor at `50 -> 52`, and the stale raw root example rejects. `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeContentRefinement.projectedCommitmentRows_values`, `projectedCommitmentRows_indexes`, and `canonical_commitment_rows_bind_ordered_decoded_content` prove that canonical commitment rows expose exactly the ordered decoded commitments and planned indexes. `canonical_nullifier_rows_bind_ordered_decoded_content`, `canonical_bridge_replay_rows_bind_planned_replay_keys`, `raw_ingress_full_byte_field_projection_binds_replay_set_content`, and `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_binds_replay_set_content` add the same row-content binding for replay sets: canonical nullifier rows equal ordered decoded nullifiers, canonical bridge replay rows equal ordered planned bridge replay keys, and both are packaged with accepted raw-ingress full-byte publication and final replay-set uniqueness over the same raw witness. The production regression `canonical_index_rebuild_projects_decoded_materialized_wire_rows_replay_sets` checks decoded nullifier rows, materialized/planned replay keys, canonical rebuild plan rows, and persisted sled markers agree in order. The consensus append-certificate gate adds the first production replay surface for actual Merkle mutation: it records prior/result root-history tails, leaf index/value, per-level sibling orientation/default facts, per-level parents, and result root/count while sharing the hot append mutation body. `Hegemon.Consensus.CommitmentTreeAppend` and `GenerateCommitmentTreeAppendVectors` lift leaf-index/count progression, sibling orientation/default choice, bounded root-history retention, and zero-limit unbounded-history behavior into Lean-generated vectors checked against that production certificate and real Merkle replay. The consensus append slice and state-merkle crate additionally run independent batch-extension atomicity gates, so oversized append batches reject before root, leaf-count, frontier/history, path, or root-history mutation. `decodedRowsFollowLeafCursor_of_action_plan_accepts`, `orderedDecodedCommitmentIndexes_eq_rangePrime_of_cursor`, and `rawIngressAppendSummaries_indexes_eq_orderedDecodedCommitmentIndexes_of_action_plan` now prove the pure flattened append-index equality from the accepted row-start plan. `raw_ingress_commitment_tree_content_binds_append_publication_surface` and `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_binds_commitment_append_publication` feed that equality into accepted raw-ingress publication facts, so append-summary leaf indexes equal the ordered decoded commitment indexes and canonical commitment-row indexes while carrying the existing commitment-root and leaf-cursor publication facts. `raw_ingress_full_byte_field_projection_binds_ledger_integrity_publication` composes the field-projection facts, append-publication facts, and replay-set content facts into a single raw-ingress ledger-integrity certificate carrying accepted ledger/tree replay, replayed supply, commitment-root publication, leaf-cursor publication, final nullifier uniqueness, final bridge replay uniqueness, exact canonical commitment/nullifier/bridge replay rows, ordered content, and append-summary index/count equality. The remaining gap is exhaustive Merkle/hash implementation equivalence and arbitrary raw-byte parser refinement. `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeContentRefinement.accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_binds_commitment_tree_nullifier_integrity_certificate` composes accepted full-byte field projection, ordered canonical commitment/nullifier/bridge replay rows, root and leaf-cursor publication, append mutation index/count binding, final nullifier uniqueness, final replay uniqueness, and replayed supply on one raw-ingress witness. `Hegemon.Native.CommitmentTreeContentRefinement.raw_ingress_integrity_and_path_family_rows_bind_native_publication_ledger_integrity_certificate` then consumes that certificate plus the native publication path-family row evidence, carrying mined commit, announced import, sync import, reorg replay, canonical-index rebuild, block_range, and startup repair exact row content while preserving mined/startup decoded-row and canonical-row agreement plus rebuild/block_range canonical-row agreement.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production implementation-equivalence refinement across existing Lean action-stream, action-plan, tree-transition, accepted-ledger replay, and raw projection kernels. It now includes a raw-decoded ledger/tree replay lift, a Lean-vector-checked production append-transition certificate gate, a focused state-merkle independent append/path oracle, and an accepted raw-ingress append-publication surface whose append-summary indexes are derived from the executable action-plan row-start model and tied to canonical commitment-row indexes. It does not yet claim complete commitment-tree integrity.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native commitment-tree refinement theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the native commitment-tree refinement module in the Lean root and claims ledger.
Consensus append certificates must replay to the same root as hot append, reject trace drift, and oversized batch extension must reject before mutating root, leaf count, frontier, or history.
State-merkle append roots, authentication paths, root history, stale-root rejection, tree-full behavior, path bounds, and oversized batch-extension atomicity must match the independent tree oracle.
Lean-generated append mutation vectors must match production append certificates over leaf index/count, sibling orientation/default flags, root-history retention, and real Merkle replay.
Accepted raw-ingress commitment-tree content must derive the append-publication surface over the same raw witness rather than leaving append vectors disconnected from publication facts.
Accepted raw-ingress field projection must package replayed supply, final replay-set uniqueness, canonical rows, and append mutation facts in one ledger-integrity certificate.
The raw-ingress ledger-integrity certificate must compose with mined/import/reorg/startup path-family row evidence in one implementation-equivalence certificate.
Canonical index rebuild must preserve exact ordered decoded nullifier rows and planned bridge replay-key rows through the rebuild plan and persisted sled markers.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a non-production implementation-equivalence refinement across existing Lean action-stream, action-plan, tree-transition, accepted-ledger replay, raw projection, canonical publication, and raw-ingress full-byte field-projection kernels. It now carries exact ordered decoded commitment content and indexes through canonical commitment rows, exact ordered decoded/planned replay-set content through canonical nullifier and bridge replay rows, a Lean-vector-checked production append-transition certificate regression, a focused independent state-merkle append/path/root-history oracle, atomic oversized batch-extension rejection for consensus and state-merkle trees, an accepted raw-ingress append-publication package whose append-summary indexes are derived from the executable action-plan row-start model and tied to decoded and canonical commitment-row indexes, a raw-ingress ledger-integrity certificate that packages supply replay, final nullifier uniqueness, final bridge replay uniqueness, canonical rows, and append mutation facts over the same witness, and a path-family ledger-integrity certificate tying that witness to mined commit, announced import, sync import, reorg replay, canonical-index rebuild, block_range, and startup repair exact row content. It does not prove arbitrary parser internals, exhaustive production Merkle-tree mutation implementation equivalence for all depths/sequences, nullifier/replay-key/hash cryptographic security or equivalence, sled durability below accepted predicates, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: The executable Lean native-backend release-posture policy accepts the default release package exactly when review_state is candidate-under-review, maturity_label is structural-candidate, and external cryptanalysis is not marked complete; accepted release mode accepts only with review_state accepted, no known incomplete external-review marker, and a checked-in acceptance artifact that states external acceptance. The accepted-mode production release gate additionally requires `requireAccepted = true` to expose accepted native-backend preconditions before packaging native backend posture with dependency, PQ, and CI release gates.
nodes.informal_argument: The repo still does not claim machine-checked cryptographic soundness for the native lattice backend. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.NativeBackendReleasePosture.accepts_iff_release_posture_preconditions` pins the release-posture decision table, companion theorems pin candidate and accepted acceptance/rejection cases, generated vectors are checked by `scripts/check_native_backend_release_posture_policy_vectors.py`, and the existing shell gate still evaluates the packaged review posture used by release workflows. `Hegemon.Release.ReleasePostureCertificate.accepted_native_backend_release_posture_in_accepted_mode_exposes_preconditions` and `accepted_release_posture_with_native_accepted_mode_yields_production_gate_certificate` prevent candidate-under-review posture from being treated as production crypto: an accepted-mode release must expose accepted native-backend preconditions and carry them with dependency, PQ, and CI gates.

target_review.notes: Accepted as an explicit residual-risk node with theorem-backed release-posture gating. This does not promote the native backend to production cryptography.

falsification_cases.description: Lean-generated native-backend release-posture vectors must fail on wrong candidate posture, completed external-review marker in candidate mode, wrong accepted posture, incomplete accepted external review, missing accepted artifact, and malformed accepted artifact text.
The native backend posture script must report candidate-under-review and structural-candidate for the current package.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim the native backend is production-ready cryptography.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean raw-ingress action-hash tx-leaf publication surface, accepted raw-ingress pending-action tx-leaf publication facts imply one named review surface exposing action-hash admission components, exact pending/block-action decode facts, raw and canonical publication facts, replayed supply, final replay-set uniqueness, and native tx-leaf/canonical statement artifact binding over the same accepted raw-ingress witness, while carrying parser/hash/proof soundness assumptions explicitly.
nodes.informal_argument: `accepted_raw_ingress_action_hash_tx_leaf_publication` first obtains `RawIngressPendingActionTxLeafPublicationFacts` from the existing accepted raw-ingress pending-action publication theorem. `raw_ingress_pending_action_tx_leaf_publication_facts_expose_action_hash_and_statement_binding` then projects the nested pending-action byte publication, raw-ingress publication, canonical publication, and native tx-leaf full statement artifact facts into one record, and `action_hash_admission_preconditions_components` decomposes accepted action-hash admission into count/hash/uniqueness components.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a zero-runtime-cost Lean projection surface over existing raw-ingress pending-action tx-leaf publication facts. Parser, hash, statement-hash, and native tx-leaf proof-system soundness remain explicit assumptions.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the raw-ingress action-hash tx-leaf theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
The formal-core claims and blueprint gates must reject the slice if the theorem, evidence path, or inventory entry is removed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves an accepted-predicate composition and review surface. It does not prove arbitrary raw parser internals, hash security/equivalence, deployed proof-system soundness, native tx-leaf proof soundness, DA availability, storage durability, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean raw-ingress DA sidecar canonical publication surface, accepted raw-ingress sidecar replay, accepted canonical publication gates, and accepted raw ledger/tree replay over the same raw decoded witness imply a single record carrying DA sidecar binding, proof sidecar admission facts, raw/canonical replay facts, commitment-root publication, replayed supply, replayed leaf cursor, and final nullifier/bridge replay uniqueness, with DA/parser/hash/storage/proof/native-node assumptions explicit.
nodes.informal_argument: `accepted_raw_ingress_da_sidecar_canonical_publication` calls `raw_ingress_publication_equivalent_to_raw_ledger_tree_replay` and `accepted_raw_canonical_publication_refines_ledger_tree_replay` with the same `rawProjectedLedgerTreeStateAfter initial blocks = some final` witness. It also decomposes proof metadata and decoded proof sidecar preconditions, so the accepted sidecar rows, DA-root binding, proof binding-hash facts, canonical publication, supply replay, commitment-root publication, and replay-set uniqueness facts are visible in one theorem package.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a zero-runtime-cost Lean composition over raw-ingress sidecar and canonical publication predicates. DA availability, parser internals, hash security/equivalence, storage durability, proof soundness, and complete native-node equivalence remain explicit assumptions.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the raw-ingress DA sidecar canonical theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
The formal-core claims and blueprint gates must reject the slice if the theorem, evidence path, or inventory entry is removed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a DA-sidecar/canonical-publication composition over accepted production-bound predicates. It does not prove arbitrary raw parser internals, DA availability or retention, hash security/equivalence, recursive/native proof soundness, sled durability, or complete native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean raw-ingress full-byte publication surface, accepted raw-ingress sidecar replay, exact pending-action decode, block-action decode, action-hash admission, canonical publication gates, accepted raw ledger/tree replay, parsed native tx-leaf artifact bytes, explicit parser-to-canonical projection assumptions, the canonical statement surface, accepted block-action validation, named production projection facts, ordered field-projection evidence, accepted mined-block commit publication, row-equivalence evidence for every native publication path, and ordered content binders imply one flattened record carrying parser exactness, canonical decode acceptance, wire replay, action-hash, DA sidecar, canonical publication, supply, commitment-root, replay-set uniqueness, tx-leaf artifact shape, statement/proof artifact facts, decoded/validated/materialized/planned/wire/canonical field-row agreement, mined commit row-publication binding, a native publication path-family row-equivalence package, exact commitment/nullifier/bridge replay/ciphertext index/ciphertext archive content for every path-family member, a single accepted-block admission-safety certificate, and a canonical admission-safety certificate with explicit parser/hash/proof/DA/storage/native-node residual assumptions over the same full-byte facts.
nodes.informal_argument: `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_surface` calls `accepted_raw_ingress_pending_action_bytes_bind_parsed_tx_leaf_publication`, then flattens the nested raw-ingress publication, pending-action byte publication, canonical publication, and parsed tx-leaf canonical artifact facts into a single review surface. `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_surface_binds_production_projection_rows` additionally constructs the pending-action replay row-count facts from that flattened surface and composes them with accepted block-action validation plus named production projection facts, so decoded payload rows, validated action rows, materialized action rows, materialized payload rows, and wire replay rows agree at the raw-ingress full-byte boundary. `PendingActionByteReplayRowCountBinding.pending_action_raw_byte_projection_requires_canonical_decode` proves that the accepted production field-row package necessarily had accepted exact pending-action decode and accepted exact block-action decode, so trailing/noncanonical bytes cannot be treated as if they had publication rows inside this surface. `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_surface_binds_field_projection_rows` lifts the ordered field-row package into the same boundary, including exact commitment, nullifier, bridge replay, ciphertext index, and ciphertext archive row projection. `AcceptedBlockAdmissionSafety.accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_yields_accepted_block_admission_safety` packages the same full-byte facts into one accepted-block certificate carrying parser exactness, sidecar availability and sizes, DA-root matching, statement/root binding, replayed ledger/tree facts, commitment-root publication, replayed supply/leaf cursor, nullifier uniqueness, bridge replay uniqueness, and full tx-leaf artifact/statement binding. `accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_yields_canonical_admission_safety_certificate` then packages that same witness into a canonical admission-safety certificate carrying exact canonical publication, replayed supply, commitment-root/leaf-cursor publication, nullifier/replay uniqueness, tx-leaf statement facts, ciphertext gates, accepted-block admission facts, and explicit parser/hash/proof/DA/storage/native-node residuals. `NativePublicationRowEquivalence.accepted_raw_ingress_full_byte_publication_binds_native_publication_rows` names that row-equivalence package as a review theorem, `accepted_mined_block_commit_binds_native_publication_rows` composes it with accepted mined-block commit publication facts, and `accepted_native_publication_path_family_binds_native_publication_rows` packages raw-ingress, mined-commit, announced-import, sync-import, reorg-replay, canonical-index-rebuild, block_range, and startup-repair row-equivalence facts into one path-family review surface. `CommitmentTreeContentRefinement.native_publication_rows_equivalence_binds_commitment_replay_content` derives exact ordered commitment values/indexes, nullifier rows, and bridge replay keys from any native publication row-equivalence package; `native_publication_rows_equivalence_binds_ciphertext_content` derives exact canonical ciphertext index/archive rows from the same row-equivalence package; and the two path-family content theorems lift those facts to every path-family member while preserving mined/startup decoded-row sharing and rebuild/block_range canonical-row sharing. The production regression `canonical_index_rebuild_rejects_malleable_action_bytes_before_projection_rows` appends trailing bytes to an otherwise canonical action fixture and requires both direct action decode and canonical-index rebuild to reject before materialized/planned/canonical row projection. The raw-ingress full-byte publication surface now includes the commitment/nullifier integrity certificate, so exact canonical commitment/nullifier/bridge replay rows, append mutation index/count binding, replayed supply, commitment-root and leaf-cursor publication, and final uniqueness facts are available on the same accepted raw-byte publication witness. `canonical_admission_safety_certificate_exposes_replay_and_da_integrity`, `canonical_admission_safety_certificate_exposes_pre_admission_gates`, `canonical_admission_safety_certificate_exposes_exact_parser_and_wire_projection`, and `canonical_admission_safety_certificate_exposes_canonical_raw_replay_publication` turn the canonical certificate into directly consumable replay/DA integrity, exact parser/wire projection, raw replay publication, and pre-admission gate facts. `canonical_admission_safety_certificate_exposes_tx_leaf_statement_artifact` exposes tx-leaf byte-shape, projection-assumption, action-binding, and full statement artifact facts from the same canonical admission certificate.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a zero-runtime-cost top-level review surface over existing accepted parser/publication/projection predicates plus ordered field-row projection evidence, exact commitment/replay/ciphertext content binders, the accepted-block admission-safety certificate, and the canonical admission-safety certificate with explicit residuals. Parser internals, projection assumptions, hash security, proof soundness, DA availability, and complete native-node refinement remain explicit.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the raw-ingress full-byte publication surface theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
The formal-core claims and blueprint gates must reject the slice if the theorem, evidence path, or inventory entry is removed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the accepted-block admission-safety or canonical admission-safety certificate must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Removing or weakening the raw-ingress full-byte production projection theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Removing or weakening the native publication row-equivalence theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Removing or weakening the native publication path-family row-equivalence theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Removing or weakening the native publication path-family commitment/nullifier/bridge replay content theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Removing or weakening the native publication path-family ciphertext index/archive content theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
Changing the production raw bytes to validation/materialization/planning/wire projection path so decoded fields or row contents diverge must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Changing canonical rebuild or sync block-range publication so exact canonical rows diverge from decoded/materialized/planned rows must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Allowing trailing or otherwise malleable action bytes to reach canonical index row projection must fail the targeted Rust gate.

Changing mixed canonical rebuild so nonempty commitment, nullifier, bridge replay, ciphertext-index, or archive rows diverge from decoded/materialized/planned content must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Changing mined atomic commit, raw action bytes, canonical rebuild rows, or reopened startup replay rows so they stop agreeing for the live coinbase import path must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Allowing an exact-decodable substituted mined action body to publish rows from a different committed action slice must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Allowing an exact-decodable substituted announced action body to persist or publish under the original committed action root must fail the targeted Rust gate.
Allowing startup canonical replay to accept a nonempty block body whose valid action bytes no longer match the committed action root must fail the targeted Rust gate.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean network resource predicates, accepted network and PQ frame decoding occurs exactly when the encoded byte length is within the configured production cap, the codec marker matches the frame family, postcard decoding succeeds for postcard-encoded frames, and the postcard decoder consumes all bytes. Encode admission occurs exactly when the four-byte marker plus body length is within the configured cap. Oversize frames reject before marker or parser work, missing markers reject before postcard work, postcard failures reject before trailing-byte acceptance, and trailing bytes reject. The PQ session plaintext cap plus AEAD tag equals the session ciphertext cap. Peer-send and event-channel queue reserve/send admission accepts exactly when the message bytes fit the queue cap, queued-byte addition does not overflow usize, and aggregate queued bytes stay within the configured queue cap; failed full/closed sends roll queued bytes back, and reserve rejection takes precedence over channel state. Peer-store capacity admission additionally keeps retained peer records within max_entries, makes the retained records equal to the recency-prefix subset of learned records, and partitions the ordered peer view into retained prefix plus dropped recency suffix.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Network.FrameResourceAdmission.evaluate_frame_decode_accepts_iff` and companion rejection theorems prove the executable frame admission table and rejection precedence. `accepted_frame_decode_exposes_facts` packages the bound, marker, postcard, and no-trailing-byte facts exposed by accepted inputs. `evaluate_frame_encode_accepts_iff` and `accepted_frame_encode_within_bound` prove outbound marker-plus-body size admission. Marker length and domain-separation theorems pin the HNW/PNH/PNS/PNT byte markers, while the PQ plaintext-plus-tag theorem connects postcard session plaintext admission to the encrypted frame cap. `Hegemon.Network.QueueResourceAdmission.queue_reserve_accepts_iff`, `accepted_queue_reserve_exposes_facts`, `accepted_bounded_send_exposes_facts`, and rollback/precedence theorems prove the executable queue byte-budget table for reserve, accepted send, queue-full, and queue-closed outcomes. `gen_frame_resource_admission_vectors` and `gen_queue_resource_admission_vectors` emit Lean-derived constants and acceptance/rejection cases; formal-core checks those vectors against production `network::wire`, `pq-noise::codec`, and `network_backend` queue accounting constants and helper behavior. The formal-core wrapper now also runs arbitrary-byte mutation corpora for the network and PQ frame decoders, comparing production acceptance to an independent marker/bound/postcard/full-consumption oracle across malformed, marker-prefixed, oversized, truncated, trailing-byte, and mutated-valid frames. `Hegemon.Network.PeerStoreCapacityAdmission.accepted_peer_store_capacity_exposes_bound`, `peer_store_capacity_retains_recency_prefix`, `peer_store_capacity_drops_recency_suffix`, and `peer_store_capacity_retained_and_dropped_partition_entries` prove the retained peer-store view is bounded by `max_entries`, equals the ordered recency prefix, and partitions with the dropped suffix. Schema-v1 vectors now carry expected retained IDs, dropped IDs, prefix status, and dropped-absence facts for below-limit, exact-limit, over-limit, and zero-limit production retention through `PeerStore::enforce_max_entries`. Focused runtime gates also require active P2P connections to begin under the smaller handshake frame cap before promotion to the secure wire cap, inbound/outbound handshakes to be bounded by timeout and inbound backlog limits, address exchange to be capped and dialable-only, address books to be bounded per peer and globally, and peer sends to use nonblocking `try_send` so full queues cannot stall broadcast/ping loops.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors and arbitrary-byte oracle corpora for network/PQ frame caps, marker-domain admission, postcard success, no-trailing-byte rejection, and bounded peer/event queue accounting. Scope is admission, codec-resource behavior, parser-drift detection, and queue byte-budget accounting, not a proof of postcard internals, primitive crypto, scheduler fairness, or full transport/session liveness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the frame-resource admission theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production network HNW frame caps, marker bytes, oversize rejection, missing-marker rejection, postcard failure handling, or trailing-byte rejection away from the Lean executable table must fail the generated-vector conformance test.

Changing production network HNW frame decode acceptance away from the marker, size-bound, postcard-success, and full-consumption oracle over malformed and mutated byte streams must fail the arbitrary-byte corpus gate.

Changing production PQ Noise frame caps, marker bytes, oversize rejection, missing-marker rejection, postcard failure handling, transcript marker, session plaintext cap, or trailing-byte rejection away from the Lean executable table must fail the generated-vector conformance test.

Changing production PQ Noise handshake/session/transcript frame decode acceptance away from the marker, size-bound, postcard-success, and full-consumption oracle over malformed and mutated byte streams must fail the arbitrary-byte corpus gate.

Changing production peer-send or event-channel queue byte caps, reserve overflow/over-budget precedence, accepted-send queued-byte increments, full/closed rollback behavior, or reserve-before-channel-state behavior away from the Lean executable table must fail the generated-vector conformance test.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate frame-resource and queue-resource vectors and run all production conformance tests.
Production peer-store retention must match Lean max_entries capacity, recency-prefix retention, and dropped-suffix cases.
Active P2P connections must reject oversized handshake frames before secure-wire promotion raises the frame cap.
Peer address recording must filter nondialable addresses and cap retained address entries per peer and across the address book.
Peer broadcast, directed send, and ping loops must not await full peer queues.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks bounded frame admission, marker-domain separation, postcard-decode success, no-trailing-byte rejection, outbound marker-plus-body bounds, PQ plaintext/tag cap accounting, and peer/event queue byte-budget reserve/send/rollback accounting for the network and PQ Noise surfaces. It also checks arbitrary-byte mutation corpora against an independent marker/bound/postcard/full-consumption oracle for parser-drift detection. It does not prove postcard implementation correctness, SHA/HKDF/AEAD/ML-KEM/ML-DSA security, tokio length-delimited transport correctness, scheduler fairness, network liveness, peer honesty, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: Every residual that remains after the highest-standard completion certificate is classified as exactly one of three auditable buckets: parser/native/proof/bridge residuals are mechanized-refinement tracks, primitive PQ/hash/STARK/ciphertext/RNG/native-lattice-review residuals are named cryptographic assumptions, and DA/storage/global-privacy/release/scanner/performance residuals are fail-closed system-model assumptions.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Release.AssumptionClosureRoadmap` defines `MechanizedRefinementTracks`, `NamedPrimitiveCryptoAssumptions`, `FailClosedSystemModelAssumptions`, and `ResidualAssumptionClosureRoadmap`. The projection theorems expose each bucket, and `residual_closure_roadmap_splits_all_open_assumptions` exposes the full split in one theorem. The checked-in ExecPlan makes the burn-down strategy self-contained: implementation-equivalence gaps must be closed by Lean/Rust refinement work, not waved away as crypto assumptions, while primitive hardness and environmental behavior remain named, reviewable assumptions with fail-closed evidence requirements.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a classification ratchet and roadmap. It does not discharge parser/native/proof/bridge residuals; it names them as mechanized work and prevents them from being hidden under broad cryptographic or infrastructure assumptions.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the residual-assumption split theorem must fail the Lean formal gate or claims check.
falsification_cases.gate: cd formal/lean && lake build Hegemon.Release.AssumptionClosureRoadmap Hegemon

falsification_cases.description: The formal-core claims and blueprint gates must reject the slice if the theorem, evidence path, dependency, or ExecPlan is removed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint config/formal-security-blueprint.json --claims config/formal-security-claims.json

falsification_cases.description: Parser/native/proof/bridge residuals must remain listed as mechanized-refinement tracks, not downgraded into generic primitive cryptographic assumptions.
DA/storage/global-privacy/release/scanner/performance residuals must remain explicit system-model assumptions with fail-closed evidence or monitoring obligations.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node classifies and ratchets residual work. It is not a proof of arbitrary-byte parser internals, complete native-node equivalence, deployed proof-system/AIR soundness, positive bridge mint verifier soundness, primitive cryptographic hardness, DA retention, storage hardware behavior, global unlinkability, live GitHub enforcement, scanner completeness, or performance preservation.

nodes.formal_statement: Accepted system-model residual evidence must expose DA-retention monitoring, storage-durability monitoring, global-privacy-boundary monitoring, release-infrastructure monitoring, dependency-scanner freshness monitoring, performance-budget monitoring, fail-closed policy, and release-blocking policy; the formal-core checker must reject missing, non-fail-closed, non-release-blocking, stale, or unevidenced system-model categories.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Release.SystemModelAssumptionGate` proves the executable decision table for accepted system-model monitoring facts. The formal-core command `check-system-model-gates` validates the checked-in JSON ledger, requiring every required category to be represented by a fail-closed, release-blocking, freshness-bounded monitor with evidence paths. This turns DA/storage/global-privacy/release/scanner/performance assumptions into explicit fail-closed operational gates rather than prose residuals.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a release-blocking system-model evidence gate. It does not prove external environmental honesty; it fails closed if required monitoring categories or evidence are removed.

falsification_cases.description: Removing the accepted system-model gate theorem surface must fail the Lean or claims gate.
falsification_cases.gate: cd formal/lean && lake build Hegemon.Release.SystemModelAssumptionGate Hegemon

falsification_cases.description: Deleting any required system-model category, disabling fail-closed/release-blocking policy, exceeding freshness bounds, or removing evidence paths must fail the formal-core checker.
The full formal-core wrapper must include the system-model gate phase.
The checker unit tests must reject missing categories and non-fail-closed gates.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and checks fail-closed evidence presence for environmental assumptions. It does not prove DA availability, storage hardware/fsync semantics, global unlinkability against timing/topology/miner-order observers, GitHub or runner integrity, advisory database completeness, scanner soundness, or that benchmark samples bound all production deployments.
